## PAGE 06515: Understanding content of the Merged Result panel

- page_id: `243968579`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968579/Understanding+content+of+the+Merged+Result+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:03.252+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['merged-result', 'element-decorations', 'change-states', 'recognizing-changes', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

177724953

INLINE

177724956

INLINE

177724954

INLINE

Elements with all change types are displayed in the **Merged Result** tree/list that is located at the top left of the **Merge** window.

You can filter the elements displayed in the **Merged Results** tree/list by type of changes. Use the **Changes filter** drop-down list to filter out a specific type of changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

By default, the changes from the Target and equivalent changes (changes made both in Target and Source) are automatically hidden from the **Merge** **Result** tree/list, meaning that only the Source changes are shown. This allows reducingthe number of changes needed to review for the user. If the filter is applied, the following notification appears:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Understanding element marking**

When a change occurs on a single element, the ticks and crossesbefore the element icon indicate the state of the change which can be either accepted or rejected.

| Marking | Change state | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| (green tick before the element icon) | Accepted | A new element had been created in one of the contributors and its addition was accepted. |
| (red cross before the element icon) | Rejected | A new element had been created in one of the contributors and its addition was rejected. |

To understand the concept of change states, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change concept' space='']Change states.

Elements with resolved conflicting changes are marked as shown in the following table. Owners of these elements are also marked.

| Marking | Change applied by | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| (red diamond on the lower-left corner of the element icon) | System | The element had a conflicting modification change which was resolved automatically. |
| (yellow diamond on the lower-left corner of the element icon) | User | The element had a conflicting modification change which was resolved by the user. |

**Recognizing addition changes**

For the addition change description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change concept' space=''].

Added elements are highlighted in green background. 
 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Recognizing deletion changes**

For the deletion change description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change concept' space=''].

Deleted elements are highlighted in red background.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

**Recognizing modification changes**

For the modification change description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change concept' space=''].

Elements whose properties have been changed are highlighted in blue. 
 [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
To see what properties have been modified, click the element and see changes in the **Specification** panel.

For more information about using the Specification panel, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Inspecting changes in the Change details panel' space=''].

**Recognizing movement changes**

For the movement change description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change concept' space=''].

Elements with movement changes are highlighted in the same blue background that is used to highlight modification changes. Since movement changes are some kind of modification changes. Icon of an element with movement changes is represented with an arrow symbol on the left.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can navigate from the element original location to the new one (and vice versa) using the commands on the element shortcut menu.

**Recognizing order changes**

For the order change description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change concept' space=''].

Elements with order changes are highlighted in the same blue background that is used to highlight modification changes, since order changes are some kind of modification changes. 
After accepting the change, all the elements in the collection are reordered so that it would be the same as in one of the contributors.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can turn off the order changes detection. For more about it, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Finishing and canceling merge' space='']

**Recognizing elements with changed owned elements** 
Elements whose owned elements (in any nesting level) have changes are highlighted using red diagonals.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

177724951

INLINE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=271515667 space=MT version=16 -->
## PAGE 06516: Understanding Diagram Toolbar

- page_id: `271515667`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/271515667/Understanding+Diagram+Toolbar
- version_number: 16
- version_date: `2025-11-17T12:42:27.187+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The diagram toolbar provides quick access to common diagram editing and navigation commands. These tools help you arrange elements, adjust connector routing, apply styles, and manage the diagram's appearance. The table below lists commands, descriptions, and their icons to help you identify them in the toolbar.

| Icon | Command | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Back to Index | Returns to the previously viewed diagram or diagram location. |
|  | Forward | Returns to the next viewed diagram or diagram location. |
|  | Select in Containment Tree | Selects the active element in the Containment tree. |
|  | Quick Diagram Layout | Automatically arranges the visible diagram elements using the default layout algorithm. To learn more, refer to the Layout page. |
|  | Align | Aligns the selected symbols based on the chosen alignment options described below:IconCommandDescriptionMake Same SizeResizes all selected shapes to match the size of the largest shape.Make Same WidthResizes all selected shapes to match the width of the widest shape.Make Same HeightResizes all selected shapes to match the height of the tallest shape.Center HorizontallyAligns the selected shapes to a common horizontal center.Center VerticallyAligns the selected shapes to a common vertical center.Space Evenly HorizontallyEvenly spaces the selected shapes horizontally.Space Evenly VerticallyEvenly spaces the selected shapes vertically.Align RightAligns the selected shapes to the right edge of the rightmost shape.Align LeftAligns the selected shapes to the left edge of the leftmost shape.Align TopAligns the selected shapes to the top edge of the topmost shape.Align BottomAligns the selected shapes to the bottom edge of the lowest shape. |
| Icon | Command | Description |
|  | Make Same Size | Resizes all selected shapes to match the size of the largest shape. |
|  | Make Same Width | Resizes all selected shapes to match the width of the widest shape. |
|  | Make Same Height | Resizes all selected shapes to match the height of the tallest shape. |
|  | Center Horizontally | Aligns the selected shapes to a common horizontal center. |
|  | Center Vertically | Aligns the selected shapes to a common vertical center. |
|  | Space Evenly Horizontally | Evenly spaces the selected shapes horizontally. |
|  | Space Evenly Vertically | Evenly spaces the selected shapes vertically. |
|  | Align Right | Aligns the selected shapes to the right edge of the rightmost shape. |
|  | Align Left | Aligns the selected shapes to the left edge of the leftmost shape. |
|  | Align Top | Aligns the selected shapes to the top edge of the topmost shape. |
|  | Align Bottom | Aligns the selected shapes to the bottom edge of the lowest shape. |
|  | Remove Break Points | Removes all user-defined breakpoints from the selected connectors. |
|  | Rectilinear | Changes the selected connector to a rectilinear routing style. |
|  | Reset Label Position | Returns the selected label to its default position. |
|  | Rotate Label | Rotates the selected label to the next available orientation. |
|  | Copy | Copies the selected diagram elements or symbols to the clipboard. |
|  | Paste | Pastes the copied elements or symbols into the current diagram. |
|  | Delete | Deletes the selected element from both the diagram and the model. |
|  | Remove from Diagram | Removes the selected symbol from the diagram without deleting the underlying model element. |
|  | Fill Color | Applies a fill color to the selected symbol. |
|  | Apply Default Symbol Style | Restores the selected symbol to the default style defined for its element type. |
|  | Paste Style | Applies the copied style properties to the selected symbol. |
|  | Suppress All | Hides all compartments and visual details in the selected symbol. |
|  | Show as Box | Displays the selected element as a box symbol. |
|  | Show as Image | Displays the selected element using its assigned image instead of the default box symbol. |
|  | Make Preferred Size | Resizes the selected symbol to its preferred size. |
|  | Validate Diagram | Runs validation on the active diagram and displays any detected issues. |
|  | Options | Opens the diagram’s display and presentation options. |
|  | Legend | Opens the diagram legend for managing style categories and legend items. To learn more, refer to the page. |
|  | Find in Diagram | Locates and highlights the selected element in the active diagram. |
|  | Run | Runs the Simulation. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ri:filename="Copy.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Copy</td><td>Copies the selected diagram elements or symbols to the clipboard.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image diagram.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="24"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Delete.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Delete</td><td>Deletes Diagram.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Remove from Diagram</td><td>Removes the selected symbol from the diagram without deleting the underlying model element.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="24"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Fill.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Fill Color</td><td>Applies a fill color to the selected symbol.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="24"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Apply Default Symbol Style.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Apply Default Symbol Style</td><td>Restores the selected symbol to the default style defined for its element type.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="24"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Paste Style.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Paste Style</td><td>Applies the copied style properties to the selected symbol.</td></tr><tr><td><div <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968535 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06517: Understanding merge types

- page_id: `243968535`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968535/Understanding+merge+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:01.883+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Merge
- labels: ['merge-type', '2-way-merge', '3-way-merge', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1993946884

INLINE

1993946886

INLINE

1993946885

INLINE

There are two types of model merge that can be used to merge two selected project versions (Source and Target):

- 3-way merge
- 2-way merge

##### 3-way merge

The 3-way merge allows merging both local and server projects. To merge two selected project versions, the common ancestor (a common project version for both target and source) is used. The common ancestor is calculated automatically when working with Teamwork Cloud projects; however, you have to select a correct common ancestor manually for local projects. This is the recommended way to merge project versions because the 3-way merge is able to identify the changes made in the source and target project versions separately by comparing changes between the ancestor and both contributors. This allows merging the selected project versions more smoothly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Visual representation of the 3-way merge

##### 2-way merge

The 2-way merge compares two local project versions selected by the user. Typically, the target version of the project is considered to be the common ancestor (a common project version for both target and source). As a result, only the differences between the source and the target project versions are identified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Visual representation of the 2-way merge

1993946883

INLINE

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing and managing merge results' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Finishing and canceling merge' space='']

To merge two selected project versions, the common ancestor (a common project version for both target and source) is used. The common ancestor is calculated automatically when working with Teamwork Cloud projects; however, you have to select a correct common ancestor manually for local projects. This is the recommended way to merge project versions because the 3-way merge is able to identify the changes made in the source and target project versions separately by comparing changes between the ancestor and both contributors. This allows merging the selected project versions more smoothly. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3-way_merge_explanation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Visual representation of the 3-way merge</h6><h3>2-way merge</h3><p>The 2-way merge compares two local project versions selected by the user. Typically, the target version of the project is considered to be the common ancestor (a common project version for both target and source). As a result, only the differences between the source and the target project versions are identified. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="2-way_merge_explanation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Visual representation of the 2-way merge</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a15a32b-4b65-4453-b599-d525df849345"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1993946883</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro conf-macro output-block"><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding change concept" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Preparing for merge" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting Model Merge" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Analyzing and managing merge results" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Finishing and canceling merge" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

## PAGE 06519: Union

- page_id: `249567475`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567475/Union
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:31:00.896+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > XML schemas > XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

- The UML Class must have the additional stereotype XSDunion.
- “memberTypes” and inner simpleTypes maps to several UML Generalizations between the simple type and members types.
- In order to have an inner simpleType element, the parent of this Generalization must be an inner Class of an outer UML Class.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Union model example.

#### PANEL: Union model and XML code samples

Union model and XML code samples

```text
<xs:schema xmlns:nm = "http://nomagic.com" xmlns:xs = "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" targetNamespace = "http://nomagic.com" >	<xs:simpleType name = "my_simple_union" >
```

```text
<xs:union id = "unionID" memberTypes = "xs:string xs:number" />	</xs:simpleType>
```

```text
<xs:simpleType name = "my_simple_union2" >
```

```text
<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:documentation >very important documentation</xs:documentation>
```

```text
</xs:annotation>
```

```text
<xs:union id = "unionID" memberTypes = "xs:number" >  			<xs:simpleType > 				<xs:restriction base = "xs:number" />
```

```text
</xs:simpleType> 		</xs:union>
```

```text
</xs:simpleType>
```

```text
</xs:schema>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

unique mapping to UML attributes


```text
id = ID
```

```text
name = NCName
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?, (selector, field+))
```

```text
</unique>
```

## PAGE 06521: Unparsed code

- page_id: `243970777`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970777/Unparsed+code
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:57.346+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Report Wizard template language
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Velocity allows the template designer to easily use a large amount of uninterpreted and unparsed content in *VTL* code. This is especially useful if you want to avoid multiple directives or sections whose content would otherwise be invalid (and thus unparseable) *VTL*.

The syntax is as follows:

```text

```

For example, the following code:

```text

```

Will be rendered as:

```text

```

The Unparsed Code support is available in *HTML*, *XML*, and *text* files only.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Velocity allows the template designer to easily use a large amount of uninterpreted and unparsed content in <em>VTL</em> code. This is especially useful if you want to avoid multiple directives or sections whose content would otherwise be invalid (and thus unparseable) <em>VTL</em>.</p><p>The syntax is as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5a3869f-c0c0-476c-9311-8f89a7e42302"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#[[ Unparsed Code ]]#]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example, the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30037ae2-c7d3-4834-8a2e-cd91e03226a6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#[[ 
#foreach($c in $Class)
$c.name
#end
]]#]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Will be rendered as:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f30d65f-a173-4205-926a-e18035f01714"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($c in $Class)
$c.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c56979c-b821-4170-bf1c-c408f9c7926a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Unparsed Code support is available in <em>HTML</em>, <em>XML</em>, and <em>text</em> files only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969414 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06522: Unresolved dependencies

- page_id: `243969414`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969414/Unresolved+dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:28.043+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Identifying Package Dependencies
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1334920193

INLINE

1334920195

INLINE

1334920194

INLINE

Before exporting or sharing a package as an independent used project, you need to identify and solve package dependencies first. More specifically, you have to make sure that the packagedoes not depend on any external elements (except other used projects) in a project.

The same situation occurs when you edit the used project inside the current project (the used project permissions should be set to read-write) and introduce dependencies from the used project back to the project where it is being used. In this case, you will be asked to solve these dependencies when saving the used project.

However, the modeling tool allows for continuing without solving these dependencies right away. The elements, which were referenced, but are missing in the used project will be shown as recovered elements (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='']).

You can choose how the dependency checking will be carried on in your projects by changing the **Check Dependencies on Package Export/Sharing** environment option (from the **Options** menu, select **Environment**> **General** > **Save/Load**). You can choose from one of the following:

- Do not allow dependencies . If this option is selected, then unsolved dependencies should not be allowed while exporting or sharing packages. The Export (or Share ) button is not available until the dependencies are solved.
- Allow dependencies . If this option is selected, then the dependencies are identified, but it is not required to solve them and the Export (or Share ) button is available.
- Do not check . If this option is selected, then the dependencies will not be checked at all. In this case, the selected package will be exported or shared as is. Be aware, that this might cause an increasing number of the recovered elements in your project (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space=''] ).

1334920192

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package Dependencies panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing dependencies among elements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6868b1e2-7a3e-4c6e-b349-738be8387b43"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1334920193</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60b7793e-1a87-40ae-8297-374027b77b08"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1334920195</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df63988d-8c32-41d6-a99f-39739d21abd7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1334920194</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Before exporting or sharing a package as an independent used project, you need to identify and solve package dependencies first. More specifically, you have to make sure that the package</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> does not depend on any external elements (except other used projects) in a project.</span></p><p>The same situation occurs when you edit the used project inside the current project (the used project permissions should be set to read-write) and introduce dependencies from the used project back to the project where it is being used. In this case, you will be asked to solve these dependencies when saving the used project.</p><p>However, the modeling tool allows for continuing without solving these dependencies right away. The elements, which were referenced, but are missing in the used project will be shown as recovered elements (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Maintaining decomposed model integrity" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Maintaining Decomposed Model Integrity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). </p><p>You can choose how the dependency checking will be carried on in your projects by changing the <strong>Check Dependencies on Package Export/Sharing</strong> environment option (from the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Environment </strong>&gt; <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Save/Load</strong>). You can choose from one of the following:</p><ul><li><strong>Do not allow dependencies</strong>. If this option is selected, then unsolved dependencies should not be allowed while exporting or sharing packages. The <strong>Export</strong> (or <strong>Share</strong>) button is not available until the dependencies are solved.</li><li><strong>Allow dependencies</strong>. If this option is selected, then the dependencies are identified, but it is not required to solve them and the <strong>Export</strong> (or <strong>Share</strong>) button is available.</li><li><strong>Do not check</strong>. If this option is selected, then the dependencies will not be checked at all. In this case, the selected package will be exported or shared as is. Be aware, that this might cause an increasing number of the recovered elements in your project (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Maintaining decomposed model integrity" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Maintaining Decomposed Model Integrity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d902ca69-e3c7-442d-9445-4778fe3c3017"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1334920192</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Identifying Package Dependencies" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package Dependencies panel" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Analyzing dependencies among elements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565598 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06523: UPDM 1.x Migration to UPDM 2.0

- page_id: `249565598`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565598/UPDM+1.x+Migration+to+UPDM+2.0
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:38.347+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### How to Migrate

You will be suggested migrating project each time you open the UPDM 1.x version project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Before starting the mapping procedure, please consider the following:

- The project does not use any UPDM 1.x version modules. If it does, please convert modules one by one before migrating the project.
- You have made a copy of your project.
- You have read this manual.

If you agree migrating the project, autocheck of the used modules will be performed. You will be warned if there are modules to be converted. You will be able to cancel the migration procedure.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><h3>How to Migrate</h3><p>You will be suggested migrating project each time you open the UPDM 1.x version project.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UPDM Migration Manual.jpg" /></ac:image></p><p>Before starting the mapping procedure, please consider the following:</p><ul><li>The project does not use any UPDM 1.x version modules. If it does, please convert modules one by one before migrating the project.</li><li>You have made a copy of your project.</li><li>You have read this manual.</li></ul><p>If you agree migrating the project, autocheck of the used modules will be performed. You will be warned if there are modules to be converted. You will be able to cancel the migration procedure.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565594 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06524: UPDM 2.0 Migration to UPDM 2.1

- page_id: `249565594`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565594/UPDM+2.0+Migration+to+UPDM+2.1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:38.242+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### How to Migrate

The migration from UPDM 2.0 to UPDM 2.1 will be performed automatically. During the migration, autocheck of the used modules will be performed. You will be warned if there are modules to be converted. You will be able to cancel the migration procedure.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><h3>How to Migrate</h3><p>The migration from UPDM 2.0 to UPDM 2.1 will be performed automatically. During the migration, autocheck of the used modules will be performed. You will be warned if there are modules to be converted. You will be able to cancel the migration procedure.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e9ea9f7e-96a6-439d-aa3e-06e9476c2e5b" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565557 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06525: UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1

- page_id: `249565557`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565557/UPDM+2.1+migration+to+UAF+1.1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:37.101+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1265258636

1265258639

1265258637

##### UAF definition

The Unified Profile for DoDAF and MODAF (UAF) is the standard of an Object Management Group (OMG) initiative to develop a modeling standard that supports both the US Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) and UK Ministry of Defense Architecture Framework (MODAF).

##### The purpose of UAF migration

The purpose of UAF migration to the latest UAF specification version is to convert earlier UAF versions metamodel-based projects into the latest UAF version metamodel-based projects without losing data.

Migration back to earlier UAF versions is not possible, so we highly recommend you to back-up your current UAFprojects before proceeding with migration to the latest UAFversion.

##### The purpose of the migration guide

The purpose of this guide is to help you to understand the differences between UAFspecifications and guide you along the data mappingprocess.

This manual describes the data mapping processwhile migrating from UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.1.

1265258635

**Related pages**

The Unified Profile for DoDAF and MODAF (UAF) is the standard of an Object Management Group (OMG) initiative to develop a modeling standard that supports both the US Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) and UK Ministry of Defense Architecture Framework (MODAF).

The purpose of UAF migration to the latest UAF specification version is to convert earlier UAF versions metamodel-based projects into the latest UAF version metamodel-based projects without losing data.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565520 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06527: UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.3

- page_id: `249565520`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565520/UPDM+2.1+migration+to+UAF+1.3
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-12T12:08:51.782+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is**NO DIRECT MIGRATION**from**UPDM 2.1**(still supported) to **UAF 1.3**(first supported modeling tool version - **2026x**).

##### How to migrate

To be able to migrate your **UPDM 2.1** project, you need to perform the following steps:​

1. Open your UPDM 2.1 project with the modeling tool 2022x or 2024x version, and migrate it to UAF 1.2 . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2' space='']>>]]>
2. Open the **UAF 1.2** project with the modeling tool **2026x** version and migrate it to **UAF 1.3**. [Learn more >>>](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565451/Migration+from+UAF+1.2+to+UAF+1.3)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">There is<span> </span><strong>NO DIRECT MIGRATION </strong>from<span> </span><strong>UPDM 2.1</strong><span> </span>(still supported) to <strong>UAF 1.3</strong><span> </span>(first supported modeling tool version - <strong>2026x</strong>).</span></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">How to migrate</h3><p style="text-align: left;"><span>To be able to migrate your <strong>UPDM 2.1</strong> project, you need to perform the following steps:</span><span>​</span></p><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="7a80db4a-c1cd-42b8-9155-06005ce55ecd">Open your <strong>UPDM 2.1</strong> project with the modeling tool <strong>2022x</strong> or <strong>2024x</strong> version, and migrate it to <strong>UAF 1.2</strong>.<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="91853a5f-5223-4747-a81f-f6576a57d472"><span>Open the <strong>UAF 1.2</strong> project with the modeling tool <strong>2026x</strong> version and migrate it to <strong>UAF 1.3</strong>. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565451/Migration+from+UAF+1.2+to+UAF+1.3" rel="nofollow">Learn more &gt;&gt;&gt;</a></span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565530 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06530: UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. New elements

- page_id: `249565530`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565530/UPDM+2.1+to+UAF+1.2.+New+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:36.344+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Elements mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| UAF 1.3 | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Achieved Effect | A dependency relationship that exists between an Actual State (e.g., observed/measured during testing) of an element that attempts to achieve a Desired Effect and an Achiever. |
| Achiever | An Actual Resource, Actual Project or Actual Enterprise Phase that can deliver a Desired Effect. |
| Actual Condition | The Actual State of an environment or location describing its situation. |
| Actual Effect | A real world phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon. |
| Actual Enduring Task | An actual undertaking recognized by an enterprise as being essential to achieving its goals - i.e. a strategic specification of what the enterprise does. |
| Actual Enterprise Phase | An Actual State that describes the phase of an Enterprise endeavour. |
| Actual Environment | The Actual State that describes the circumstances of an Environment. |
| Actual Outcome | Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an "end effect". |
| Actual Project Role | An Actual Project that is applied to a Project Role. |
| Actual Resource | Role in an Organization, where the role carries the authority to undertake a function - though the Actual Organizational Resource given the role has the responsibility. |
| Actual Resource Role | An instance of a System Resource. |
| Actual Responsibility | The duty required of a Person or Organization. |
| Actual Responsible Resource | An abstract grouping of responsible Organizational Resources. |
| Actual Service | An instance of a Service Specification. |
| Actual State | Abstract element that applies temporal extent to a set of elements realized as Instance Specifications. |
| Architecture | An abstract element that represents a generic architecture. Subtypes are Logical Architecture and Physical Architecture. |
| Asset | Asset as applied to Security views, an abstract element that indicates the types of elements that can be considered as a subject for security analysis. |
| Asset Role | Asset Role as applied to Security views, an abstract element that indicates the type of elements that can be considered as a subject for security analysis in the particular context. |
| Capability For Task | An abstraction relationship that asserts that a Capability is required in order for an Enterprise to conduct a phase of an Enduring Task. |
| Capability Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Resource, Security, Service, and Strategic. |
| Challenge | An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals. |
| Challenge Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge. The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind: Business, Enterprise, Mission, Other, and Strategic. |
| Compares To | An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result. |
| Competence To Conduct | An abstraction relationship used to associate a Function with a specific set of Competencies needed to conduct the Function |
| Concern | Interest in an Enterprise Phase (Enterprise Phase is synonym for System in ISO 42010) relevant to one or more of its stakeholders. |
| Consumes | A Consumes relationship is an abstraction relationship that asserts that a service in someway contributes or assists in the execution of an Operational Activity. |
| Creates | A dependency relationship denoting that an Actual Strategic Phase brings into existence a Strategic Asset. |
| Data Role | A usage of Data Element that exists in the context of an Resource Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Data Elements. |
| Driver | A factor which will have a significant impact on the activities, and goals of an enterprise. |
| Driver Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Driver. The following are enumeration literals for DriverKind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Project, Resource, Security, Service, Standard, and Strategic. |
| Effect | A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes. |
| Enables | A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Enhances | A dependency relationship relating the Tailored Security Control to a Security Control. |
| Enterprise Mission | Mission captures at a high level what you will do to realize your vision. |
| Enterprise Objective | A statement of an attainable, time-targeted, and measurable target that the enterprise seeks to meet in order to achieve its Goals. |
| Evoked By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Risk is drawn out by an Opportunity. |
| Fielded Capability | An actual, fully-realized capability. A Fielded Capability is typed by a Capability Configuration. |
| Governed By | An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs. |
| Impacted By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an Opportunity. |
| Information Role | A usage of Information Element that exists in the context of an Operational Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Information Elements. |
| Maps To Goal | A dependency relationship denoting that some Strategic Information contributes to achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Measurable Element | Abstract grouping for elements that can be measured by applying Measurement Sets to them. |
| Mitigation Role | An abstract element that indicates the types of elements that can be considered as a subject for mitigating against a risk. |
| Motivated By | A dependency relationship denoting the reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way. |
| Operational Asset | An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Information Element allowing them to own Information Roles. |
| Operational Interface | A declaration that specifies a contract between the Nodes it is related to and any other Nodes it can interact with. |
| Operational Mitigation | A set of security measures intended to address against specific cyber risks. Comprises a subset of Security Controls that are required to protect the asset at node (Operational Role). |
| Operational Signal | An Operational Signal is a specification of a kind of communication between operational performers in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply. |
| Opportunity | An existing or potential favorable circumstance or combination of circumstances which can be advantageous for addressing enterprise Challenges. |
| Organization In Enterprise | An abstraction relationship relating an Actual Organization to an Actual Enterprise Phase to denote that the Actual Organization plays a role or is a stakeholder in an Actual Enterprise Phase. |
| Owns Value | An abstraction relationship denoting that an Actual Organizational Resource owns a Value Item. |
| Performs In Context | An abstraction relationship that relates an Operational Action to a Operational Role, or a Function Action to a Resource Role. It indicates that the action can be carried out by the role when used in a specific context or configuration. |
| Phases | An abstraction relationship that exists between a Phaseable Element and an Actual Strategic Phase that it is assigned to. |
| Presented By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Challenge must be overcome for addressing a Driver. |
| Project Activity | An activity carried out during a project. |
| Project Activity Action | The Project Activity Action is defined as a call behavior action that invokes the activity that needs to be preformed. |
| Project Role | Usage of a Project in the context of another Project. Creates a whole-part relationship. |
| Protects | A dependency that asserts that a Security Control is required to protect an Asset. |
| Protects In Context | A dependency relationship that relates a Security Control Action to a Operational Role, or a Resource Role. It indicates that Security Control is required to protect an Asset in a specific context or configuration. |
| Protocol Stack | A sub-type of Protocol that contains the Protocol Layers, defining a complete stack. |
| Required Service Level | A sub type of Actual Service that details a specific service level required of the provider. |
| Resource Asset | An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Data Element allowing them to own Data Roles |
| Resource Interface | A declaration that specifies a contract between the System Resources it is related to and any other System Resources it can interact with. It is also intended to be an implementation of a specification of an Interface in the Business and/or Service layer. |
| Resource Mitigation | A set of security measures intended to address specific cyber risks. Comprises a subset of Tailored Security Controls that are used to protect the asset at resource (Resource Role). |
| Resource Service | A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services. |
| Resource Service Interface | A contract that defines the Resource Methods and Resource Signal receptions that the Resource Services realize. |
| Resource Signal | A Resource Signal is a specification of a kind of communication between resources (ResourcePerformers) in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply. |
| Risk | A statement of the impact of an event on Assets. It represents a constraint on an Asset in terms of adverse effects, with an associated measure of the likelihood of the event’s occurrence. Software related security risks are those risks that arise from the loss of confidentiality, integrity, or availability of information or information systems. |
| Security Constraint | A type of rule that captures a formal statement to define access control policy language. |
| Security Control | A type of Operational Activity that specifies a safeguard or countermeasure prescribed for Operational Performer. It is intended to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of its information. |
| Security Control Action | A call of a Security Control in the context of another Security Control. It is used to show how a set of Security Controls can be used to protect an asset at node (Operation Role). |
| Security Control Family | An element that organizes security controls into a family. |
| Security Endave | An element that is a collection of information systems connected by one or more internal networks under the control of a single authority and security policy. The systems may be structured by physical proximity or by function, independent of location. |
| Security Process | The security-related procedure that satisfies the security control requirement. |
| Security Process Action | A call of a Security Control in the context of another Security Control. It is used to show how a set of Security Controls can be used to protect an asset at node (Node Role). |
| Security Risk | The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65]. |
| Sequence | A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another. |
| Service Architecture | An element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Services perspective. |
| Service Connector | A channel for exchange between two Service Specifications. Where one acts as the consumer of the other. |
| Service Contract | A constraint governing the use of one or more Services. |
| Service Control Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Services (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Service Exchange Item | An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange. |
| Service Exchange Kind | Enumeration of the possible kindsEnumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange.The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers. |
| Service Object Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Signal | A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply. |
| Service Signal Property | A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal. |
| Service Specification | The specification of a set of functionality provided by one element for the use of others. |
| Service Specification Role | Usage of a Service Specification in the context of another Service Specification. Creates a whole-part relationship. |
| Strategic Constraint | A Rule governing a Capability. |
| Strategic Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Strategic Information | Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action. |
| Subject Of Risk | An abstract grouping of elements that can be the subject of a Risk. |
| Subject Of Security Constraint | An abstract grouping of elements that can be the subject of a Security Constraint. |
| Tailored Security Control | A type of Function that implements a Security Control, it specifies a safeguard or countermeasure prescribed for a System Resource. It is intended to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of its information. |
| Technology | A sub type of Resource Artifact that indicates a technology domain, i.e. nuclear, mechanical, electronic, mobile telephony etc. |
| Value Item | An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view. |
| Value Item Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time. |
| Value Stream | An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020]. |
| Version Succession | A dependency relationship between two Version Of Configurations that denotes that one Version Of Configuration follows from another. |
| Versioned Element | An abstract grouping of System Resource and Service Specification that allows Version Of Configuration to be related to Actual Project Milestones. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table class="relative-table"><colgroup><col style="width: 295.0px;" /> <col style="width: 1527.0px;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th colspan="1">UAF 1.3</th><th colspan="1">Description</th></tr><tr><td>Achieved Effect</td><td>A dependency relationship that exists between an Actual State (e.g., observed/measured during testing) of an element that attempts to achieve a Desired Effect and an Achiever.</td></tr><tr><td>Achiever</td><td>An Actual Resource, Actual Project or Actual Enterprise Phase that can deliver a Desired Effect.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Condition</td><td>The Actual State of an environment or location describing its situation.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Effect</td><td>A real world phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Enduring Task</td><td>An actual undertaking recognized by an enterprise as being essential to achieving its goals - i.e. a strategic specification of what the enterprise does.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Enterprise Phase</td><td>An Actual State that describes the phase of an Enterprise endeavour.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Environment</td><td>The Actual State that describes the circumstances of an Environment.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Outcome</td><td>Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an &quot;end effect&quot;.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Project Role</td><td>An Actual Project that is applied to a Project Role.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Resource</td><td>Role in an Organization, where the role carries the authority to undertake a function - though the Actual Organizational Resource given the role has the responsibility.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Resource Role</td><td>An instance of a System Resource.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Responsibility</td><td>The duty required of a Person or Organization.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Responsible Resource</td><td>An abstract grouping of responsible Organizational Resources.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Service</td><td>An instance of a Service Specification.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual State</td><td>Abstract element that applies temporal extent to a set of elements realized as Instance Specifications.</td></tr><tr><td>Architecture</td><td>An abstract element that represents a generic architecture. Subtypes are Logical Architecture and Physical Architecture.</td></tr><tr><td>Asset</td><td>Asset as applied to Security views, an abstract element that indicates the types of elements that can be considered as a subject for security analysis.</td></tr><tr><td>Asset Role</td><td>Asset Role as applied to Security views, an abstract element that indicates the type of elements that can be considered as a subject for security analysis in the particular context.</td></tr><tr><td>Capability For Task</td><td>An abstraction relationship that asserts that a Capability is required in order for an Enterprise to conduct a phase of an Enduring Task.</td></tr><tr><td>Capability Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Resource, Security, Service, and Strategic.</td></tr><tr><td>Challenge</td><td>An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals.</td></tr><tr><td>Challenge Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge. The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind: Business, Enterprise, Mission, Other, and Strategic.</td></tr><tr><td>Compares To</td><td>An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result.</td></tr><tr><td>Competence To Conduct</td><td>An abstraction relationship used to associate a Function with a specific set of Competencies needed to conduct the Function</td></tr><tr><td>Concern</td><td>Interest in an Enterprise Phase (Enterprise Phase is synonym for System in ISO 42010) relevant to one or more of its stakeholders.</td></tr><tr><td>Consumes</td><td> A Consumes relationship is an abstraction relationship that asserts that a service in someway contributes or assists in the execution of an Operational Activity.</td></tr><tr><td>Creates</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that an Actual Strategic Phase brings into existence a Strategic Asset.</td></tr><tr><td>Data Role</td><td>A usage of Data Element that exists in the context of an Resource Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Data Elements.</td></tr><tr><td>Driver</td><td>A factor which will have a significant impact on the activities, and goals of an enterprise.</td></tr><tr><td>Driver Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Driver. The following are enumeration literals for DriverKind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Project, Resource, Security, Service, Standard, and Strategic.</td></tr><tr><td>Effect</td><td>A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes.</td></tr><tr><td>Enables</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective.</td></tr><tr><td>Enhances</td><td>A dependency relationship relating the Tailored Security Control to a Security Control.</td></tr><tr><td>Enterprise Mission</td><td>Mission captures at a high level what you will do to realize your vision.</td></tr><tr><td>Enterprise Objective</td><td>A statement of an attainable, time-targeted, and measurable target that the enterprise seeks to meet in order to achieve its Goals.</td></tr><tr><td>Evoked By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that a Risk is drawn out by an Opportunity.</td></tr><tr><td>Fielded Capability</td><td>An actual, fully-realized capability. A Fielded Capability is typed by a Capability Configuration.</td></tr><tr><td>Governed By</td><td>An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs.</td></tr><tr><td>Impacted By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an Opportunity.</td></tr><tr><td>Information Role</td><td>A usage of Information Element that exists in the context of an Operational Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Information Elements. </td></tr><tr><td>Maps To Goal</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that some Strategic Information contributes to achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective.</td></tr><tr><td>Measurable Element</td><td>Abstract grouping for elements that can be measured by applying Measurement Sets to them.</td></tr><tr><td>Mitigation Role</td><td>An abstract element that indicates the types of elements that can be considered as a subject for mitigating against a risk.</td></tr><tr><td>Motivated By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting the reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way.</td></tr><tr><td>Operational Asset</td><td>An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Information Element allowing them to own Information Roles.</td></tr><tr><td>Operational Interface</td><td>A declaration that specifies a contract between the Nodes it is related to and any other Nodes it can interact with.</td></tr><tr><td>Operational Mitigation</td><td>A set of security measures intended to address against specific cyber risks. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565528 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 06531: UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Removed elements and stereotypes

- page_id: `249565528`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565528/UPDM+2.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Removed+elements+and+stereotypes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:36.260+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Elements mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All removed elements are no longer used in UAF 1.3 and do not map to the other elements.

If the element, element property, or relationship does not exists in UAF 1.2, the additional information is added into the migrated element's To Do property. For more information about how to use To Do property, see in the page [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using To Do property' space=''].

| UPDM 2.1 | UAF 1.3 |
| --- | --- |
| Activity Part Of Project | Dependency |
| Activity Subject |  |
| Asynchronous Message | Signal |
| Association Of Information | Association |
| Competence Provider |  |
| Competence Requirer |  |
| Condition Kind |  |
| Condition Property | Property |
| Condition Type |  |
| Contract [Information Flow] | Information Flow |
| Design Rule |  |
| Desired State |  |
| Details | Dependency |
| Enduring Task |  |
| Entity Attribute | Property |
| Entity Item | Operational Information |
| Entity Relationship | Association |
| External Individual |  |
| External Type |  |
| External Tuple |  |
| External Tuple Type |  |
| Geo Political Extent | Instance Specification |
| Known Resource | Operational Role |
| Mission | Operational Activity |
| Ontology Reference |  |
| Operational Event Trace | Interaction |
| Operational State | State |
| Overlap |  |
| Participant | Class |
| Process |  |
| Project | Actual Project |
| Resource Event Trace | Interaction |
| Resource State | State |
| Semantic | Data Type |
| Semantic Attribute | Property |
| Service Description | Architectural Description |
| Service Feature |  |
| Service Function Edge | Service Object FlowService Control Flow |
| Service Interaction | Interaction |
| Service Level Value | Actual Measurement |
| Service Message Handler | Reception |
| Standard Configuration | Comment without stereotype |
| Stereotype Extension | Comment |
| Subject Of Operational State Machine |  |
| Transactional | Data Type |
| Transactional Attribute | Property |
| View |  |
| Viewpoint |  |
| Wrapper | Class |
| Wrapper Attribute | Property |

**Removed properties:**

- Concern::enterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: vision
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: goal
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: concern
- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect
- DesiredEffect::achievedEffect
- EnterpriseGoal::enterprisePhase
- EnterpriseVision::enterprisePhase
- Risk::riskOwner

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>All removed elements are no longer used in UAF 1.3 and do not map to the other elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9412190d-a793-4d3f-9f98-c169823c85d9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the element, element property, or relationship does not exists in <span>UAF 1</span>.2, the additional information is added into the migrated element's To Do property. For more information about how to use To Do property, see in the page <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using To Do property" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 28.0307%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 49.6078%;" /><col style="width: 50.3922%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>UPDM 2.1</th><th colspan="1">UAF 1.3</th></tr><tr><td>Activity Part Of Project</td><td colspan="1">Dependency</td></tr><tr><td>Activity Subject</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td>Asynchronous Message</td><td colspan="1">Signal</td></tr><tr><td>Association Of Information</td><td colspan="1">Association</td></tr><tr><td>Competence Provider</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td>Competence Requirer</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Condition Kind</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td>Condition Property</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td>Desired State</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td>Details</td><td colspan="1">Dependency</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Enduring Task</td><td colspan="1"><br Resource</td><td colspan="1">Operational Role</td></tr><tr><td>Mission</td><td colspan="1">Operational Activity</td></tr><tr><td>Ontology Reference</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td>Operational Event Trace</td><td colspan="1">Interaction</td></tr><tr><td>Operational State</td><td colspan="1">State</td></tr><tr><td>Overlap</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td>Participant</td><td colspan="1">Class</td></tr><tr><td>Process</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td>Project</td><td colspan="1">Actual Project</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Event Trace</td><td colspan="1">Interaction</td></tr><tr><td>Resource State</td><td colspan="1">State</td></tr><tr><td>Semantic</td><td colspan="1">Data Type</td></tr><tr><td>Semantic goal</li><li>ActualEnterprisePhase:: concern</li><li>AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect</li><li>DesiredEffect::achievedEffect</li><li>EnterpriseGoal::enterprisePhase</li><li>EnterpriseVision::enterprisePhase</li><li>Risk::riskOwner</li></ul>
````
