<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565526 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06532: UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Renamed and replaced elements

- page_id: `249565526`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565526/UPDM+2.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Renamed+and+replaced+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:02:14.060+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Elements mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The replaced elements mean that their names and/or metaclasses are changed. Pay attention, that some elements from UPDM 2.1 are replaced to one element in UAF 1.2.

| UPDM 2.1 | UAF 1.2 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Activity Part Of Capability [Dependency] | Maps To Capability [Abstraction] | Maps To Capability relationship denotes that an Activity contributes to providing a Capability. |
| Activity Performed By Performer [Dependency]Is Capable Of Performing [Dependency] | Is Capable To Perform [Abstraction] | Is Capable To Perform relationship defines the traceability between the Capable Elements to the Activities that they can perform. |
| Actual Organization Relationship | Actual Resource Relationship | Actual Resource Relationship is an abstract element that details the Actual Organizational Resources that are able to carry out an Actual Responsibility. |
| Actual Property | Actual Measurement | Actual Measurement is an actual value that is applied to a Measurement. |
| Capability Of Performer [Dependency] | Exhibits [Abstraction] | Exhibits relationship that exists between a capable element and a Capability that it meets under specific environmental conditions. |
| Capability Property | Capability Role | A high level specification of the enterprise's ability to execute a specified course of action. |
| ClimateLight Condition | Environment | A definition of the environmental factors in which something exists or functions. The definition of an Environment element can be further defined using EnvironmentKind. |
| Desired Effect | Desires | A dependency relationship relating the Desirer (a Capability or OrganizationalResource) to an ActualState. |
| Deployed MilestoneNo Longer Used MilestoneOut Of Service MilestoneIncrement MilestoneActual Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone is an event with a start date in an Actual Project from which progress is measured. Actual Project Milestone can have such kinds:In Service.Deployed.No Longer Used.Out Of Service.Other. |
| EnergyMateriel | Natural Resource | Natural Resource is any type of physical resource that occurs in nature such as oil, water, gas, or coal. |
| Entity Item | Operational Information | An item of information that flows between OperationalPerformers and is produced and consumed by the OperationalActivities that the OperationalPerformers are capable to perform (see IsCapableToPerform). |
| Exchange Element | Resource InformationOperational Information | If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Resource Interaction, it is migrated to Data Element. The Data Element is a formalized representation of data that is managed by or exchanged between systems.If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Operational Exchange or wasn't conveyed, it is migrated to Information Element. The Information Element is an item of information that flows between Operational Performers and is produced and consumed by the Operational Activities that the Operational Performers are capable to perform (see Is Capable To Perform). |
| Expose | Dependency | A Dependency relationship will be used instead of Expose relationship. |
| Functional Standard | Standard | A ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture. |
| Function Edge [Activity Edge] | Function Control Flow [Control Flow]Function Object Flow [Object Flow] | Function Edge is an abstract grouping for Function Control Flow and Function Object Flow.Function Object Flow connects Function Actions using Pins.Function Control Flow connects Function Actions directly. |
| Project | Actual Project | Actual Project defines a time-limited endeavor to provide a specific set of Actual Resources that meet specific Capability needs. |
| Individual Person Role | Actual Post | An actual, specific post, is an instance of a Post "type" - e.g., "President of the United States of America" where the Post would be president. |
| Location | Actual Location | Actual Location is an Actual State that describes a physical location, for example using text to provide an address, Geo-coordinates, etc. |
| Location Type | Location | Location element denote a specification of the generic area in which a location holder is required to be located. |
| Logical Architecture | Operational Architecture | Operational Architecture is an element used to denote a model of the architecture, described from the business operational perspective. |
| Measure | Actual Measurement Set | Actual Measurement Set denote a set of Actual Measurements. |
| Measure TypeSecurity Attributes Group | Measurement Set | Measurement Set denote a set of Measurements. |
| Milestone Sequence | Milestone Dependency | Milestone Dependency relationship is relationship between two Actual Project Milestones that denotes one Actual Project Milestone follows from another. |
| Mission | Operational Activity | An Activity that captures a logical process, specified independently of how the process is carried out. |
| Needline | Operational Connector | Operational Connector is a Connector that goes between Operational Roles representing a need to exchange resources. It can carry a number of Operational Exchanges. |
| Node Operation | Operational Method | Operational Method is a behavioral feature of a Operational Performer whose behavior is specified in an Operational Activity. |
| Node Parent | Operational Agent | An abstract element grouping Operational Architecture and Operational Performer. |
| Node Port | Operational Port | Operational Port is used of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. The whole-part relationship is created. |
| Node Role | Operational Role | Usage of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. Creates a whole-part relationship. |
| Operational Activity Edge [Activity Edge] | Operational Control Flow [Control Flow]Operational Object Flow [Object Flow] | Operational Activity Edge is an abstract grouping for Operational Control Flow and Operational Object Flow.Operational Object Flow connects Operational Activity Actions using Pins.Operational Control Flow connects Operational Activity Actions directly. |
| Organizational Project RelationshipProject Ownership | Responsible For | Responsible For relationship is relationship between an Actual Responsible Resource and an Actual Responsibility or Actual Project. It defines the duties that the Actual Responsible Resource is Responsible For. |
| Organization Type | Organization | Organization element denote a group of organizational resources (Persons, Posts, Organizations and Responsibilities) associated for a particular purpose. |
| NodePerformerSecurity Domain | Operational Performer | Operational Performer denote a logical entity that Is Capable To Perform Operational Activities which produce, consume and process resources. |
| Person Type | Post | Post denote a type of job title or position that a person can fill (e.g. Lawyer, Solution Architect, Machine Operator or Chief Executive Officer). |
| Physical Architecture | Resource Architecture | Resource Architecture is an element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the System Resource perspective. |
| Project Type | Project | Project is an element that describes types of time-limited endeavours that are required to meet one or more Capability needs. |
| Property | Measurement | Measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. |
| RequestService | Operational PortResource Port | Operational Port is used of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. The whole-part relationship is created.If Request port belongs to System Resource it is migrated to Resource Port.If Request port belongs to Operational Performer it is migrated to Operational Port.Resource Port is an interaction point for a System Resource through which it can interact with the outside environment and which is defined by a Resource Connector. If Service port belongs to System Resource it is migrated to Resource Port.If Service port belongs to Operational Performer it is migrated to Operational Port. |
| Resource Interaction | Resource Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between ResourcePerformers (i.e. flows of data, people, material, or energy). |
| Resource Interaction Item | Resource Exchange Item | Resource Exchange Item is an abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between System Resources and conveyed by a Resource Exchange. |
| Resource Interface | Resource Connector | Resource Connector is a channel for exchange between two Resource Roles. |
| Resource State Machine | Resource State Description | A state machine describing the behavior of a System Resource, depicting how the System Resource responds to various events and the actions. |
| Resource Operation | Resource Method | A behavioral feature of a ResourcePerformer whose behavior is specified in a Function. |
| Role Type | Responsibility | Responsibility element is the type of duty required of a Person or Organization. |
| Service Access | Resource Service | Capability Configuration represents a composite structure the physical and human resources (and their interactions) in an enterprise, assembled to meet a Capability.Service Specification defines the specification of a set of functionality provided by one element for the use of others. |
| Service Attribute | Flow Property | Flow Property is a SysML v1 element with already defined direction that is inout. A Flow Property signifies a single flow element that can flow to/from a Block. A flow property’s values are either received from or transmitted to an external Block. Flow properties are defined directly on Blocks or Flow Specifications that are those specifications which type the Flow Ports. Flow properties enable item flows across connectors connecting parts of the corresponding block types, either directly (in case of the property is defined on the Block) or via Flow Ports. |
| Service Channel | Resource ConnectorOperational Connector | If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is System Resource it is be migrated to Resource Connector.If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is Operational Performer it is be migrated to Operational Connector. |
| Service Level Value Set | Provided Service Level | Provided Service Level is a sub type of Actual Service that details a specific service level delivered by the provider. |
| Service State Machine | Service State Description | Service State Description is a state machine describing the behavior of a Service Specification, depicting how the Service Specification responds to various events and the actions. |
| Skill | Competence | Competence element is a specific set of abilities defined by knowledge, skills and aptitude. |
| Skill Of Person Type | Provides Competence | Provides Competence is a dependency relationship that asserts that an Actual Organizational Resource provides a specific set of Competencies. |
| Standard Configuration | Comment | The Standard Configuration is replaced by the Comment. |
| System | Resource Artifact | Resource Artifact element is a type of man-made object that contains no human beings (i.e. satellite, radio, petrol, gasoline, etc.). |
| System Resource | Resource Performer | The Resource Performer covers the Resource Architecture, , Known Resource.Physical Resource |
| Service Operation | Service Method | A behavioral feature of a Service Specification whose behavior is specified in a Service Function. |
| Technical Standard | Standard | Standard is a ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture. |
| Trustline | Operational Exchange | Operational Exchange asserts that a flow can exist between Operational Performers (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Vision | Enterprise Vision | An Enterprise Vision describes the future state of the enterprise, without regard to how it is to be achieved. |
| Activity Part Of Project [Dependency]Overlap [Dependency] | Dependency | The Dependency relationship is used instead of stereotyped dependencies such as Activity Part Of Project and Overlap. |
| Association Of Information [Association]Entity Relationship [Association] | Association | The Association relationship is used instead of stereotyped associations such as Association Of Information and Entity Relationship. |
| Asynchronous Message [Signal] | Signal | The Signal element is used instead of stereotyped signal element Asynchronous Message. |
| Condition Property [Property]Entity Attribute [Property] | Property | The Property is used instead of stereotyped properties such as Condition Property and Entity Attribute. |
| Design Rule [Constraint] | Constraint | The Constraint is used instead of stereotyped constraint Design Rule. |
| Geo Political Extent [Instance Specification] | Instance Specification | The Instance Specification is used instead of stereotyped instance specification Geo Political Extent . |
| Logical Data Model [Data Model]Physical Data Model [Data Model] | Information Model | The Data Model is used instead of stereotyped data models such as Logical Data Model and Physical Data Model. |
| Operational Event Trace [Interaction]Resource Event Trace [Interaction]Service Interaction [Interaction] | Interaction | The Interaction is used instead of stereotyped interactions such as Operational Event Trace, Resource Event Trace, and Service Interaction. |
| Operational State [State]Resource State [State] | State | The State is used instead of stereotyped states such as Operational State and Resource State. |
| Project Activity Edge [Activity Edge] | ControlFlowObjectFlow | The Activity Edge is used instead of stereotyped activity edge Project Activity Edge. An Activity Edge is abstract abstract.Object Flow connects using Pins.Control Flow connects directly. |
| Service Description [Package] | Architectural Description | The Package is used instead of stereotyped package Service Description. |
| Service Function Edge [Activity Edge] | Service Control Flow [Control Flow]Service Object Flow [Object Flow] | Service Function Edge is an abstract grouping for Service Control Flow and Service Object Flow.Service Object Flow connects Service Function Actions using Pins.Service Control Flow connects Service Function Actions directly. |
| Service Level Value [Slot] | Slot | The Slot is used instead of stereotyped slot Service Level Value. |
| Service Message Handler [Reception] | Reception | The Reception is used instead of stereotyped reception Service Message Handler. |
| UPDM Element | UAF Element | Abstract super type for all of the UAF elements. It provides a way for all of the UAF elements to have a common set of properties. |

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.3 profile.

| Element | Property UPDM 2.1 [name] | Property UAF 1.3 [name] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Viewpoint | languagesmethods | languagemethod |
| Property | Information Role | Operational Information Role |
| Property | Data Role | Resource Information Role |
| Property | Service Specification Role | Service Role |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Operational Port.</p><p>Resource Port is an interaction point for a System Resource through which it can interact with the outside environment and which is defined by a Resource Connector. <span> </span></p><p>If Service port belongs to System Resource it is migrated to Resource Port.</p><p>If Service port belongs to Operational Performer it is migrated to<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> Operational Port</span>.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Resource Interaction</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Resource Exchange</span></td><td colspan="1">Asserts that a flow can exist between ResourcePerformers (i.e. flows of data, people, material, or energy).</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Interaction Item</td><td colspan="1">Resource Exchange Item</td><td colspan="1">Resource Exchange Item is an abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between System Resources and conveyed by a Resource Exchange.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Interface</td><td colspan="1">Resource Connector</td><td colspan="1">Resource Connector is a channel for exchange between two Resource Roles.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource State Machine</td><td colspan="1">Resource State Description</td><td colspan="1">A state machine describing the behavior of a System Resource, depicting how the System Resource responds to various events and the actions.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Operation</td><td colspan="1">Resource Method</td><td colspan="1">A behavioral feature of a ResourcePerformer whose behavior is specified in a Function.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Role Type</td><td colspan="1">Responsibility</td><td colspan="1">Responsibility element is the type of duty required of a Person or Organization.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Access</td><td colspan="1"><p>Resource Service</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Capability Configuration represents a composite structure the physical and human resources (and their interactions) in an enterprise, assembled to meet a Capability.</p><p>Service Specification defines the specification of a set of functionality provided by one element for the use of others.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Attribute</td><td colspan="1">Flow Property</td><td colspan="1"><p>Flow Property is a SysML v1 element with already defined direction that is <em>inout</em>. A Flow Property signifies a single flow element that can flow to/from a Block. A flow property’s values are either received from or transmitted to an external Block. Flow properties are defined directly on Blocks or Flow Specifications that are those specifications which type the Flow Ports. Flow properties enable item flows across connectors connecting parts of the corresponding block types, either directly (in case of the property is defined on the Block) or via Flow Ports.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Channel</td><td colspan="1"><p>Resource Connector</p><p>Operational Connector</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is System Resource it is be migrated to Resource Connector.</p><p>If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is Operational Performer it is be migrated to Operational Connector.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Level Value Set</td><td colspan="1">Provided Service Level</td><td colspan="1">Provided Service Level is a sub type of Actual Service that details a specific service level delivered by the provider.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service State Machine</td><td colspan="1">Service State Description</td><td colspan="1">Service State Description is a state machine describing the behavior of a Service Specification, depicting how the Service Specification responds to various events and the actions.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Skill</td><td colspan="1">Competence</td><td colspan="1">Competence element is a specific set of abilities defined by knowledge, skills and aptitude.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Skill Of Person Type</td><td colspan="1">Provides Competence</td><td colspan="1">Provides Competence is a dependency relationship that asserts that an Actual Organizational Resource provides a specific set of Competencies.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Standard Configuration</td><td colspan="1">Comment</td><td colspan="1">The Standard Configuration is replaced by the Comment.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">System</td><td colspan="1">Resource Artifact</td><td colspan="1">Resource Artifact element is a type of man-made object that contains no human beings (i.e. satellite, radio, petrol, gasoline, etc.).</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">System Resource</td><td colspan="1">Resource Performer</td><td colspan="1">The Resource Performer covers the Resource Architecture, , Known Resource.Physical Resource</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Operation</td><td colspan="1">Service Method</td><td colspan="1">A behavioral feature of a Service Specification whose behavior is specified in a Service Function.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Technical Standard</td><td colspan="1">Standard</td><td colspan="1">Standard is a ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Trustline</td><td colspan="1">Operational Exchange</td><td colspan="1">Operational Exchange asserts that a flow can exist between Operational Performers (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Vision</td><td colspan="1">Enterprise Vision</td><td colspan="1">An Enterprise Vision describes the future state of the enterprise, without regard to how it is to be achieved.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Activity Part Of Project [Dependency]</p><p>Overlap [Dependency]</p></td><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1">The Dependency relationship is used instead of stereotyped dependencies such as Activity Part Of Project and Overlap.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Association Of Information [Association]</p><p>Entity Relationship [Association]</p></td><td colspan="1">Association</td><td colspan="1">The Association relationship is used instead of stereotyped associations such as Association Of Information and Entity Relationship.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Asynchronous Message [Signal]</td><td colspan="1">Signal</td><td colspan="1">The Signal element is used instead of stereotyped signal element Asynchronous Message.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Condition Property [Property]</p><p>Entity Attribute [Property]</p></td><td colspan="1">Property</td><td colspan="1">The Property is used instead of stereotyped properties such as Condition Property and Entity Attribute.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Design Rule [Constraint]</td><td colspan="1">Constraint</td><td colspan="1">The Constraint is used instead of stereotyped constraint Design Rule.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Geo Political Extent [Instance Specification]</td><td colspan="1">Instance Specification</td><td colspan="1">The Instance Specification is used instead of stereotyped instance specification Geo Political Extent .</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Logical Data Model [Data Model]</p><p>Physical Data Model [Data Model]</p></td><td colspan="1">Information Model</td><td colspan="1">The Data Model is used instead of stereotyped data models such as Logical Data Model and Physical Data Model.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Operational Event Trace [Interaction]</p><p>Resource Event Trace [Interaction]</p><p>Service Interaction [Interaction]</p></td><td colspan="1">Interaction</td><td colspan="1">The Interaction is used instead of stereotyped interactions such as Operational Event Trace, Resource Event Trace, and Service Interaction.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Operational State [State]</p><p>Resource State [State]</p></td><td colspan="1">State</td><td colspan="1">The State is used instead of stereotyped states such as Operational State and Resource State.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Project Activity Edge [Activity Edge]</td><td colspan="1"><p>ControlFlow</p><p>ObjectFlow</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>The Activity Edge is used instead of stereotyped activity edge Project Activity Edge. An Activity Edge is abstract abstract.</p><p>Object Flow connects using Pins.</p><p>Control Flow connects directly.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Description [Package]</td><td colspan="1">Architectural Description</td><td colspan="1">The Package is used instead of stereotyped package Service Description.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Function Edge [Activity Edge]</td><td colspan="1"><p>Service Control Flow [Control Flow]</p><p>Service Object Flow [Object Flow]</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Service Function Edge is an abstract grouping for Service Control Flow and Service Object Flow.</p><p>Service Object Flow connects Service Function Actions using Pins.</p><p>Service Control Flow connects Service Function Actions directly.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Level Value [Slot]</td><td colspan="1">Slot</td><td colspan="1">The Slot is used instead of stereotyped slot Service Level Value.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Message Handler [Reception]</td><td colspan="1"><p>Reception</p></td><td colspan="1">The Reception is used instead of stereotyped reception Service Message Handler.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">UPDM Element</td><td colspan="1">UAF Element</td><td colspan="1">Abstract super type for all of the UAF elements. It provides a way for all of the UAF elements to have a common set of properties.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following table describes properties  which are renamed in UAF 1.3 profile.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="1"><p>Element</p></th><th><p>Property UPDM 2.1 [name]</p></th><th><p>Property UAF 1.3 [name]</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="1">Viewpoint</td><td colspan="1"><p>languages</p><p>methods </p></td><td colspan="1"><p>language</p><p>method</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Property</td><td colspan="1"><span>Information Role</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Operational Information Role</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Property</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Data Role</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Resource Information Role</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Property</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Service Specification Role</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Service Role</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565532 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06533: UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. View mapping

- page_id: `249565532`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565532/UPDM+2.1+to+UAF+1.2.+View+mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:36.427+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 2.1 and UAF 1.2.

| UPDM 2.1 | UAF 1.2 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Service Channels Summary Table | Deleted. | The Service Channels Summary Table is no longer used for service channels analysis, creation, and modification. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector. |
| Service Channels Summary Matrix | Deleted. | The Service Channels Summary Matrix is no longer used for service channels analysis and creation. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 2.1 and <span>UAF 1</span>.2.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>UPDM 2.1</th><th><span>UAF 1</span>.2</th><th>Comments</th></tr><tr><td>Service Channels Summary Table</td><td>Deleted.</td><td>The Service Channels Summary Table is no longer used for service channels analysis, creation, and modification. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Channels Summary Matrix</td><td>Deleted.</td><td>The Service Channels Summary Matrix is no longer used for service channels analysis and creation. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565518 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06534: UPDM projects migration manual

- page_id: `249565518`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565518/UPDM+projects+migration+manual
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:35.929+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of this manual is to help you understanding differences between UPDM specifications and guide you along the data mapping process.

The following chapters describe how the migration works.

##### Basic Concepts and Instructions

The Unified Profile for DoDAF/MODAF (UPDM) is the standard of an Object Management Group (OMG) initiative to develop a modeling standard that supports both the US Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) and UK Ministry of Defense Architecture Framework (MODAF).

The purpose of UPDM migration to the latest UPDM specification version is to convert earlier UPDM versions metamodel-based projects into the latest UPDM version metamodel-based projects without losing data.

A migration back to earlier UPDM versions is not possible, so we highly recommend you to back-up your current UPDM projects before proceeding with migration to the latest UPDM version.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The purpose of this manual is to help you understanding differences between UPDM specifications and guide you along the data mapping process.</p><p>The following chapters describe how the migration works.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2c93637d-a5d3-424f-bdae-9a0f68668ae1" /></p><h3>Basic Concepts and Instructions</h3><p>The Unified Profile for DoDAF/MODAF (UPDM) is the standard of an Object Management Group (OMG) initiative to develop a modeling standard that supports both the US Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) and UK Ministry of Defense Architecture Framework (MODAF).</p><p>The purpose of UPDM migration to the latest UPDM specification version is to convert earlier UPDM versions metamodel-based projects into the latest UPDM version metamodel-based projects without losing data.</p><p>A migration back to earlier UPDM versions is not possible, so we highly recommend you to back-up your current UPDM projects before proceeding with migration to the latest UPDM version.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971118 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06535: Uploading generated reports to servers

- page_id: `243971118`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971118/Uploading+generated+reports+to+servers
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:24:23.353+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Generating reports from the command line
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

1333579467

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

1333579468

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

1333579468

The available command arguments include:

- -upload is the key argument to upload a generated report to a server.

The following code is the uploaded command syntax of the upload value.

```text

```

where square brackets [...] are delineated as optional.

- {scheme} The {scheme} defines the namespace, purpose, and the syntax of the remaining part of a URL. This field is mandatory. A scheme must be followed by “://”. The supported schemes are:
- ftp
- ftps
- sftp
- http
- https
- **{userInfo}** 
The {userInfo} defines the username and password for authentication. The syntax is displayed as follows. This optional password must be predicated by “:”, and it can be an empty string. If the password is omitted, the command line will prompt for the password. 
UserInfo must be followed by “@”, and this field is optional. Warning{username} and {password} should not contain special characters such as “:”, “/”, and “@” for they may cause an invalid result or error when the uploaded command is parsed.

- **{host}** 
The {host} defines a host name or an IP address that gives the destination location of a URL. This field is mandatory.
- **{port}** 
The {port} defines a port number where the server is listed. The port value has to be a value from 1 to 65535. It is necessary when the port number is different from the default port for well-known services. If the port is omitted, the default port will be used to connect to the server. The table below shows a port number for each scheme. The table below lists the Default Port Numbers for Schemes

| Scheme | Port Number |
| --- | --- |
| ftp | 21 |
| ftps | 990 |
| sftp | 22 |
| http | 80 |
| https | 443 |

The port must be preceded by ":". This field is optional.

- **{path}** 
The {path} defines a remote location where the report will be saved. Paths are Unix-style paths; therefore use “/” (forward-slash) as delimiters. This field is optional.
- **{query}** 
The {query} defines a query string that contains data to be passed to software running on the server. It may contain name or value pairs separated by ampersands.
- **{fragment}** 
The {fragment} defines a fragment identifier that, if present, specifies a part or a position within the overall resource or document.

#### INFO: Info

Info

For this feature, the parser ignores query and fragment. Both fields are included in {path}.

The following are some examples of valid server addresses.

- 
  - ftp://magicreport:1234@10.1.2.4:25/report SchemeftpUser namemagicreportPassword1234Host10.1.2.4Port25Pathreport
  - ftp://10.1.2.4:25/report SchemeftpHost10.1.2.4Port25Pathreport
  - ftp://10.1.2.4 SchemeftpHost10.1.2.4Port21 (Default port for ftp)
  - ftp://magicreport@10.1.2.4:25/report SchemeftpUser namemagicreportHost10.1.2.4Port25Pathreport TipThe command line prompts for password.
  - ftp://magicreport:@10.1.2.4:25/report SchemeftpUser namemagicreportPasswordHost10.1.2.4Port25Pathreport TipThe command line uses an empty password. The following are some examples of invalid server addresses. Invalid Server AddressCause10.1.2.4:25The scheme is required.Xx://10.1.2.4:25The scheme is invalid.ftp://magicreport@The host is required.ftp://@10.1.2.4:25The username is required.

1333579465

**Related pages**

- Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server
- Properties filename
- Syntax rules
- Using the Generate command to generate reports

password.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following are some examples of invalid server addresses.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Invalid Server Address</th><th>Cause</th></tr><tr><td>10.1.2.4:25</td><td>The scheme is required.</td></tr><tr><td>Xx://10.1.2.4:25</td><td>The scheme is invalid.</td></tr><tr><td>ftp://magicreport@</td><td>The host is required.</td></tr><tr><td>ftp://@10.1.2.4:25</td><td>The username is required.</td></tr></tbody></table></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="73e71955-0e7f-4f98-8a2a-5ffcc4ee8ff2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1333579465</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Generating+a+report+from+Teamwork+Cloud+Server">Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Properties+filename">Properties filename</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Syntax+rules">Syntax rules</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Using+the+Generate+command+to+generate+reports">Using the Generate command to generate reports</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971061 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06536: Uploading reports to remote locations

- page_id: `243971061`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971061/Uploading+reports+to+remote+locations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:03.138+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1336874444

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1336874446

1336950205

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1336874445

You can use the Report Wizard to upload a generated report to a predefined remote location server. Each remote location server has a profile that holds all necessary information, e.g., protocol, URL, username, and password. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile Management dialog' space=''] is used to create, edit, or delete profiles containing the configuration for remote location servers.

In this section, you will learn how to create a new remote location server step by step to upload a generated report to a newly created server, and how to upload a generated report to an existing server.

##### Uploading reports to a new server

To upload a generated report to a newly created server

1. Open the Report Wizard dialog. On the main menu, click Tools > Report Wizard . Proceed through each step by selecting a report template, report data, and Element scope. You will be at the Output options step. In the Report Wizard dialog, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Three-dot image.png'] in the Publish to server area.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

2. The **Profile Management** dialog opens. Click **New** to create a new server.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. The **New server profile** dialog opens. Enter the name of the server to create. You cannot leave the **Name** box empty or assign a name that has already been used in an existing set of servers. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
4. Choose a protocol from the **Protocol** drop-down list and fill out a URL address in the **Address** box. You can leave out the **User Name** and/or **Password** boxes if desired. Click **Save Profiles** to save the newly added server, and close the **Profile Management** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you leave out the **User Name** and/or **Password** boxes in the **Profile Management** dialog when creating a new server, you must enter the authentication information in the **Authentication** dialog after the report generation, then click **OK** to proceed.

5. You are now back to the **Report Wizard** dialog. Select the newly created server from the **Select server** drop-down list. Click **Generate** to generate the report to upload it to the newly created server. Once the report has been generated, it will be uploaded to the server previously created.

6. In case the server has not been trusted, the dialog will be opened asking to accept or reject the server certificate. Accept the server certificate for publishing the generated report to this server, or reject the server certificate to end the publishing report process.

7. If the report has been successfully uploaded, a message box informing that the resources have been uploaded successfully opens. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Uploading reports to an existing server

To upload a generated report to an existing server

1. Open the Report Wizard dialog. On the main menu, click Tools > Report Wizard . Proceed through each step by selecting a report template, report data, and Element scope. You will be at the Output options step. Select an existing server from the Select server drop-down list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

2. Click **Generate** to generate the report to upload it to the chosen existing server. Once the report has been generated, it will be uploaded to the server previously selected.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If the selected existing server does not have the username and/or password specified in the **Profile Management** dialog when the server was created, you must enter the authentication information in the **Authentication** dialog after the report generation, then click **OK** to proceed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. If the report has been successfully uploaded, a message box informing that the resources have been uploaded successfully opens. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This feature does not support SharePoint Server.

1336874443

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile Management dialog' space='']

**Description**

A Usage is a BPMNRelationship in which one element requires another element (or set of elements) for its full implementation or operation.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968833 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06538: Usages and Dependencies Search Options dialog

- page_id: `243968833`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968833/Usages+and+Dependencies+Search+Options+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:44.619+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Analyzing usages and dependencies
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Usage information

1389154636

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Usage information

1389154638

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Usage information

1389154637

INLINE

Use the **Usages/Dependencies Search Options** dialog to find model elements that either use the selected element, or are used by it. You can search using different sets of criteria and analyze the search results to understand the relationships between model elements.

To open the **Usages/Dependencies Search Options** dialog

- In the model browser or a diagram, right-click the element whose usages or dependencies you want to find out, and select Related Elements > Used by . The dialog allows specifying the search options to find the elements that use the selected elements in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- In the model browser or a diagram, right-click the element whose usages or dependencies you want to find out, and select Related Elements > Depends On . The dialog allows specifying the search options to find model elements that are used by the selected elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Usages/Dependencies Search Options dialog with the default search criteria selected.

The following table describes the search options of the **Usages/Dependencies Search Options**dialog.

| Dialog element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Load autoloadable used projects | Select this check box to include unloaded used projects in the usages search scope. Before starting the search, you can select the unloaded projects you want to load.This check box is available only if you select the Used By command. |
| Search recursively | Select this check box to search for usages/dependencies for the selected element and all the elements it owns. |
| Ignore derived properties | Select this check box to exclude the of the selected element from the usages/dependencies search results.It is recommended to select this check box, as including derived properties in the search scope may cause performance downgrade. |
| Ignore inter interrelations | Select this check box to exclude the usages/dependencies between the elements owned by the selected element from the usages/dependencies search results. This option allows you to analyze how the selected element and its owned elements are related to the outer elements (not owned by the selected element) of the model.Usage informationThis option is only effective if you select the Search recursively check box as well. |
| Search in diagrams with symbols only | Select this check box to check the element usage in diagrams containing symbols only. |
| Level of details | Select one of the predefined values (Classifiers or Packages), or click and select desired element types to define the level of details. If you select element types manually, the usages/dependencies search results will only display the elements of these types. |
| Treat relationships as search result | Select this check box to find a complete list of used/dependent relationships together with other results. |
| Treat model elements at the end of relationships as search result | Select this check box to exclude relationships from search results and only find the usages/dependencies connected with these relationships. |
| Show results in the new tab | Select this check box to display the results of each search in a new tab. |
| Show this dialog before searching | Select this check box to open the Usages/Dependencies Search Options dialog before a new search is started. If you leave the check box empty, the dialog will not open, and the next search will use the search options specified during your last search.To open the Usages/Dependencies Search Options dialog, click on the toolbar of the usages/dependencies search results window. |

1389154634

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usages and dependencies search results window' space='']

This option allows you to analyze how the selected element and its owned elements are related to the outer elements (<span>not owned by the selected element)</span> of the model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35f5f9c8-d1db-4328-9d2e-f110f528d63b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Usage information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This option is only effective if you select the <strong>Search recursively</strong> check box as well.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Search in diagrams with symbols only</strong></td><td colspan="1">Select this check box to check the element usage in diagrams containing symbols only.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Level of details</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Select one of the predefined values (<strong>Classifiers</strong> or <strong>Packages</strong>), or click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_medium.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select desired element types to define the level of details. If you select element types manually, the usages/dependencies search results will only display the elements of these types.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Treat relationships as search result</strong></p></td><td><p>Select this check box to find a complete list of used/dependent relationships together with other results.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Treat model elements at the end of relationships as search result</strong></p></td><td><p>Select this check box to exclude relationships from search results and only find the usages/dependencies connected with these relationships.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Show results in the new tab</strong></td><td><p>Select this check box to display the results of each search in a new tab.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Show this dialog before </strong><strong>searching</strong></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Select this check box to open the <strong>Usages/Dependencies Search Options</strong> dialog before a new search is started. If you leave the check box empty, the dialog will not open, and the next search will use the search options specified during your last search.</p><p><span>To open the </span><strong>Usages/Dependencies Search Options</strong><span> dialog, c</span>lick <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_dependencies_search_options.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the usages/dependencies search results window.</p></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f1e7632-c154-4db2-8a34-5feff71e891a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1389154634</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Analyzing usages and dependencies" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Usages and dependencies search results window" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968839 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06539: Usages and dependencies search results window

- page_id: `243968839`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968839/Usages+and+dependencies+search+results+window
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:45.280+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Analyzing usages and dependencies
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1387766720

INLINE

100.00002%

1387766722

INLINE

1387766721

INLINE

The usages/dependencies search results window lists the usages/dependencies of the element for which you perform the search. In the usages/dependencies window you can analyze, group and filter search results, find element locations, and more.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The dependencies search results window.

The following table describes how you can use the commands of the usages/dependencies search results dialog toolbar to manage usages/dependencies search results.

| Toolbar element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Expand All | Expands all search results listed in groups. To expand the content of a single group, click the plus sign next to the group name. |
| Collapse All | Collapses all search results listed in groups. To collapse the content of a single group, click the minus sign next to the group name. |
| Select in Containment Tree | Selects the desired element in the containment tree. To enable the button, select one of the search results in the usages/dependencies search result window. |
| Open Specification | Opens the Specification window of the desired element. To enable the button, select one of the search results in the usages/dependencies search result window. |
| Add to Search Results | Moves all usages/dependencies search results to the Search Results tab of the model browser. In the Search Results tab, all results are organized in two groups: Found in loaded diagrams and Found in model. |
| Open all diagrams that contain current Usages/Dependencies | Opens all diagrams that use at least one the usages/dependencies search results. The button is inactive when there are no search results used in diagrams. |
| Display the full path of the elements | Displays full paths to the elements next to their names in the usages/dependencies search results window. |
| Group by children | Groups all usages/dependencies search results by children. |
| Refresh | Refreshes usages/dependencies search results if elements or symbols are deleted from the model or new usages/dependencies are created. |
| Usages/Dependencies Search Options | Opens the Usages/Dependencies Search Options dialog. |
| Print | Prints the usages/dependencies search results table. Make sure that you expand the search results listed in groups if you want them to be shown on the printed page. |
| Export | Exports usages/dependencies search results to a .html or .csv file. |
| Filter | The filter boxes above each column of the usages/dependencies search results table allow you to filter search results by element type, used/dependent element name and usage type. Click a filter box to select the desired filter option. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The usage search results window

The following table describes usages search results window.

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Results | Displays results with found usage. |
| Type | Displays element type in which usage was found. |
| Used element | Displays used elements. |
| Used as | Displays usage type. |
| Project | Displays the name of the used project file that owns the model element, which the package/model element uses. |

1387766719

INLINE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966405 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06541: Use Case diagram

- page_id: `243966405`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966405/Use+Case+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:58.793+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

307575757

307575759

307575758

**Overview**

A Use Case diagram falls under the behavioral diagramming family.A Use Case diagram models the users' expectations for using the system. The people and systems that interact with the target system are called *actors*, whereas the features of the system used by actors are called *Use Cases*.

In general, a Use Case is a description of the functionality (a specific usage of a system) that a system provides. They can be written in many different ways, but the most common one is to represent a view of the system from outside the system. The Use Case descriptions can exist in a textual form (a simple table), where the Use Case diagram provides additional information about the relationship between the use cases and the external users. The Use Case diagram also allows a definition of the system's boundary.

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MED'] are described only in terms of how they appear when viewed externally by the user (a system's behavior as the user perceives it), and do not describe how the functionality is provided inside the system. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MED'] are not object-oriented, but they are included in the UML to simplify the approach of the project's lifecycle – from specification to implementation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Purpose**

A Use Case diagram defines the expectations of the external actors, for example, people, systems, or devices that need to interact with the system. Their interactions may be to provide input, to receive output, or to dialog with the system in order to cooperate in the completion of a task. All these interactions are focused using specific features of the system - *Use Cases*. It should be noted that Use Case diagrams do not provide any details about the implementation of these features. The goal of the Use Case approach is to focus the development effort on the essential objectives of the system without getting lost in, or driven by, particular implementations or practices.

**Usage**

Use Case diagrams, together with use case narratives and scenarios, define the goals of a system, or another classifier, such as an enterprise, subsystem, or component.

A Use Case diagram is ideal to:

- represent the goals of system-user interactions
- define and organize functional requirements in a system
- identify internal and external factors that may influence the system and should be taken into consideration
- specify the context and requirements of a system
- model the basic flow of events in a Use Case.

**Summary**

Use Case diagrams are valuable because they:

- identify the client's expectations for the system
- identify specific features of the system
- identify shared behavior among system features
- allows clients to view their requirements in a simple and easy way.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Use Case diagram

307575756

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']

The people and systems that interact with the target system are called <em>actors</em>, whereas the features of the system used by actors are called <em>Use Cases</em>.</p><p>In general, a Use Case is a description of the functionality (a specific usage of a system) that a system provides. They can be written in many different ways, but the most common one is to represent a view of the system from outside the system. The Use Case descriptions can exist in a textual form (a simple table), where the Use Case diagram provides additional information about the relationship between the use cases and the external users. The Use Case diagram also allows a definition of the system's boundary.</p><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Use Case" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are described only in terms of how they appear when viewed externally by the user (a system's behavior as the user perceives it), and do not describe how the functionality is provided inside the system. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Use Case" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are not object-oriented, but they are included in the UML to simplify the approach of the project's lifecycle – from specification to implementation.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The schematic view of the use cases in the system" ac:alt="The schematic view of the use cases in the system"><ri:attachment ri:filename="use_cases_system.gif" /></ac:image></p><p><strong>Purpose</strong></p><p>A Use Case diagram defines the expectations of the external actors, for example, people, systems, or devices that need to interact with the system. Their interactions may be to provide input, to receive output, or to dialog with the system in order to cooperate in the completion of a task. All these interactions are focused using specific features of the system - <em>Use Cases</em>. It should be noted that Use Case diagrams do not provide any details about the implementation of these features. The goal of the Use Case approach is to focus the development effort on the essential objectives of the system without getting lost in, or driven by, particular implementations or practices. </p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>Use Case diagrams, together with use case narratives and scenarios, define the goals of a system, or another classifier, such as an enterprise, subsystem, or component. </p><p>A Use Case diagram is ideal to:</p><ul><li>represent the goals of system-user interactions</li><li>define and organize functional requirements in a system</li><li>identify internal and external factors that may influence the system and should be taken into consideration</li><li>specify the context and requirements of a system</li><li>model the basic flow of events in a Use Case.</li></ul><p><strong>Summary</strong></p><p>Use Case diagrams are valuable because they: </p><ul><li>identify the client's expectations for the system</li><li>identify specific features of the system</li><li>identify shared behavior among system features</li><li>allows clients to view their requirements in a simple and easy way. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="use_case_diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Use Case diagram<br /><br /></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56022e7e-0dde-4bac-8862-50030959494e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">307575756</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965908 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06542: Use Case diagram elements

- page_id: `243965908`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965908/Use+Case+diagram+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:49.096+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import > Special Transformation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

490104335

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

490104338

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

490104336

This page describes all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space=''] elements.

##### Actor with Properties

An [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MED']with properties will be transformed into a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED']with the «EAActor»****stereotype.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An actor with properties.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

An Actor that has been converted to a Class with «EAActor» will not display some properties (such as Fill Color) because the stereotype image will be shown instead.

##### Use Case with Invalid Inner Elements

A NestedClassifier, ownedComment, ownedRule, ownedAttribute, or ownedOperation cannot be an inner element of a uml:UseCase. It will be moved to a new created realized [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Use Case with invalid inner elements.

##### Boundary

A boundary in EA will be converted into a rectangle with rounded corners. The boundary can contain inner elements. Unlike the rectangular boundary, the boundary in EA will take all inner elements with it whenever it is moved.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Boundaries.

490104334

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="147514d7-1e12-4680-8704-c12fb6885c1d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">490104335</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40ab1d08-53b8-4d46-a3c1-aa7fff9bbd4a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">490104338</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3157d0fd-6709-48a4-ad88-9a9366c676ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">490104336</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This page describes all <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link> elements.</p><h3>Actor with Properties</h3><p>An <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Actor ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>with properties will be transformed into a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>with the «EAActor»<strong> </strong>stereotype.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actor_withproperty.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">An actor with properties.</span></h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e58d8d1-6032-4ae9-ab51-92f87e6befc3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>An Actor that has been converted to a Class with «EAActor» will not display some properties (such as Fill Color) because the stereotype image will be shown instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Use Case with Invalid Inner Elements</h3><p>A NestedClassifier, ownedComment, ownedRule, ownedAttribute, or ownedOperation cannot be an inner element of a uml:UseCase. It will be moved to a new created realized <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UC_withinvalidelements.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A Use Case with invalid inner elements.</h6><h3>Boundary</h3><p>A boundary in EA will be converted into a rectangle with rounded corners. The boundary can contain inner elements. Unlike the rectangular boundary, the boundary in EA will take all inner elements with it whenever it is moved.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="boundaries.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">Boundaries.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f463f40e-a056-4449-b286-a2086c2b258c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">490104334</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971182 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06543: Use Case driven

- page_id: `243971182`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971182/Use+Case+driven
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:07.493+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1337564773

1337564774

1337564774

Report Wizard provides a set of templates to support the Use Case Driven approach methodology. A project that creates the Use Case Driven approach development steps can generate a report to capture elements.

Report Wizard provides 3 templates for the Use Case Driven approach:

1. Use Case Specification Report
2. Method Specification Report
3. Use Case Project Estimation Report

You can create a new project using the Use Case Driven approach either as:

(i) A blank project with the *UseCase_Profile.xml* module or 
(ii) A Use Case project

To create a new project as a blank project using *UseCase_Profile.xml*

1. On the main menu, click File .
2. Click Use Module . The Use Module dialog will open.
3. Select the UseCase_Profile.xml module.
4. Click Finish . The project will apply the Use Case Description profile.

To create a new project as a Use Case Project

- A project will apply the Use Case Description profile automatically.

1337564773

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971185 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06544: Use Case project estimation report

- page_id: `243971185`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971185/Use+Case+project+estimation+report
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T12:00:55.227+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Use Case driven
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The use case project estimation or 'Use Case Points' is a method for sizing and estimating projects developed with the object-oriented method, developed by Gustav Karner of Objectory (now Rational Software). The method is an extension of Function Point Analysis and Mk II Function Point Analysis (an adaption of FPA mainly used in the UK). 
The Use Case Project Estimation report is intended to estimate the project size and duration of hourly manpower required based on the use case model. The report supports use case and SysML v1 use case diagrams.

The use case point technique is applied to reestimate the project size. All the use cases used in the example below are based on the use case diagram. The following weight criteria are used:

#### Classifying Actors

The table below lists Actors Classifications

| Actor Complexity | Litmus Test to recognize classifications | Weight |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Low Complexity | An external system with a well-defined API. | 1 |
| Average complexity | Either a human with a command line interface or an external system via some protocols or a database. | 2 |
| High complexity | A human with a GUI or a web page. | 3 |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specification of Actor.

#### Unadjusted Actor Weights

Unadjusted Actor Weights (UAW) is obtained by counting how many actors there are in each category, multiplying each total by its weight, and adding up the products.

#### Determining Scenarios and Transactions of Use Cases

The table below lists Use Case Complexity

| Use Case Complexity | Litmus Test To Decide Classifications | Weight |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Low complexity | 1 - 3 transactions | 5 |
| Average complexity | 4 - 7 transactions | 10 |
| High complexity | > 7 transactions | 15 |

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Use Case Driven - Use Case Complexity.

#### Unadjusted Use Case Weights

A transaction is a set of activities, which is either performed entirely or not at all. To determine the number of transactions, you need to count the use case steps. Next, multiply each use case type by the weighting factor and add up the products to get **Unadjusted Use Case Weights (UUCW)**.

#### Unadjusted Use Case Point

Add UAW and UUCW to get Unadjusted Use Case Point (UUCP): UUCP = UAW + UUCW.

Reference Documents

- The Estimation of Effort Based on Use Cases, published by Rational software [ftp://ftp.software.ibm.com/software/rational/web/whitepapers/2003/finalTP171.pdf](ftp://ftp.software.ibm.com/software/rational/web/whitepapers/2003/finalTP171.pdf)
- COCOMO II [http://sunset.usc.edu/csse/research/COCOMOII/cocomo_main.html](http://sunset.usc.edu/csse/research/COCOMOII/cocomo_main.html)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The method is an extension of Function Point Analysis and Mk II Function Point Analysis (an adaption of FPA mainly used in the UK).<br />The Use Case Project Estimation report is intended to estimate the project size and duration of hourly manpower required based on the use case model. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971183 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06546: Use Case specification report

- page_id: `243971183`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971183/Use+Case+specification+report
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T12:01:06.266+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Use Case driven
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Use Case Specification report shows the details of actors and use cases in the project. The first part, which is the actor summary, shows the list of all actors and their associated use cases. The second part presents the use case specification in general information of relations and scenarios. The report supports use case and SysML v1 use case diagrams.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The second part presents the use case specification in general information of relations and scenarios. The report supports use case and SysML v1 use case diagrams.</p>
````
