Use only with -properties.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-generatepassword password</pre></td><td>Generates an encrypted password to be used with a properties file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7554e345-c388-435f-8ab7-f2dbdab52cfd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Options</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Options</strong></h4><p>The command line feature supports a set of options useful in adding additional configuration possibilities.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 27.1396%;" /><col class="" style="width: 72.8749%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Command</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-report reportDataName|reportDataElementID</pre></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specifies the name of a <ac:link ac:anchor="WorkingwithReportdata"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Data]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> file or Report Data element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c51a68e-6150-4ec6-8cc3-3b74eab3eb12"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><p>The Report Data file must exist in the modeling tool prior to using this command, otherwise an error will occur.</p></li><li><p>The value for this command can be either a Report Data name or Report Data element ID.</p></li><li><p>If the <strong>-report</strong> parameter is not specified, the previous Report Data will be set and used instead.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-autoImage 0|1|2|3</pre></td><td><p>Specifies how an image will be shown in a report document. The default value for this argument is <strong>1</strong>.</p><ul><li>0 – No resize.</li><li>1 – Fit image to paper (large only).</li><li>2 – Fit and rotate image (clockwise) to paper (large only).</li><li>3 – Fit and rotate image (counter-clockwise) to paper (large only)</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-imageFormat jpg|png|svg|emf|wmf</pre></td><td><p>Specifies an image format in a report document. The default value for this argument is <strong>jpg</strong>.</p><ul><li><em>jpg</em> – Joint Photographic Expert Group</li><li><em>png</em> – Portable Network Graphics</li><li><em>svg</em> – Scaling Vector Image</li><li><em>emf</em> – Microsoft Windows Enhanced Metafile</li><li><em>wmf</em> – Windows Metafile</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-recursive true|false</pre></td><td><p>Specifies how to select a package in a project. The default value for this argument is <strong>true</strong>.</p><ul><li><strong>true</strong> – Select the specified package and its recursive package.</li><li><strong>false</strong> – Select only the specified package.</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-includeAuxiliary true|false</pre></td><td>Selects packages, including auxiliary packages. The default value is <strong>false</strong>.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-outputOnBlankField stringValue</pre></td><td>Shows a string value in a blank field in a report document. The default value for this argument is “”.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-category categoryName</pre></td><td>Specifies a template category.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-fields[name=value]</pre></td><td>Specifies a variable.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-spassword encryptedPassword</pre></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specifies an encrypted password to log on to the server. This option is available only in a properties file.</p><p>You can get encrypted password by entering this command below in command line prompt.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d67a932d-8f64-4d04-bcb5-1d2d547b650e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -generatepassword "enter_your_password_here"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-upload serverAddress</pre></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specifies a serverAddress consisting of a scheme, userInfo, host, port, and path to connect and upload a generated report to a server (see <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Uploading generated reports to servers" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Upload generated report to server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em>).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90772485-a785-41b4-9f51-8e75e68e2281"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In case the server has not been trusted, a message will be displayed in the command line, asking you to choose to accept or reject the server certificate. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-trustAll true|false</pre></td><td><p>Trusts any certificate of the specific publishing server in -upload command. The default value is <strong>false</strong>.</p><p>If the value is <strong>true</strong>, the certificate will be trusted without <span>asking </span>any questions.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-overwrite true|false</pre></td><td><p>Overwrites any previously generated files. The default value for this argument is <strong>true</strong>.</p><ul><li><strong>true</strong> – Overwrites all files automatically (removes any existing file before generating a report.)</li><li><strong>false</strong> – If the file exists, the following prompt message will open:</li></ul><table class="wrapped" data-mce-resize="false"><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td><pre>Output file already exists.</pre><pre>Would you like to replace the existing file? [y][n]:</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p>If you type ‘y’ or ‘yes’, the Report Wizard will generate a new report that replaces the previous one. If you type ‘n’ or ‘no’, the Report Wizard will not generate a report and terminate the process.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-pversion projectVersion</pre></td><td>Specifies a server project version. </td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-ssl true|false </pre></td><td><p>Specifies if a Secured Connection (SSL) is used. The default value is <strong>false</strong>.</p><ul><li><strong>true</strong> - Use Secured Connection (SSL)</li><li><strong>false</strong> - Do not use Secured Connection (SSL)</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-tag tagValue</pre></td><td><span>An additional argument to generate reports from a server project by specifying a server project tag name. </span><span>The tagValue is non-case sensitive. More than one project version may be returned from specifying one tag value. </span><span>Hence, one report generation process may generate multiple reports. You cannot use this command with –pversion.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-incremental true|false</pre></td><td><p>Specifies the incremental method. The default value is <strong>true</strong>.</p><ul class="alternate"><li><p><strong>true</strong> - The output report shall be generated by appending a new generated output file name with the incremental number.</p></li><li><p><strong>false</strong> - The latest project version in the returned project collection. Use this command with -tag only.</p></li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-leaveprojectopen</pre></td><td>Leaves the project open per property file after generating the report, in a series of report generation. The default value is <strong>false</strong>.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-loadmodule true|false</pre></td><td><p>Loads all unloaded modules in a project. The default value is <strong>false</strong>.</p><ul><li><strong>true</strong> - All modules including unloaded modules are loaded while opening a project.</li><li><strong>false</strong> - All modules except unloaded modules are loaded while opening a project.</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-projectpassword projectPassword</pre></td><td><span>Specifies a password for the protected Teamwork Cloud project. The </span><span>password is required to </span><span>open a protected Teamwork Cloud project for generating a report.</span></td></tr><tr><td>-<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">serverType PLATFORM_ON_PREMISE| PLATFORM_CLOUD</span></td><td><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">While launching a project stored in the 3DEXPERIENCE platform, specifies the platform deployment type:</span></p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">PLATFORM_ON_PREMISE= </span></span>OnPremise deployment serverType</span></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">PLATFORM_CLOUD= </span></span>Cloud deployment serverType</span></span></li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb5d27c3-e73f-4227-b0d3-db5f070fea18"><ac:parameter ac:name="">UsingCommand</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Using command line</strong></h4><p>You can use the command line on Windows, Linux, or Unix.</p><p><br /></p><p>To use the command line on Windows</p><hr /><ol><li>Run a command line console. In the Console window, go to the local installation of the modeling tool's application.</li><li>Go to the plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard folder, and type the command line there.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To use the command line on Linux or Unix</p><hr /><ol><li>Run a terminal (command line console). In the Console window, go to the local installation of the modeling tool's application.</li><li>Go to the plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard folder, and type the command line there.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>See the following examples of command line on Windows and Linux or Unix respectively.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4d05e7b-d85d-4d58-af06-e3a97955dc57"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On Windows</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -properties "C:\\output\\prop.properties"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9372603-379a-4b2c-80eb-6f11de66f4d8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On Windows</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "C:\\MagicDraw\\samples\\diagrams\\class diagram.mdzip" -output "C:\\output\\output.rtf" -template "Class Specification Report" -package "Model" -report "Built-in" -autoImage 1 -imageFormat png -recursive false -outputOnBlankField "#NA" -fields "[Author=NMA][Company=No Magic]"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2715ac73-abb4-455f-af9b-c8bd01b544be"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On Linux or Unix</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[./generate.sh -project "/home/project/project.mdzip" -output "/home/output/output.rtf" -template "Class Specification Report" -package "Model" -report "Built-in" -autoImage 1 -imageFormat png -recursive false -outputOnBlankField "#NA" -fields "[Author=NMA][Company=No Magic]"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7388279-fb41-4326-a5d2-5b618215afa1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">MultipleReports</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Generating multiple reports </h4><p>You can open multiple projects and generate multiple reports before the modeling tool shuts down. The <strong>generate</strong> command with the -properties option accepts multiple properties files as shown below.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup class=""><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td>generate -properties &lt;file1.properties&gt; &lt;file2.properties&gt; &lt;file3.properties&gt;...</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup class=""><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td>generate -properties “property1.properties” “property2.properties”</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f51f4e8-3acb-491d-b2d8-9f3171611a39"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Template</strong>, <strong>output</strong>, <strong>package</strong>, and <strong>project</strong> are the required data for each property file.</p><p>The following parameters are reset when finishing generating a report for each property file. Other parameter values affect subsequent property files, if there are no overridden values.</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">-project</p></li><li>-output</li><li>-template</li><li>-templatePath</li><li>-tag</li><li>-pVersion</li><li>-recursive</li><li>-category</li><li>-includeAuxiliary</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="417ea8b0-2a85-4a3b-87a0-f818565da74c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When large-size projects need to be loaded and closed for many property files, it takes longer time to generate reports. In this case, you can use<strong> -leaveprojectopen</strong> as an option to reduce memory for loading each project when starting to generate a new report.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a6aa847-76e1-46dd-bcc5-cd6809a96045"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -leaveprojectopen true -properties “property1.properties” “property2.properties”]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1da55347-e339-4281-b8fb-fb0ac99bba30"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><em>-leaveprojectopen</em> needs to be specified before<em> -properties.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Generating+a+report+from+Teamwork+Cloud+Server">Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Properties+filename">Properties filename</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Uploading+generated+reports+to+servers">Uploading generated reports to servers</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Syntax+rules">Syntax rules</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967326 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06590: Using the grid

- page_id: `243967326`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967326/Using+the+grid
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:28.378+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

903695269

903695272

903695271

The grid helps to arrange diagram symbols on the diagram pane. By default, the grid is not visible.

To show the grid on the diagram, do one of the following

- From the main menu, select View > Grid > Show Grid .
- From the diagram shortcut menu, select Show Grid .
- In the Diagram Properties dialog, set the Show Grid property to true .

To snap the paths to gridlines

- From the main menu, select View > Grid > Snap Paths to Grid .

To snap the shapes to gridlines

- From the main menu, select View > Grid > Snap Shapes to Grid .

To change the grid size

1. From the main menu, select View > Grid > Grid Size . The Grid Size dialog opens.
2. Enter a grid size between 2 and 30 (default is 10).
3. Click OK .

To change the grid style

1. From the Options menu, select Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. In the Diagram group, from the Grid Style option drop-down list, select one of the following styles:
  - Dense
  - Sparse (default)

903695268

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='']

By default, the grid is not visible.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show the grid on the diagram, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Grid</strong> &gt; <strong>Show Grid</strong>.</li><li>From the diagram shortcut menu, select <strong>Show Grid</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Show Grid</strong> property to <em>true</em>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To snap the paths to gridlines</p><hr /><ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Grid</strong> &gt; <strong>Snap Paths to Grid</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To snap the shapes to gridlines</p><hr /><ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Grid</strong> &gt; <strong>Snap Shapes to Grid</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the grid size</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Grid</strong> &gt; <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Grid Size</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. The </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Grid Size</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> dialog opens. </span></li><li>Enter a grid size between 2 and 30 (default is 10).</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To change the grid style</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Environment</strong>. The <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Diagram </strong>group, from the <strong>Grid Style</strong> option drop-down list, select one of the following styles:<ul><li>Dense </li><li>Sparse (default)</li></ul></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8085ad5-38e6-4b66-a513-d5c6d6b37b4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">903695268</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965720 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06591: Using the Model Browser

- page_id: `243965720`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965720/Using+the+Model+Browser
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:21:49.403+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Model Browser
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425704795

1425704797

1425704796

INLINE

Model Browser is displayed when at least one project is open. By default, the Model Browser is placed on the left side of the main window. It is possible to move the Model Browser to any place on the window. Also, all tabs can be viewed separately and you can set up the Model Browser according to your needs by hiding the desired tabs. 
 
To change the size of either part of the Model Browser

- Drag the bar that separates the two parts.

To zoom the Model Browser

1. Select a tab in the Model Browser.
2. Use one of the following shortcuts:

- 
  - For Mac

1. 
  1. 
    1. Cmd+Mouse scroll wheel
    2. Cmd+Numpad +/-

- 
  - For Windows

1. 
  1. 
    1. Ctrl+Mouse scroll wheel
    2. Ctrl+Numpad +/-

To change the Model Browser position

- Using the **Options** menu

1. 
  1. From the **Options** menu, select **Environment**. The **Environment Options** dialog opens.
  2. Click on the **Browser** option group.
  3. In the Browser environment options pane, change the **Browser Position** property to **Right** or **Left**.

- Using Toggle 
In the upper-right corner of the selected tab in the Model Browser, click the Toggle auto-hide [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or Toggle floating [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. This action changes the layout of the interface.

To close or reopen the desired tab of the Model Browser

- Using the **Window** menu, choose the Model Browser tab you want to close or open.

To reset all Model Browser tabs to the default position

- From the **Window** menu, choose the **Reset Windows Configuration** command.

To sort items in the Model Browser alphabetically

1. From the Options menu, select Environment. The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. Click the Browser option group.
3. In the Browser options pane, set the Sort Alphabetically value to true (the default value is true).

1425704794

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="720ef575-46c5-49bd-99ca-93fab10eed1c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1425704795</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b593c93-35a2-4012-9445-e8237c68fef4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1425704797</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1943562-78d2-4b16-95aa-f31465023cf9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1425704796</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32c73566-de55-467d-a780-9ab59b25df05"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Model Browser is displayed when at least one project is open. Also, all tabs can be viewed separately and you can set up the Model Browser according to your needs by hiding the desired tabs. </span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> To change the size of either part of the Model Browser</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag the bar that separates the two parts.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To zoom the Model Browser</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a tab in the Model Browser.</li><li>Use one of the following shortcuts:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li>For Mac</li></ul></li></ul><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ol><li>Cmd+Mouse scroll wheel</li><li>Cmd+Numpad +/-</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li>For Windows</li></ul></li></ul><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ol><li style="text-align: left;">Ctrl+Mouse scroll wheel</li><li style="text-align: left;">Ctrl+Numpad +/-</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To change the Model Browser position</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Using the <strong>Options</strong> menu</span></li></ul><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Environment</strong>. The <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog opens.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click on the <strong>Browser</strong> option group.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the Browser environment options pane, change the <strong>Browser Position</strong> property to <strong>Right</strong> or <strong>Left</strong>.</span></li></ol></li></ol><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Using Toggle</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> In the upper-right corner of the selected tab in the Model Browser, click the Toggle auto-hide <ac:image ac:height="15" ac:width="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="toggle_autohide_button.png" /></ac:image> or Toggle floating <ac:image ac:height="12" ac:width="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="toggle_floating_button.png" /></ac:image> button.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="563c9201-b432-4f1e-9384-8874b3fbeb60"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">This action changes the layout of the interface. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To close or reopen the desired tab of the Model Browser</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Using the <strong>Window</strong> menu, choose the Model Browser tab you want to close or open. </span></li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> To reset all Model Browser tabs to the default position</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">From the <strong>Window</strong> menu, choose the <strong>Reset Windows Configuration</strong> command.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To sort items in the Model Browser alphabetically</span></p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options </strong>menu, select <strong>Environment. </strong>The <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Click the <strong>Browser </strong>option group.</li><li>In the <strong>Browser</strong> options pane, set the <strong>Sort</strong> <strong>Alphabetically </strong>value to <em>true </em>(the default value is <em>true).</em></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="590e840e-437b-4f42-a27a-718c3b385e2a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1425704794</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="ancestor-link parent-link"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Model+Browser">Model Browser</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565623 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06592: Using To Do property

- page_id: `249565623`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565623/Using+To+Do+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:39.110+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked "added to the To Do property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration is placed in the element's To Do property.

To find all elements which contain information in the To Do property

1. From the main toolbar click Edit > Find TODO . All elements which contain information in the To Do property are listed in the Search Results tab.

For example, if you had Desired Effect relation connected to State *Searching (Aircraft)* before the migration, the information about it is stored in the To Do property of Capability *Search* (source of the relationship).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked &quot;added to the To Do property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration is placed in the element's To Do property.</p><p>To find all elements which contain information in the To Do property</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main toolbar click <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Find TODO</strong>.<br />All elements which contain information in the To Do property are listed in the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4a2d810-8712-4983-8a2e-5d42a26d7ad6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, if you had Desired Effect relation connected to State <em>Searching (Aircraft)</em> before the migration, the information about it is stored in the To Do property of Capability <em>Search</em> (source of the relationship).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_of_capability_search.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254421845 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06593: Using UAF and UPDM2 plugins in Cameo Enterprise Architecture

- page_id: `254421845`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/254421845/Using+UAF+and+UPDM2+plugins+in+Cameo+Enterprise+Architecture
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:33:44.775+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Choosing the default plugin

Before the first startup of the Cameo Enterprise Architecture, you can set which plugin - UAF or UPDM2 - will be installed by default. If no changes are made, by default UAF plugin is installed.

To change the default plugin before the first startup

1. Go to the <cea_install_dir>\bin\ and open for editing the cameoea.properties file.
2. In the file, find the system property -Dstart.application.with.updm2.or.uaf.plugin=uaf
3. Change the uaf value to updm2 .
4. Save the file and start the Cameo Enterprise Architecture modeling tool.

In case you will decide to change the plugin later, see chapter [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing between UAF and UPDM 2 plugins' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] for information about how to do that.

##### Displaying which plugin is installed

You can choose to display the plugin name in brackets next to the Cameo Enterprise Architecture title in the modeling tool's title bar.

To display the installed plugin name near the modeling tool title

1. Go to the <cea_install_dir>\bin\ and open for editing the cameoea.properties file.
2. In the file, find the system property -Dadd.plugin.name.to.application.title\=false
3. Change the property value to true .
4. Save the file and start the Cameo Enterprise Architecture modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Choosing the default plugin</h3><p>Before the first startup of the Cameo Enterprise Architecture, you can set which plugin - UAF or UPDM2 - will be installed by default. If no changes are made, by default UAF plugin is installed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the default plugin before the first startup</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the &lt;cea_install_dir&gt;\bin\ and open for editing the <em>cameoea.properties</em> file.</li><li>In the file, find the system property <em>-Dstart.application.with.updm2.or.uaf.plugin=uaf</em></li><li>Change the <em>uaf</em> value to <em>updm2</em>.</li><li>Save the file and start the Cameo Enterprise Architecture modeling tool.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c93f7330-b046-4e85-be90-7075c3f9d206"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In case you will decide to change the plugin later, see chapter <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" ri:content-title="Changing between UAF and UPDM 2 plugins" /></ac:link> for information about how to do that.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Displaying which plugin is installed</h3><p>You can choose to display the plugin name in brackets next to the Cameo Enterprise Architecture title in the modeling tool's title bar. </p><p><br /></p><p>To display the installed plugin name near the modeling tool title</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the &lt;cea_install_dir&gt;\bin\ and open for editing the <em>cameoea.properties</em> file.</li><li>In the file, find the system property <em>-Dadd.plugin.name.to.application.title\=false</em></li><li>Change the <em>property </em>value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Save the file and start the Cameo Enterprise Architecture modeling tool.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="plugin_name_in_title.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561362 space=MT version=8 -->
## PAGE 06594: Using Units

- page_id: `249561362`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561362/Using+Units
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2025-09-08T18:52:28.756+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Unit represents a standard unit of measure. For example,metre, kilometre, or foot are units of length.Units are used to specify [Value Properties](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919459/Value+Property).This section explains how to specify units for a Value Property, how to display or hide units, and unit symbols in various places.

##### Specifying Unit for a Value Property

When a Block has a Value Property defined, it requires a numeric value and must be typed by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Type' space='MED'], either with or without (unitless) a specified Unit.

To specify the Unit for a Value Property

1. Select the Value Property in the Block compartments.
2. If the ISO-80000 library is not uploaded in your project, click the**ISO** button on the smart manipulator toolbar. If you work with projects older than version 18.0, which use the QUDV library and SysML 1.3 library, the ISO-80000 library will not be compatible.If you work with Teamwork projects, the ISO-80000 library is uploaded by default. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Open the **Select Type** menu inone of the following way:
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - Press Ctrl+T.
  - From the shortcut menu, select **Type**.
4. Select the Value Type by typing the quantity name and unit name in the following syntax: quantity name[unit name]. Unit libraries- If you cannot find a suitable Unit in the standard libraries, you can create custom Units. To learn more about creating units, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Units' space=''] page. 
 - You can manage the scope of provided units with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package import' space='MED'] feature. The unit is specified for a Value Property. As an example, see the figure below. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *stoppingDistance* Value Property typed by *distance[metre]* Value Type.

##### Displaying/hiding Units

After the unit is specified you can:

- Display/hide units for Value Property.
- 
- Display/hide unit symbolsfor values.

###### value_propertyDisplaying/hiding units for Value Property

The full name of the unit (e.g. metre, kilogram) is shown by default next to the Value Property on the Block [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''].

To hide/display the full name of a unit for a Value Property

1. Select the Block shape.
2. From its shortcut menu, select **Symbol Properties**.
3. Select the **All** options display mode.
4. Find the**Show Attributes Tagged Values** option.
5. Set its value to *true* to show the unit (default), or *false*to hide it. The full name of the unit is shown/hidden next to the values in brackets in the compartments.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### symbolDisplaying/hiding unit symbols for Value Property

You can show the unit symbol in brackets (e.g. (m), (kg)) next to the Value Property on the Block [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='']. If the Value Property has a value defined, the unit symbol in brackets is hidden (see the figure below). >]]>

To display/hide a unit symbol next to a Value Property

1. Select the Block shape.
2. From its shortcut menu, select **Symbol Properties**.
3. Find the**Show Unit on Value Property** option.
4. Set its value to *true* to show the unit symbol, or *false*(default) to hide it. The unit symbol is shown/hidden next to the Value Property. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### valuesDisplaying/hiding units symbols for values

You can hide or display a unit's symbol (e.g. m, kg):

- 
- 
- 

**on blockDisplaying/hiding unit symbols next to values**

You can display or hide the unit symbols (e.g. m, kg) of values in the entire project when the Unit is already defined for the Value Property and it has a value specified. For projects older than version 18.5, you must enable the **Show Units** option in the **Project Options** dialog if you want to see unit symbols. As of version 18.5, this option is enabled by default for any new projects.

show_unitsTo display/hide unit symbols next to values

1. Select **Options** > **Project**.
2. In the **Project Options** dialog, expand the **General** option group and select **SysML**.
3. Set the **Show Units** option value to *true* to show unit symbols, or *false*to hide. 
The unit symbols are shown next to the values in the compartments. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

**slotDisplaying/hiding unit symbols next to slots**

You can display a unit symbol for a slot on the Instance Specification shape by specifying it in the following ways:

- Using the **Specify Type** button from the smart manipulator toolbar.
- .
- .

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

The following procedures work only if the **Show Units** option is enabled. How to enable the **Show Units** option >

typeTo specify or change the unit symbol of a slot using the **Specify Type** button

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select the value in the compartment, and click the **Specify****Type** button. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Right-click the value in the compartment, and select **Type**.
  - Select the value in the compartment, and press Ctrl+T.
2. Specify the new unit, or select an existing unit from the **Select Type** menu list. InformationThe list of units in the **Select Type** menu consists of those units that are defined in the Block property type, or in its subtypes. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

manuallyTo specify or change unit by typing it manually

1. Select the slot on the Instance Specification shape.
2. Click it to edit.
3. Type the required unit symbol next to the slot value.
4. Press Enter.

right-clickTo specify or change the unit using the right-click menu

1. Select the slot in the Instance Specification compartment, the row in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space=''], or the Slot in the Containment tree.
2. Right-click it, select the **Unit** button, and choose the required unit from the list. InformationThe list of units under the **Unit** button consists of those units that are defined either in the Block property type or in its subtypes.

**cells_columnsDisplaying unit symbols in the Instance table**

You can display units in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space=''] in the following ways:

- 
- 

unit_in_cellTo show unit symbols in the cells of an Instance Table

1. Click the **Options** button.
2. Select the **Show Units on Values** option. 
The unit symbols are displayed next to the values in the cells.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

Show Units on Values

Show Unit

Project Options

Show Units

unit_in_columnTo show the unit symbol in the column header of the Instance Table

1. Click the **Options** button.
2. Select the **Show Units on Columns**option. 
The unit symbols are displayed on the column header.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">symbols next to values</span><br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, expand the <strong>General</strong> option group and select <strong>SysML</strong>.</span></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Set the <strong>Show Units</strong> option value to <em>true</em> to show unit symbols, or <em>false </em>to hide.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The unit symbols are shown next to the values in the compartments.</span><br /></span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_units_option.png" /></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45461112-3769-496f-8c1d-f667cb9d53c1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">slot</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Displaying/hiding unit symbols next to slots<br /></strong></p><p>You can display a unit symbol for a slot on the Instance Specification shape by specifying it in the following ways:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link" style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="type"><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Using the <strong>Specify Type</strong> button from the smart manipulator toolbar</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="manually"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Typing the appropriate unit symbol directly next to slot value]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="right-click"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[From the right-click menu]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f06fca2-e4aa-4fa6-a65e-80664466287c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The following procedures work only if the <strong>Show Units</strong> option is enabled. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="show_units"><ac:link-body>How to enable the <strong>Show Units</strong> option &gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45782e97-40db-400c-b3fa-48bb220c1232"><ac:parameter ac:name="">type</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To specify or change the unit symbol of a slot using the <strong>Specify Type</strong> button</span><br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:<br /></span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the value in the compartment, and click the <strong>Specify</strong><strong> Type</strong> button.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <ac:image ac:title="The Specify Type button from smart manipulator toolbar." ac:alt="The Specify Type button from smart manipulator toolbar."><ri:attachment ri:filename="specify_value_type.png" /></ac:image></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Right-click the value in the compartment, and select <strong>Type</strong>. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the value in the compartment, and press Ctrl+T.</span></li></ul></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the new unit, or select an existing unit from the <strong>Select Type</strong> menu list.</span><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af7b6b38-79ce-4ab2-a6b5-eca3eda9db0f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The list of units in the <strong>Select Type</strong> menu consists of those units that are defined in the Block property type, or in its subtypes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:image ac:title="The Select Type dialog opens automatically when specifying unit." ac:alt="The Select Type dialog opens automatically when specifying unit."><ri:attachment ri:filename="specify_unit.png" /></ac:image></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9aecab09-acdd-471e-9f17-20276ef29709"><ac:parameter ac:name="">manually</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To specify or change unit by typing it manually</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the slot on the Instance Specification shape.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click it to edit.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Type the required unit symbol next to the slot value.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Press Enter.<br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c8af13e-24fe-4c80-9cfe-1b62acc63d01"><ac:parameter ac:name="">right-click</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To specify or change the unit using the right-click menu</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the slot in the Instance Specification compartment, the row in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Instance table" /></ac:link>, or the Slot in the Containment tree.</span></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Right-click it, select the <strong>Unit</strong> button, and choose the required unit from the list.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe649446-f729-4ffb-8837-64caaf21b8c9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The list of units under the <strong>Unit</strong> button consists of those units that are defined either in the Block property type or in its subtypes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e6fd710-1cd2-4e29-9a42-aff72f795328"><ac:parameter ac:name="">cells_columns</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Displaying unit symbols in the Instance table</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can display units in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Instance table" /></ac:link> in the following ways:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="unit_in_cell"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Next to the values in cells.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="unit_in_column"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[On column header of the table.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90e96b33-f49e-4b25-a9ea-528bed5ff7d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">unit_in_cell</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To show unit symbols in the cells of an Instance Table</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Options</strong> button.</span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the <strong>Show Units on Values</strong> option.<br />The unit symbols are displayed next to the values in the cells.<br /></span></p></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_units_on_values.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5355ab6d-2428-4f15-97b1-2e904096dadf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The<strong> Show Units on Values</strong> option does not allow you to specify units directly in the row of the Instance Table. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you want to edit them, enable the <strong>Show Unit</strong> option in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. <ac:link ac:anchor="show_units"><ac:link-body>How to enable <strong>Show Units</strong> option &gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link> <br /></span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a0eca118-d180-4d72-95db-f6338b07ad98"><ac:parameter ac:name="">unit_in_column</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To show the unit symbol in the column header of the Instance Table</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Options</strong> button.</span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the <strong>Show Units on Columns </strong>option.<br />The unit symbols are displayed on the column header.</span></p></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_units_on_columns.png" /></ac:image></span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966182 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06595: Validating project integrity

- page_id: `243966182`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966182/Validating+project+integrity
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:57:35.305+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Project partitioning
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

762190336

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

762190338

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

762190337

Some aspects are validated automatically, such as:

- Mount location inconsistencies in used projects
- Required version inconsistencies in used projects

##### Mount location inconsistencies in used projects

This occurs when packages of the used project have different mount locations defined in separate projects.

#### INFO: Example

Example

The used project *Tools* has mounted a package of the used project *ComponentLibrary*, while the used project *MainBlocks* has also mounted the same package *ComponentsLibrary*. As shown in the following figure, the mount location conflict occurs because the project*RadioReceiverProject*is using both the *Tools*and *MainBlocks*used projects at the same time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can solve the conflict in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='CM']**U****sed Project**dialogby unifying the**Mounted On**value in both projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of conflicting mount locations warning displayed in Used Project dialog.

##### Required version inconsistencies in used projects

This occurs when a project directly and indirectly uses several different versions of the same used project at the same time.

#### INFO: Example

Example

The used project *Tools* is using the 4th version of the used project *ComponentLibrary* and the used project *MainBlocks* is using the 2nd version of the used project *ComponentLibrary*. In the project *RadioReceiverProject*, a used project *ComponentLibrary* version conflict occurs as two different versions of this project are required at the same time (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can solve the conflict in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='']**U****sed Project**dialogby unifying the required versions in both projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of conflicting versions warning displayed in Used Project dialog

##### Cyclic dependencies among used projects

The figure below illustrates an example of cyclic dependency:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of cyclic dependency.

The Project Integrity Correctness suite includes the rules for **detecting** Cyclic dependencies among used projects.While [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='']the model, use the **Project Integrity Correctness** validation suite to find cyclic dependency issues in your project composition.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Project Integrity Correctness suite

Violations of your project integrity will be displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='']**Validation Results** panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of violations detected while checking project integrity correctness

You can resolve the cyclic dependency issues in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='']**Used Project** dialog or you can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map' space='MCS'].

762197964

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969457 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06596: Validation

- page_id: `243969457`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969457/Validation
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-01-26T13:05:41.118+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis
- labels: ['validation', 'active-validation', 'validation-rules', 'validation-suites', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Purpose

Validation checks the accuracy, completeness, and correctness of a model, marks invalid elements in the model, and suggests solutions.

##### Validation suites

Models are validated against a set of validation rules. Each validation rule captures an imperative condition that must be checked against the model. Validation rules are grouped into meaningful groups called validation suites. There are predefined validation suites that you can customize. You can also create your own rules and group them into suites. Some validation rules are active; that is, they run all the time. Validation rules and suites can be exported into a separate project in order to reuse them. They are stored in the Teamwork Cloud server for exchange.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Predefined validation suites' space='']>]]>
- Learn more about SysML validation suites >>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation suites' space='']>]]>
- Learn more about SysML active validation suites >>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirements validation suites' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation suites' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new validation rules' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global validation rules' space='']>]]>

##### Validating your model

The validation can run on the entire model or on a selected part of it. All the elements in that scope are checked against relevant validation rules, and elements that violate these rules are reported in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='']**Validation Results** panel. They are also marked in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''], and their symbols are highlighted. Diagrams, tables, matrices, and relation maps displaying these highlighted symbols have the validation results marker bar.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting the validation' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation results marker bar' space='']Learn more about validation results marker bar >>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='']Learn more about Validation Results panel >>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation dialog' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pre-commit validation' space='']>]]>

##### Invalid elements representation

The invalid elements are marked as follows:

- [ATTACHMENT filename='error_icon.png'] - error or fatal error.
- [ATTACHMENT filename='warning_icon.png'] - warning.
- [ATTACHMENT filename='info_icon.png'] - debug or info.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing severity levels' space='']>]]>

You can see invalid elements in the following areas:

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] an invalid element is marked with a small symbol that depends on the failure severity. [ATTACHMENT filename='invalid_element_in_model_brower.png']
- On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] an invalid symbol is highlighted. [ATTACHMENT filename='invalid_symbol_on_diagram_pane.png']
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation results marker bar' space=''] every marker of an invalid symbol is colored according to the violation severity. [ATTACHMENT filename='invalid_symbol_in_validation_results_marker_bar.png']
- On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] the failure indicator displays the severity icon, number of errors, and the first letter of the error severity (F - fatal error; E - error; W - warning; D - debug; I - info). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Solvers of invalid elements

Solvers of invalid elements

You can analyze invalid elements and solve problems by selecting appropriate commands from the invalid element or symbol shortcut menu or in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='']**Validation Results** panel.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Purpose</h3><p>Validation checks the accuracy, completeness, and correctness of a model, marks invalid elements in the model, and suggests solutions.</p><h3>Validation suites</h3><p>Models are validated against a set of validation rules. Each validation rule captures an imperative condition that must be checked against the model. Validation rules are grouped into meaningful groups called validation suites. There are predefined validation suites that you can customize. You can also create your own rules and group them into suites. Some validation rules are active; that is, they run all the time. Validation rules and suites can be exported into a separate project in order to reuse them. They are stored in the Teamwork Cloud server for exchange.</p><ul><li data-uuid="3c8aa257-b581-4c82-b115-29a38ae4e662"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Predefined validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about predefined validation suites >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="32561615-a4e3-4d70-b86b-c8f4f280748f"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/SysML+validation+suites">Learn more about SysML validation suites &gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="0c529fc6-8bb1-4a78-9da8-26ad558e7c6c"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Active validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about active validation suites >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="3b010ce3-291f-479c-9f38-405a4c830950"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/SysML+active+validation+suites">Learn more about SysML active validation suites &gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="e8842525-8a0b-4111-8145-7cf0d64fd36a"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Requirements validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Requirements validation suites >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="9ee830fa-1604-4f52-8e33-eb57f84f2326"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to create new validation suites >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="bf3979fd-e2e7-4ee2-b28c-d88b86213201"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating new validation rules" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to create new validation rule >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="4b556ab3-a8d9-4d1f-92df-5c34ea474cf1"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Global validation rules" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about global validation rules >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Validating your model</h3><p>The validation can run on the entire model or on a selected part of it. All the elements in that scope are checked against relevant validation rules, and elements that violate these rules are reported in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation Results panel" /><ac:link-body><strong>Validation Results</strong> panel</ac:link-body></ac:link>. They are also marked in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>, and their symbols are highlighted. Diagrams, tables, matrices, and relation maps displaying these highlighted symbols have <span class="confluence-link">the validation results marker bar</span>.</p><ul><li data-uuid="84fb6614-c29e-448d-ab90-9c436c70cec5"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting the validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to start the validation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="5d3dda1a-8490-45f9-abcc-4a014e3d6668"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation results marker bar" /><ac:link-body>Learn more about <span class="current">validation results marker bar &gt;&gt;</span></ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="d3e361c0-d91a-44aa-bde9-902057d36ebc"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation Results panel" /><ac:link-body>Learn more about <span class="current">Validation Results panel &gt;&gt;</span></ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="131bea33-6168-4f84-a661-e4b377308acd"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Validation dialog >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="b039b066-7296-4053-8a5d-a906f062eecd"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pre-commit validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Pre-Commit Validation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Invalid elements representation</h3><p>The invalid elements are marked as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="848670b0-2b50-4626-94a7-00994913bccd"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="error_icon.png" /></ac:image> - error or fatal error.</li><li data-uuid="73b46a4d-6df5-4b78-8de2-a1f4f1e157d4"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="warning_icon.png" /></ac:image> - warning.</li><li data-uuid="a1189a0d-c5d3-4fc7-a5c0-a039c8dacbfa"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="info_icon.png" /></ac:image> - debug or info.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d02a399a-e347-4ed9-a562-d89caa67449f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to customize severity levels >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can see invalid elements in the following areas:</p><ul><li data-uuid="bd6c856a-eee4-4c6d-9b6b-6a259dacd1dd">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> an invalid element is marked with a small symbol that depends on the failure severity.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="invalid_element_in_model_brower.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="75ad8e69-7364-4e0c-b726-2190c1dc3ec3">On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> an invalid symbol is highlighted.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="invalid_symbol_on_diagram_pane.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="38211605-9ca8-48c0-85bd-7d84239799ff">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation results marker bar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[validation results marker bar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> every marker of an invalid symbol is colored according to the violation severity.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="invalid_symbol_in_validation_results_marker_bar.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="c0567f58-44ea-4c8f-bc93-8f3f8b5568f5"><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[status line]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the failure indicator displays the severity icon, number of errors, and the first letter of the error severity (F - fatal error; E - error; W - warning; D - debug; I - info).<br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="81"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_error.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b6e9bd0-8afb-4361-9b9f-432a6b38dbdb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Solvers of invalid elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can analyze invalid elements and solve problems by selecting appropriate commands from the invalid element or symbol shortcut menu or in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation Results panel" /><ac:link-body><strong>Validation Results</strong> panel</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969569 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06597: Validation dialog

- page_id: `243969569`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969569/Validation+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:32.525+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Validation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

60200619

60200621

60200618

**On this page**

43

60200620

INLINE

##### Introduction

The **Validation** dialog allows you to limit the scope of the constraints to validate against your model. Since the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation suites' space=''] check your model all the time, the validation according [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Predefined validation suites' space='']must be started by using this dialog. If you select the **All Validation Suite** as the **Validation Suite** in the **Validation** dialog, your model is validated against all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Predefined validation suites' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation suites' space=''] at the same time. To limit the scope of the constraints to be validated against, select a more specific validation suite, such as *Numbering Validation*. The model or its part will be validated against the selected validation suite only. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='']**Validation Results** panel shows the results, listing all elements that do not conform to some constraints (invalid elements) in the selected validation suite.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Validation dialog. Its options (1,2,3,4,5) are described below.

##### Opening the Validation dialog

To open the **Validation** dialog

1. Do one of the following: - On the main menu, select Analyze > Validation > Validate . - On the Validation toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
2. In the Validation dialog, specify the options. >]]>
3. Click Validate . The model or its part is validated against the selected validation suite. The results are shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='']**Validation Results** panel .

You can run the validation for the entire diagram, table, matrix, relation map, or elements. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting the validation' space='']>]]>

##### descrDescription of Validation dialog options

The **Validation** dialog consists of the following:

- The **Validation Suite** drop-down list (#1 in the image above) allows you to select a validation suite that you want to validate the model or its part against. The list of available validation suites depends on the open project; the validation suites and validation rules are stored in the model as regular model elements.
- The Validate For drop-down list (#2 in the image above) allows you to choose:
  - **Whole Project** to run validation on the entire model.
  - **Validation Selection** to run validation on special packages and/or elements. Then click the ... button and select the scope for validation. Validation is always recursive. If you select a package for validation, you do not need to select its inner elements.
- The Minimal Severity drop-down list (#3 in the image above) allows you to select the minimal severity level: debug , info , warning , error , fatal . Debug is the lowest possible severity level.
- Clear the Exclude elements from projects used in read-only mode check box (#4 in the image above) if you want to run validation on the elements stored in the read-only project usages.
- Clear the Exclude elements from additional content check box (#5 in the image above) if you want to include the content of Smart Packages while running the passive validation.
- The Validation Suites button (#6 in the image above) opens the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Suites dialog' space='']**Validation Suites** dialog .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969546 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06599: Validation results marker bar

- page_id: `243969546`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969546/Validation+results+marker+bar
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:32.314+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Validation
- labels: ['validation-results-marker-bar', 'validation', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The validation results marker bar appears at the right side of a diagram pane, if the diagram contains symbols of invalid elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The markers are shown even for the items that are not currently visible on the screen (you may need to scroll down to see them). The marker bar shrinks to fit the screen and displays relative positions of annotated symbols on the axis of ordinates. Every marker is colored according to the violation severity.

Clicking a marker on the bar selects the appropriate symbol on the diagram pane. Moving the mouse over the marker eye (the red square at the top-right corner of the diagram pane) opens the summary of the diagram validation results.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2feb933-14a9-4f08-977b-fe2421fd0853"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The validation results marker bar appears at the right side of a diagram pane, if the diagram contains symbols of invalid elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Validation results marker bar and summary of diagram validation results" ac:alt="Validation results marker bar and summary of diagram validation results"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_results_marker_bar.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The markers are shown even for the items that are not currently visible on the screen (you may need to scroll down to see them). The marker bar shrinks to fit the screen and displays relative positions of annotated symbols on the axis of ordinates. Every marker is colored according to the violation severity.</p><p>Clicking a marker on the bar selects the appropriate symbol on the diagram pane. Moving the mouse over the marker eye (the red <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="97a32128-7416-4d36-b8bc-082522573e77">square</ac:inline-comment-marker> at the top-right corner of the diagram pane) opens the summary of the diagram validation results.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969548 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06600: Validation Results panel

- page_id: `243969548`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969548/Validation+Results+panel
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:24:04.424+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Validation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1278320370

INLINE

1278320374

INLINE

1278320368

INLINE

**On this page**

43

1278320372

INLINE

INLINE

##### Introduction

The**Validation Results** panel and **Active Validation Results** panel display the results of the validation: invalid element, severity, explanation of the validation rule and its abbreviation. The **Active Validation Results** panel shows the results of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation suites' space=''], while the **Validation Results** panel shows [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Predefined validation suites' space=''].

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Validation Results and Active Validation Results panels.

##### Opening the Validation Results panel

The **Validation Results** panel opens automatically after the validation has ended. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting the validation' space='']>]]>

Since the active validation suites always check your model's correctness, you must open the **Active Validation Results** panel to see the results of active validation.

To open the **Active Validation Results** panel

- On the [status line](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Understanding+the+user+interface), click the failure indicator (e.g. [IMAGE alt='' src='']). If you cannot see the failure indicator on the status line, your model is correct according to active validation suites.

##### Description of Validation Results panel areas

The **Validation Results** panel consists of the following areas:

44

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Validation Results panel areas: toolbar, validation results, and summary of severity areas.

###### toolbarToolbar

The toolbar allows you to manage the validation results. All buttons are described in the following table.

| Button icon | Button name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Expand All | Click to expand recursively all the elements in the validation results area. |
|  | Collapse All | Click to collapse recursively all the elements in the validation results area. |
|  | Navigate to Validated Object (Alt+B) | Click the button to select the element in the .Click the arrow next to the button to open the menu of the following navigation commands:Open Specification - opens of the selected element in the validation results area.Go To - opens a submenu whose commands allow you to track and navigate to the elements. Select Rule in The Containment Tree - selects the failed validation rule in the of the selected element in the validation results area.Add to Search Results - selects an element in the . |
|  | Solve | Click to open the list of available commands to solve a selected element violation in the validation results area. |
|  | Ignore | Click to ignore a selected element violation in the validation results area. In server projects, you can select whether to ignore the violation for you as a project user or for all users who works on that server project.The button is available when you select an unignored element in the validation results area.Click the arrow next to the button to open the menu of the following commands:Ignore Validation Rule - ignores the validation rule of a selected element in the validation results area.Ignore Validation Suite - ignores the validation suite of a selected element in the validation results area. |
|  | Unignore | Click to make a selected element in the validation results area violation unignored.The button is available when you select an ignored element in the validation results area. |
|  | Display the full path of the elements | Click to show the full path of all elements in the validation results area. |
|  | Run validation with current settings | Click to run the validation with current settings. |
|  | Run validation with new settings | Click to open the Validation dialog and run the validation with new settings. |
|  | Export | Click to export the list of validation results either to .html or .csv file. |

###### Validation results area

The validation results area provides a list of violated elements. This area contains the following columns:

- Element - elements that violate the validation rule.
- Severity - severity level of the validation rule.
- Abbreviation - a short word, phase, or acronym of the validation rule used for sorting/filtering.
- Message - description of the invalid situation when a validation rule fails.
- Is Ignored - indicator of whether the violation is ignored or not.

You can use the validation results area to:

- 
- 
- 

manageTo manage validation results

- Right-click the element in the validation results area and select one of the following commands from the shortcut menu:
  - Solver name
  - Ignore
  - Navigate to Validated Object
  - Open Specification
  - Go To
  - Select Rule in The Containment Tree

All those commands are described in the .

sortTo sort the validation results

- Click the header of the column you want to sort the validation results. A small arrow appears on the column header:
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='ascending_sort.png'] - ascending sorting.
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='decending_sort.png'] - descending sorting.

filterTo filter the validation results

1. Point to the header of a column whose value you want to select for filtering the validation results.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='filter_validation_results.png'] at the right of the column header and select a value from the drop-down list.

###### Summary of severity area

The summary of the severity area displays the number and severity of violated elements in expanded packages. If you collapse the package in the validation results area, the number of violated elements are excluded from the count.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="737e9a55-800f-4d67-8dc3-ba49ae62e920"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1278320370</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e06a9f0f-fb94-419c-8cdd-e994f7543b8e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1278320374</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:macro-id="1860df39-4b8c-4731-ab80-0173f2116b98"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1278320368</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:macro-id="cbd89ece-c831-4250-8abc-0117448fd499"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter
````
