ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a38b9dd-e7f9-4fd5-9156-3864a8ff5ac9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1278320372</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c6d7197-a137-402b-892d-25cb63025ff3"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Introduction</h3><p>The<strong> Validation Results</strong> panel and <strong>Active Validation Results</strong> panel display the results of the validation: invalid element, severity, explanation of the validation rule and its abbreviation. The <strong>Active Validation Results</strong> panel shows the results of <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Active validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[active validation suites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, while the <strong> Validation Results</strong> panel shows <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Predefined validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[predefined validation suites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_results_panels.png" /></ac:image>The Validation Results and Active Validation Results panels.</h6><h3>Opening the Validation Results panel</h3><p>The <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel opens automatically after the validation has ended. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting the validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to start the validation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>Since the active validation suites always check your model's correctness, you must open the <strong>Active Validation Results</strong> panel to see the results of active validation.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Active Validation Results</strong> panel</p><hr /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Understanding+the+user+interface">status line</a>, click the failure indicator (e.g. <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="failure_indicator.png" /></ac:image>).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe2437fa-1129-4775-9719-9be6b180c832"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you cannot see the failure indicator on the status line, your model is correct according to active validation suites.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><h3>Description of Validation Results panel areas</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel consists of the following areas:</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20861bfa-1ce3-4f3c-b9fb-5ba102f312b3"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></span></p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_results_panel_areas.png" /></ac:image>The Validation Results panel areas: toolbar, validation results, and summary of severity areas.</h6><h4><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ccfcf66-04ab-413a-82e0-73baa58473fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">toolbar</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Toolbar</span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The toolbar allows you to manage the validation results. All buttons are described in the following table.</span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 111.0px;" /><col style="width: 237.0px;" /><col style="width: 584.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button icon</th><th>Button name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Expand All" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Expand All" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_all.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Expand All</td><td>Click to expand recursively all the elements in the validation results area.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Collapse All" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Collapse All" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="collapse_all.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Collapse All</td><td>Click to collapse recursively all the elements in the validation results area.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="navigate_to_validated_object.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Navigate to Validated Object (Alt+B)</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the button to select the element in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>Click the arrow next to the button to open the menu of the following navigation commands:</p><ul><li><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_specification.png" /></ac:image><strong>Open Specification</strong> - opens <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of the selected element in the validation results area.</li><li><strong><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="﻿go_to.png" /></ac:image>Go To</strong> - opens a submenu whose commands allow you to track and navigate to the elements.</li><li><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_rule_in_containment_tree.png" /></ac:image> <strong>Select Rule in The Containment Tree </strong>- selects the failed validation rule in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the selected element in the validation results area.</li><li><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_to_search_results.png" /></ac:image><strong>Add to Search Results</strong> - selects an element in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Search Results tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Search Results tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<strong> </strong></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Solve" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Solve" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="solve.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Solve</p></div></td><td><p>Click to open the list of available commands to solve a selected element violation in the validation results area.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Ignore" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Ignore" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ignore.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Ignore</td><td><p>Click to ignore a selected element violation in the validation results area. In server projects, you can select whether to ignore the violation for you as a project user or for all users who works on that server project.</p><p>The button is available when you select an unignored element in the validation results area.</p><p>Click the arrow next to the button to open the menu of the following commands:</p><ul><li><strong>Ignore Validation Rule</strong> - ignores the validation rule of a selected element in the validation results area.</li><li><strong>Ignore Validation Suite</strong> - ignores the validation suite of a selected element in the validation results area.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Unignore" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Unignore" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="unignore.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Unignore</td><td><p>Click to make a selected element in the validation results area violation unignored.</p><p>The button is available when you select an ignored element in the validation results area.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Display the full path of the elements" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Display the full path of the elements" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display_the_full_path_of_the_elements.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Display the full path of the elements</p></td><td>Click to show the full path of all elements in the validation results area.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Run validation with current settings" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Run validation with current settings" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="run_validation_with_current_settings.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Run validation with current settings</p></td><td>Click to run the validation with current settings.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Run validation with new settings" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Run validation with new settings" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="run_validation_with_new_settings.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Run validation with new settings</p></td><td><p>Click to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation dialog" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Validation</strong></span> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and run the validation with new settings.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Export" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Export" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="export.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Export</td><td><p>Click to export the list of validation results either to .html or .csv file.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p><h4>Validation results area</h4><p>The validation results area provides a list of violated elements. This area contains the following columns:</p><ul><li><strong>Element</strong> - elements that violate the validation rule.</li><li><strong>Severity</strong> - severity level of the validation rule.</li><li><strong>Abbreviation</strong> - a short word, phase, or acronym of the validation rule used for sorting/filtering.</li><li><strong>Message</strong> - description of the invalid situation when a validation rule fails.</li><li><strong>Is Ignored</strong> - indicator of whether the violation is ignored or not.</li></ul><p>You can use the validation results area to:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="manage"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Manage validation results]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="sort"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sort validation results]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="filter"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Filter validation results]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9336097-713a-4edb-bc04-a87463283b8e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">manage</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To manage validation results</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the element in the validation results area and select one of the following commands from the shortcut menu:<ul><li>Solver name</li><li><strong>Ignore</strong></li><li><strong>Navigate to Validated Object</strong></li><li><strong>Open Specification</strong></li><li><strong>Go To</strong></li><li><strong>Select Rule in The Containment Tree <br /><br /></strong></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c19c4f64-3fe0-489e-99f5-6813e0498cba"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All those commands are described in the <ac:link ac:anchor="toolbar"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table above]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e08f7231-142d-4271-8d7b-254ef4532431"><ac:parameter ac:name="">sort</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To sort the validation results</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the header of the column you want to sort the validation results. A small arrow appears on the column header:<ul><li><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="28"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ascending_sort.png" /></ac:image>- ascending sorting.</li><li><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="28"><ri:attachment ri:filename="decending_sort.png" /></ac:image>- descending sorting.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83be7782-8da8-408a-82d0-698d6ff1aef8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">filter</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To filter the validation results</p><hr /><ol><li>Point to the header of a column whose value you want to select for filtering the validation results.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_validation_results.png" /></ac:image> at the right of the column header and select a value from the drop-down list.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h4>Summary of severity area</h4><p>The summary of the severity area displays the number and severity of violated elements in expanded packages. If you collapse the package in the validation results area, the number of violated elements are excluded from the count.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969577 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06601: Validation Suites dialog

- page_id: `243969577`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969577/Validation+Suites+dialog
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-09T13:44:21.449+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating validation suites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Validation Suites** dialog allows you to create new suites, clone read-only validation suites, include one suite to another, and include/exclude already existing validation rules in validation suites.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Validation Suites dialog areas: toolbar, Validation Suites, and Validation Rules areas.

##### Opening the Validation Suites dialog

To open the **Validation Suites** dialog

- Do either: - From the main menu, select Analyze > Validation > Validation Suites . - Open the **Validation** dialog and click the Validation Suites button.

##### Description of Validation Suites dialog areas

The **Validation Suites** dialog consists of the areas described below.

###### toolToolbar

The toolbar allows you to manage the validation suites. All buttons are described in the following table.

| Button icon | Button name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Create New Validation Suite | Click to create a new validation suite. This button contains the following commands:Validation Suite - creates a new validation suite, stored in your model as a Package with «validationSuite» stereotype applied. The validation rules stored in this suite will check if a model is correct and complete only when you start your model validation manually. >]]>Active Validation Suite - creates a new active validation suite stored in your model as a Package with «activeValidationSuite» stereotype applied. The validation rules stored in this suite will be active and check if a model is correct and complete immediately. >]]> |
|  | Clone Validation Suite | Click to clone an already existing validation suite with its validation rules. It is stored in your model as a Package with «validationSuite» stereotype applied. |
|  | Rename (F2) | Click to rename the package of new or cloned validation suites. |
|  | Remove | Click to delete the new or cloned validation suites from the model. |

###### Validation Suites area

The Validation Suites area shows a list of all validation suites that comes with a profile (read-only) or your own created validation suites (editable).

Each profile contains its own validation suites.

You can manage validation suites directly in the Validation Suites area by using the or shortcut menu commands: **Create Validation Suite**, **Create Active Validation Suite**,**Clone Validation Suite**, **Rename (F2)**, or **Remove**.

All those commands are described in the .

###### Validation Rules area

The Validation Rules area contains:

- *Constraints Tree*displaying the structure of the validation suite Packages with their validation rules. It allows you to include/exclude validation rules to/from a newly created or cloned validation suite Package. For this select/clear the check box before the entire Package or an appropriate validation rule. You cannot include/exclude the validation rules to/from read-only validation suites.
- Buttons :

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Edit | Click to open the of the selected element in the Constraint Tree. |
| Load | Click to load the with its validation rules. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Validation Suites</strong> dialog allows you to create new suites, clone read-only validation suites, include one suite to another, and include/exclude already existing validation rules in validation suites.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_suites_dialog_areas.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Validation Suites dialog areas: toolbar, Validation Suites, and Validation Rules areas.</h6><h3>Opening the Validation Suites dialog</h3><p>To open the <strong>Validation Suites</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="d9fa5675-f89f-4ba0-9add-93b35796ef02">Do either:<br /> - From the main menu, select <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation Suites</strong>.<br /> - <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Validation+dialog#Validationdialog-OpeningtheValidationdialog">Open the <strong>Validation</strong> dialog</a> and click the <strong>Validation Suites</strong> button.</li></ul><h3>Description of Validation Suites dialog areas</h3><p>The <strong>Validation Suites</strong> dialog consists of the areas described below.</p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62cf3c7d-5a38-4a6c-90e4-dd87e0aaa173"><ac:parameter ac:name="">tool</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Toolbar</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The toolbar allows you to manage the validation suites. All buttons are described in the following table.</span></p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 102.0px;" /><col class="" style="width: 195.0px;" /><col class="" style="width: 792.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Button icon</th><th>Button name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new_validation_suite.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Create New Validation Suite</td><td><p>Click to create a new validation suite. This button contains the following commands:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c03049bf-f43a-40dd-b1d8-ca18f879afa1"><strong>Validation Suite</strong> - creates a new validation suite, stored in your model as a Package with «validationSuite» stereotype applied. The validation rules stored in this suite will check if a model is correct and complete only when you start your model validation manually. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting the validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to start validation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="b9aa82d4-bd65-4e97-abde-836596a5fbcd"><strong>Active Validation Suite</strong> - creates a new active validation suite stored in your model as a Package with «activeValidationSuite» stereotype applied. The validation rules stored in this suite will be active and check if a model is correct and complete immediately. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Active validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about active validation suites >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="clone_validation_suite.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Clone Validation Suite</td><td>Click to clone an already existing validation suite with its validation rules. It is stored in your model as a Package with «validationSuite» stereotype applied.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rename_validation_suite.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Rename (F2)</td><td>Click to rename the package of new or cloned validation suites.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_validation_suite.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Remove</td><td>Click to delete the new or cloned validation suites from the model.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><h4>Validation Suites area</h4><p>The Validation Suites area shows a list of all validation suites that comes with a profile (read-only) or your own created validation suites (editable).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66f99b95-6f83-4d42-a19c-262b473a3e29"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Each profile contains its own validation suites.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can manage validation suites directly in the Validation Suites area by using the<ac:link ac:anchor="tool"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ toolbar buttons]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or shortcut menu commands: <strong>Create Validation Suite</strong>, <strong>Create Active Validation Suite</strong>,<strong> Clone Validation Suite</strong>, <strong>Rename (F2)</strong>, or <strong>Remove</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d49e2382-bb85-4c77-8931-da4e8cd364fb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All those commands are described in the <ac:link ac:anchor="tool"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table above]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Validation Rules area</h4><p>The Validation Rules area contains:</p><ul><li data-uuid="ed248224-b883-4c69-b2ce-216cfd9feb5f"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><em>Constraints Tree </em>displaying the structure of the validation suite Packages with their validation rules. It allows you to include/exclude validation rules to/from a newly created or cloned validation suite Package. For this select/clear the check box before the entire Package or an appropriate validation rule.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25658120-68c2-4059-9e18-54c3c16c196c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You cannot include/exclude the validation rules to/from read-only validation suites.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="a87d391e-faf4-441e-9cc2-fb1faa6e687c"><em>Buttons</em>:<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li></ul><table class="relative-table" style="width: 38.1788%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 15.813%;" /><col class="" style="width: 69.518%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Edit</strong></td><td>Click to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of the selected element in the <em>Constraint Tree</em>.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Load</strong></td><td>Click to load the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with its validation rules.</td></tr></tbody></table>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561409 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06602: Value Propagation

- page_id: `249561409`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561409/Value+Propagation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T10:25:15.348+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Initial Values
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The value propagation mechanism enables values that are not overridden by the values from the selected context in the *initialValue* compartment to be displayed. Assuming the property and the Value Propagation options are enabled, the value available in the next context will be used to reconfigure the property if there is no value in the selected context to reconfigure the property. However, if there is no value available in any context, the class-level default value will be displayed in the *initialValue* compartment, indicating that the property is not reconfigured at all.

The value propagation mechanism is enabled by default. To disable this mechanism, follow the procedure below.

To disable the value propagation mechanism

1. On the main toolbar, click Options > Project . .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > SysML .
3. Select the Propagate SysML Values property value to false .
4. Click OK .

#### TIP: Example when the value propagation mechanism is enabled

Example when the value propagation mechanism is enabled

In the UniverseContext package, only the value of x of a Location is reconfigured to 3 in the UniverseContext context. The values of y and z are not set by the selected context. Since the value propagation is enabled, the next context, Universe, is considered. In the Universe context, the value of z is set to 2. However, the value of y is still missing; therefore, the next context, Thing, is considered. In the Thing context, the value of y is set to 1. Now, all attributes of the Location are set as follows:

- x = 3
- y = 1
- z = 2

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The value propagation mechanism enables values that are not overridden by the values from the selected context in the <em>initialValue</em> compartment to be displayed. Assuming the property and the Value Propagation options are enabled, the value available in the next context will be used to reconfigure the property if there is no value in the selected context to reconfigure the property. However, if there is no value available in any context, the class-level default value will be displayed in the <em>initialValue</em> compartment, indicating that the property is not reconfigured at all.</p><p>The value propagation mechanism is enabled by default. To disable this mechanism, follow the procedure below.</p><p>To disable the value propagation mechanism</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main toolbar, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. .</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, select <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>SysML</strong>.</li><li>Select the <strong>Propagate SysML Values</strong> property value to<em>false</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9bc5f836-d58e-41be-8162-97be77a1bb60"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example when the value propagation mechanism is enabled</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the UniverseContext package, only the value of x of a Location is reconfigured to 3 in the UniverseContext context. The values of y and z are not set by the selected context. Since the value propagation is enabled, the next context, Universe, is considered. In the Universe context, the value of z is set to 2. However, the value of y is still missing; therefore, the next context, Thing, is considered. In the Thing context, the value of y is set to 1. Now, all attributes of the Location are set as follows:</p><ul><li>x = 3</li><li>y = 1</li><li>z = 2</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2017-4-25_11-26-56.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971321 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06603: Velocity Directives

- page_id: `243971321`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971321/Velocity+Directives
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:11.237+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Velocity templating language
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1357045784

1357045785

1357045785

A Velocity directive is a keyword used by Velocity to perform certain tasks such as looping and controlling the output of the document based on values from the modeling tool.

With directives you can make dynamic templates and you can do more than just print out elements and attributes. A Velocity directive begins with **#** and followed by the directive name. Some of the frequently used Velocity directives are as follows:

1. #if , #elseif , and #else : these directives are used to decide whether or not text should be included in the output based on the conditional statement.
2. #set : this directive is used to assign a value to a variable.
3. #foreach : this directive is used to iterate through a list of variables.
4. #macro : this directive is used as a means to create a reusable script. It is especially useful if you need to call a certain line of scripts repeatedly.

1357045783

**Related pages**

A Velocity directive begins with <strong>#</strong> and followed by the directive name. Some of the frequently used Velocity directives are as follows:</p><ol><li><strong>#if</strong>, <strong>#elseif</strong>, and <strong>#else</strong>: these directives are used to decide whether or not text should be included in the output based on the conditional statement.</li><li><strong>#set</strong>: this directive is used to assign a value to a variable.</li><li><strong>#foreach</strong>: this directive is used to iterate through a list of variables.</li><li><strong>#macro</strong>: this directive is used as a means to create a reusable script. It is especially useful if you need to call a certain line of scripts repeatedly.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="620b5f44-617c-4173-9b95-77f1e5f8d1f5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357045783</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="af5875c2-eca2-406a-a317-e6cc4ae713ba" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970761 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06604: Velocity template language

- page_id: `243970761`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970761/Velocity+template+language
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:56.107+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Report Wizard template language
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The user guide for Velocity (VTL) can be found at: *[http://velocity.apache.org/engine/devel/user-guide.html](http://velocity.apache.org/engine/devel/user-guide.html).*

#### WARNING: Important

Important

- The formal reference such as ${hello} is not supported in RTF templates.
- A macro cannot be used as macro parameter in any RTF report template. This is a limitation of VTL itself: Velocity treats a macro like copy-and-paste content defined in the macro (between #macro and #end) at the inserted position.
- In an RTF report template, the style and formatting defined on a directive will have no effect on the report output. In addition, the paragraph symbol at the end of a line will also be removed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The user guide for Velocity (VTL) can be found at: <em><a href="http://velocity.apache.org/engine/devel/user-guide.html">http://velocity.apache.org/engine/devel/user-guide.html</a>.</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d6567af-e1af-47d3-8891-94e0c001b235"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The formal reference such as ${hello} is not supported in RTF templates.</li><li>A macro cannot be used as macro parameter in any RTF report template. This is a limitation of VTL itself: Velocity treats a macro like copy-and-paste content defined in the macro (between #macro and #end) at the inserted position.</li><li>In an RTF report template, the style and formatting defined on a directive will have no effect on the report output. In addition, the paragraph symbol at the end of a line will also be removed.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971319 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06605: Velocity templating language

- page_id: `243971319`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971319/Velocity+templating+language
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:11.123+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1356919600

1356919600

1356919600

Velocity Template Language is a scripting language used only by Velocity Engine to determine what the output should look like. This section is divided into three parts:

- Velocity Variable
- Velocity Directive
- Comment and unparsing code

For more information on the Velocity Template Language, visit [https://velocity.apache.org/engine/2.2/user-guide.html](https://velocity.apache.org/engine/2.2/user-guide.html)[.](http://velocity.apache.org/engine/releases/velocity-1.6.2/user-guide.html+)

1356919599

**Related pages**

This section is divided into three parts:</p><ul><li>Velocity Variable</li><li>Velocity Directive</li><li>Comment and unparsing code</li></ul><p>For more information on the Velocity Template Language, visit <a href="https://velocity.apache.org/engine/2.2/user-guide.html" title="Follow link">https://velocity.apache.org/engine/2.2/user-guide.html</a><a href="http://velocity.apache.org/engine/releases/velocity-1.6.2/user-guide.html+" class="external-link" rel="nofollow">.</a></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a0124e2-2885-40fa-8b8b-0c9c5a8076e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1356919599</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7b81369c-1839-41e1-8c96-81801c3e1a24" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971320 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06606: Velocity variable

- page_id: `243971320`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971320/Velocity+variable
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:11.180+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Velocity templating language
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Velocity variable can be either a referenced Java object or a declared variable inside a template. A Velocity variable begins with **$** followed by the name of the variable. Depending on what is added to the Velocity Context (the modeling tool automatically adds its element to the Velocity Context) the variable can either be a local variable or a reference to a Java object.

To declare a local variable inside a template, type: **$** followed by a string beginning with a letter. A referenced Java object variable is provided by Report Wizard as shown in the following table (see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Linking report templates to the modeling tool' space=''] section for a complete list of variables).

The table shows a partial list of modeling tool-specific variables.

| Variable Name | Return Value |
| --- | --- |
| $Class | Contains a list of class elements. |
| $UseCase | Contains a list of use case elements. |
| $Diagram | Contains a list of diagram elements. |

If a Velocity variable is a reference to a Java object, you can call its methods and value by:

- Directly calling the object method with the Velocity variable, for example, $object.method() .This is useful if you want to use a Java object's method to process data and print it out.
- Calling the object properties, for example, $object.name .

Velocity allows you to call the referenced Java object's method directly like you would in Java. This is very useful as you can create methods in Java that can help you create a more flexible Velocity template. For instance, if you need to perform a complex operation, such as sorting a list according to certain variables, you can create a sorting method in Java, and then call that method from a Java object.

Report Wizard provides a collection of complex statements in standard tool methods. You can find more information on the standard tool in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Helper tools' space=''] section, Report Wizard User Guide. You can also create your own custom tool, deploy, and use it inside a template. For more information on the custom tool, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Appendix A: report extensions' space='MT'], Report Wizard User Guide.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- Report Wizard does not support calling variables in double quotes. 
 
The following calls are not valid: text

- If you want to concatenate string, use the following style: text

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Velocity variable can be either a referenced Java object or a declared variable inside a template. A Velocity variable begins with <strong>$</strong> followed by the name of the variable. Depending on what is added to the Velocity Context (the modeling tool automatically adds its element to the Velocity Context) the variable can either be a local variable or a reference to a Java object.</p><p>To declare a local variable inside a template, type: <strong>$</strong> followed by a string beginning with a letter. A referenced Java object variable is provided by Report Wizard as shown in the following table  (see the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,255);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Linking report templates to the modeling tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Linking Report Wizard template to modeling tool]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> section for a complete list of variables).</p><p>The table shows a partial list of modeling tool-specific variables.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p>Variable Name</p></th><th><p>Return Value</p></th></tr><tr><td><p>$Class</p></td><td><p>Contains a list of class elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>$UseCase</p></td><td><p>Contains a list of use case elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>$Diagram</p></td><td><p>Contains a list of diagram elements.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>If a Velocity variable is a reference to a Java object, you can call its methods and value by:</p><ul><li>Directly calling the object method with the Velocity variable, for example, <strong>$object.method()</strong>.This is useful if you want to use a Java object's method to process data and print it out.</li><li>Calling the object properties, for example, <strong>$object.name</strong>.</li></ul><p>Velocity allows you to call the referenced Java object's method directly like you would in Java. This is very useful as you can create methods in Java that can help you create a more flexible Velocity template. For instance, if you need to perform a complex operation, such as sorting a list according to certain variables, you can create a sorting method in Java, and then call that method from a Java object.</p><p>Report Wizard provides a collection of complex statements in standard tool methods. You can find more information on the standard tool in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Helper tools" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Helper Modules]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section, Report Wizard User Guide. You can also create your own custom tool, deploy, and use it inside a template. For more information on the custom tool, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Appendix A: report extensions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Appendix A: Report Extensions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, Report Wizard User Guide.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24b06755-6994-436e-a7d4-cb26619babdc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Report Wizard does not support calling variables in double quotes.<br /><br />The following calls are not valid:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e895bb7-09d0-4e3a-bc4e-6e04516bdb4c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($newVTL = "$vtl")
#set($newVTL = "text $vtl")
#aMacro("text $vtl")
#aMacro("$vtl")
#if("$text.name" == "class")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you want to concatenate string, use the following style:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fbba1a9-0709-4b4f-b22f-0be37a3a0891"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($newVTL = "text " + $vtl)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565571 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06608: View mapping

- page_id: `249565571`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565571/View+mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:37.591+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Model migration
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 2.1 and UAF 1.1.

| UPDM 2.1 | UAF 1.1 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Svcv-6 | SV-6 | The SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix. Instead of SvcV-6 diagram, the SV-6 diagram is introduced and recommended to use. |
| Svcv-6 Role-based | SV-6 Role-based | The SvcV-6 Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-6 Role-based Systems Resource Flow Matrix. Instead of SvcV-6 Role-based diagram, the SV-6 Role-based diagram is introduced and recommended to use. |
| Service Channels Summary Table | Deleted. | The Service Channels Summary Table is no longer used for service channels analysis, creation, and modification. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector. |
| Service Channels Summary Matrix | Deleted. | The Service Channels Summary Matrix is no longer used for service channels analysis and creation. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix. Instead of SvcV-6 diagram, the SV-6 diagram is introduced and recommended to use.</td></tr><tr><td>Svcv-6 Role-based</td><td>SV-6 Role-based</td><td>The SvcV-6 Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-6 Role-based Systems Resource Flow Matrix. Instead of SvcV-6 Role-based diagram, the SV-6 Role-based diagram is introduced and recommended to use.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Channels Summary Table</td><td>Deleted.</td><td>The Service Channels Summary Table is no longer used for service channels analysis, creation, and modification. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Channels Summary Matrix</td><td>Deleted.</td><td>The Service Channels Summary Matrix is no longer used for service channels analysis and creation. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565601 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06609: View Mappings

- page_id: `249565601`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565601/View+Mappings
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:38.409+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 1.x Migration to UPDM 2.0
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 1.x and UPDM 2.0.

| UPDM 1.x | UPDM 2.0 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CV-3 | CV-2 | CV-3 Capability Phasing diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the CV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. Instead of the diagram, the CV-3 Capability Phasing chart is introduced and recommended to use. |
| CV-5 | CV-2 | CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the CV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. The CV-5 tabular form of the representation is introduced and recommended to use. |
| StV-3 | StV-2 | StV-3 Capability Phasing diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. Instead of the diagram, the StV-3 Capability Taxonomy chart is introduced and recommended to use. |
| StV-5 | StV-2 | StV-5 Organization Deployment Mapping diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. The StV-5 tabular form of representation is introduced and recommended to use. |
| PV-2 | PV-1 | PV-2 Project Timelines diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships diagram. Instead of the diagram, the PV-2 Project Timelines chart is introduced and recommended to use. |
| AcV-2 | AcV-1 | AcV-2 Programme Timelines diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters diagram. Instead of the diagram, the AcV-2 Acquisition Clusters chart is introduced and recommended to use. |
| SV-8 | SV-1 | SV-8 Capability Configuration Management diagram is refactored to meet UPDM 2.0 requirements. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification diagram. New form of the SV-8 diagram is introduced and recommended to use. |
| SvcV-1 | SOV-1 |  |
| SvcV-2 | SOV-2 |  |
| SvcV-3a | Service Channels Summary Matrix | Service Channels Summary Matrix is a new type of supportive tool, dedicated for service channels analysis and creation. |
| SvcV-3b | SV-12 |  |
| SvcV-4a | SOV-5 structural |  |
| SvcV-4b | SOV-5 behavioral |  |
| SvcV-5 | Dependency Matrix |  |
| SvcV-6 | Service Channels Summary Table | Service Channels Summary Table is a new type of supportive tool, dedicated for service channels analysis, creation, and modification. |
| SvcV-7 | Generic Table |  |
| SvcV-10a | SOV-4a |  |
| SvcV-10b | SOV-4b |  |
| SvcV-10c | SOV-4c |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 1.x and UPDM 2.0.</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 81.0811%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 8.6444%;" /><col style="width: 14.2436%;" /><col style="width: 77.112%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>UPDM 1.x </th><th>UPDM 2.0 </th><th><p>Comments</p></th></tr><tr><td>CV-3 </td><td>CV-2 </td><td><p>CV-3 Capability Phasing diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the CV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. Instead of the diagram, the CV-3 Capability Phasing chart is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td>CV-5 </td><td>CV-2 </td><td><p>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the CV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. The CV-5 tabular form of the representation is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td>StV-3 </td><td>StV-2 </td><td><p>StV-3 Capability Phasing diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. Instead of the diagram, the StV-3 Capability Taxonomy chart is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">StV-5 </td><td colspan="1">StV-2 </td><td colspan="1"><p>StV-5 Organization Deployment Mapping diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. The StV-5 tabular form of representation is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">PV-2 </td><td colspan="1">PV-1 </td><td colspan="1"><p>PV-2 Project Timelines diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships diagram. Instead of the diagram, the PV-2 Project Timelines chart is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">AcV-2 </td><td colspan="1">AcV-1 </td><td colspan="1"><p>AcV-2 Programme Timelines diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters diagram. Instead of the diagram, the AcV-2 Acquisition Clusters chart is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SV-8 </td><td colspan="1">SV-1 </td><td colspan="1"><p>SV-8 Capability Configuration Management diagram is refactored to meet UPDM 2.0 requirements. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification diagram. New form of the SV-8 diagram is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-1 </td><td colspan="1"><p>SOV-1</p></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-2 </td><td colspan="1"><p>SOV-2</p></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-3a </td><td colspan="1"><p>Service Channels Summary Matrix</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Service Channels Summary Matrix is a new type of supportive tool, dedicated for service channels analysis and creation.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-3b </td><td colspan="1">SV-12</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-4a </td><td colspan="1"><p>SOV-5 structural</p></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-4b </td><td colspan="1">SOV-5 behavioral</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-5 </td><td colspan="1">Dependency Matrix</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-6</td><td colspan="1"><p>Service Channels Summary Table</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Service Channels Summary Table is a new type of supportive tool, dedicated for service channels analysis, creation, and modification.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SvcV-7 </td><td colspan="1"><p>Generic Table</p></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>SvcV-10a</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>SOV-4a</p></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>SvcV-10b </span></td><td colspan="1"><span>SOV-4b</span></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>SvcV-10c</span></td><td colspan="1"><span> SOV-4c</span></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968626 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06610: Viewing and analyzing changes of modified diagrams

- page_id: `243968626`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968626/Viewing+and+analyzing+changes+of+modified+diagrams
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:33.595+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['merged-result', 'analyzing-changes', 'difference-viewer', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

199264098

INLINE

199264100

INLINE

199264099

INLINE

The different diagram versions are displayed in separate **Difference Viewer** tabsin the **Merge** window next to the **Merged Result** tree (see the following figure). 
 
To open diagram difference viewers of a modified diagram

- Double-click the modified diagram in the Merged Result tree.

One, two or three new tabsopen in the **Merge** window. In 3-way merge, the ancestor's tab is opened by default, the source's tab is opened, if changes have been detected in the source, and the target's tab is opened, if changes have been detected in the target. In 2-way merge, the target tab is opened by default. If a diagram is only available in source, only the source tab opens in the difference viewer .

All the diagram difference viewers are used to display the following changes:

- One or more symbols were moved or modified on the diagram.
- A new symbol was added to the diagram.
- A symbol was deleted from the diagram.
- The number of inner model elements has changed .
- A model element has been modified.

Be advised that symbols with property value changes that actually have no effect on their appearance are highlighted in a diagram difference viewer as well. For example, changing the **Show Stereotypes** property value in the **Symbol Properties** dialog of a class makes the class symbol highlighted as changed in a diagram difference viewer.

As you can see in the following figure, all these changes are marked by color coded lines and flags allowing the quick distinguishing between different types of changes. Numbers on flags indicate the number of changes made to that element. Clicking a flag switches to the **Merged Result** tab and selects the flagged element in the **Merged Result** tree.

The colors of lines and flags correspond to the color system explained in the difference legend:

- Blue flags indicate the number of modification/movement changes made to the element, blue lines represent modified/moved relations, and blue borders around symbols mark modified/moved elements in the diagram.
- Green flags indicate the number of addition changes made to the element, green lines represent added relations, and green borders around symbols mark added elements in the diagram.
- Red flags indicate the number of deletion changes made to the element, red lines represent deleted relations, and red borders around symbols mark deleted elements in the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can customize the types of changes you want to be displayed in the difference viewer by using filtering options available in the legend. Simply select or clear selection from the appropriate check boxes and view the differences that are relevant to you. 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you would like to see the diagram version from other project version without switching between tabs, use the slider available at the bottom of the **Difference Viewer**. Moving the slider makes the current diagram symbols to fade, and the symbols from another diagram version start appearing. When the slider is set to the middle position, different symbols from both diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can manage the content of a diagram difference viewer using the toolbar that is located above it. 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

See the button descriptions in the following table.

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Return to Merged Result | Click to open the Merged Result tree with the appropriate diagram selected within. |
|  | Synchronize Zooming | Click to turn on or off the zooming synchronization in all difference viewers of the same diagram. |
|  | Synchronize Scrolling | Click to turn on or off scrolling synchronization in all difference viewers of the same diagram. |
|  | Print Diagram | Click the button to print a diagram. The diagram will be printed with highlighted areas on it, if the highlighting of the diagram changes is turned on. |
|  | Zooming | Use the buttons to control the diagram size. |

199264096

INLINE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969447 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06611: Viewing and analyzing differences of modified diagrams

- page_id: `243969447`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969447/Viewing+and+analyzing+differences+of+modified+diagrams
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:24:01.251+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Comparing projects > Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['analyzing-changes', 'difference-viewer', 'comparison-result', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The different diagram versions are displayed in separate **Difference Viewer** tabs in the **Difference Viewer** window next to the **Differences** tree (see the following figure).

To open diagram difference viewers of a modified diagram

- Double-click the modified diagram in the Differences tree.

One, two or three new tabs open in the **Difference Viewer** window. The base tab is opened by default. If a diagram is only available in compared, only this tab is opened in the difference viewer.

All the diagram difference viewers are used to display the following changes:

- One or more symbols were moved or modified on the diagram.
- A new symbol was added to the diagram.
- A symbol was deleted from the diagram.
- The number of inner model elements has changed.
- A model element has been modified.

Be advised that symbols with property value changes that actually have no effect on their appearance are highlighted in a diagram difference viewer as well. For example, changing the **Show Stereotypes** property value in the **Symbol Properties** dialog of a class makes the class symbol highlighted as changed in a diagram difference viewer.

Asyou can see in the following figure, all these changes are marked by color coded lines and flags allowing the quick distinguishing between different types of changes. Numbers on flags indicate the number of changes made to that element. Clicking a flag switches to the **Differences**tab and selects the flagged element in the **Differences** tree.

The colors of lines and flags correspond to the color system explained in the difference legend:

- Blue flags indicate the number of modification/movement changes made to the element, blue lines represent modified/moved relations, and blue borders around symbols mark modified/moved elements in the diagram.
- Green flags indicate the number of addition changes made to the element, green lines represent added relations, and green borders around symbols mark added elements in the diagram.
- Red flags indicate the number of deletion changes made to the element, red lines represent deleted relations, and red borders around symbols mark deleted elements in the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can customize the types of changes you want to be displayed in the difference viewer by using filtering options available in the legend. Simply select or clear selection from the appropriate check-boxes and view the differences that are relevant to you. 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you would like to see the diagram version from other project version without switching between tabs, use the slider available at the bottom of the **Difference Viewer**. Moving the slider makes the current diagram symbols to fade, and the symbols from another diagram version start appearing. When the slider is set to the middle position, different symbols from both diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can manage the content of a diagram difference viewer using the toolbar that is located above it. 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

See the button descriptions in the following table.

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Return to Merged Result | Click to open the Differences tree with the appropriate diagram selected within. |
|  | Synchronize Zooming | Click to turn on or off the zooming synchronization in all difference viewers of the same diagram. |
|  | Synchronize Scrolling | Click to turn on or off scrolling synchronization in all difference viewers of the same diagram. |
|  | Print Diagram | Click the button to print a diagram. The diagram will be printed with highlighted areas on it, if the highlighting of the diagram changes is turned on. |
|  | Zooming | Use the buttons to control the diagram size. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The different diagram versions are displayed in separate <strong>Difference Viewer</strong> tabs in the <strong>Difference Viewer</strong> window next to the <strong>Differences</strong> tree (s<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">ee the following figure)</span>.</p><p> </p><p>To open diagram difference viewers of a modified diagram</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click the modified diagram in the <strong>Differences</strong> tree.</li></ul><p>One, two or three new tabs open in the <strong>Difference Viewer</strong> window. The base tab is opened by default. If a diagram is only available in compared, only this tab is opened in the difference viewer.</p><p>All the diagram difference viewers are used to display the following changes:</p><ul><li>One or more symbols were moved or modified on the diagram.</li><li>A new symbol was added to the diagram.</li><li>A symbol was deleted from the diagram.</li><li>The number of inner model elements has changed.</li><li>A model element has been modified.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="377a4e1e-54e0-46ab-b728-1a1b016810a2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Be advised that symbols with property value changes that actually have no effect on their appearance are highlighted in a diagram difference viewer as well. For example, changing the <strong>Show Stereotypes</strong> property value in the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog of a class makes the class symbol highlighted as changed in a diagram difference viewer.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">As </span>you can see in the following figure, all these changes are marked by color coded lines and flags allowing the quick distinguishing between different types of changes. Numbers on flags indicate the number of changes made to that element. Clicking a flag switches to the <strong>Differences </strong>tab and selects the flagged element in the <strong>Differences</strong> tree.</p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="244e86a6-66d8-4636-86b6-9c6d8998af98">The colors of lines and flags correspond to the color system explained in the difference legend</ac:inline-comment-marker>:</p><ul><li>Blue flags indicate the number of modification/movement changes made to the element, blue lines represent modified/moved relations, and blue borders around symbols mark modified/moved elements in the diagram.</li><li>Green flags indicate the number of addition changes made to the element, green lines represent added relations, and green borders around symbols mark added elements in the diagram.</li><li>Red flags indicate the number of deletion changes made to the element, red lines represent deleted relations, and red borders around symbols mark deleted elements in the diagram.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Project Compare difference viewer" ac:alt="Project Compare difference viewer"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comparer_difference_viewer.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>You can customize the types of changes you want to be displayed in the difference viewer by using filtering options available in the legend. Simply select or clear selection from the appropriate check-boxes and view the differences that are relevant to you.<br /> <ac:image ac:border="true" ac:title="Difference viewer legend and change slider" ac:alt="Difference viewer legend and change slider" ac:height="42" ac:width="595"><ri:attachment ri:filename="difference_viewer_legend.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>If you would like to see the diagram version from other project version without switching between tabs, use the slider available at the bottom of the <strong>Difference Viewer</strong>. Moving the slider makes the current diagram symbols to fade, and the symbols from another diagram version start appearing. When the slider is set to the middle position, different symbols from both diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:title="Difference slider" ac:alt="Difference slider" ac:height="44" ac:width="306"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comparer_slider.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>You can manage the content of a diagram difference viewer using the toolbar that is located above it. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <ac:image ac:border="true" ac:title="Toolbar for managing content of diagram difference viewer" ac:alt="Toolbar for managing content of diagram difference viewer" ac:height="24" ac:width="566"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comparer_difference_viewer_toolbar.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>See the button descriptions in the following table.</p><table><tbody><tr><td><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong>Button</strong></p></td><td><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong>Tooltip text</strong></p></td><td><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><ac:image ac:height="22" ac:width="144"><ri:attachment ri:filename="return_merge_result_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td colspan="1"><p>Return to Merged Result</p></td><td colspan="1">Click to open the <strong>Differences</strong> tree with the appropriate diagram selected within.</td></tr><tr><td><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchronize_zooming_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></td><td><p>Synchronize Zooming</p></td><td><p>Click to turn on or off the zooming synchronization in all difference viewers of the same diagram.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchronize_scrolling_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></td><td><p>Synchronize Scrolling</p></td><td><p>Click to turn on or off scrolling synchronization in all difference viewers of the same diagram.</p></td></tr><tr><td><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="print_diagram_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Print Diagram</td><td>Click the button to print a diagram. The diagram will be printed with highlighted areas on it, if the highlighting of the diagram changes is turned on.</td></tr><tr><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="zooming_options.png" /></ac:image></td><td>Zooming</td><td>Use the buttons to control the diagram size.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567303 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06614: Virtual entities

- page_id: `249567303`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567303/Virtual+entities
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:26:53.736+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > Entity-relationship (ER) modeling and diagrams
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Virtual entities can be derived from information in other entities. They are marked with the keyword «virtual» on diagrams. They can be handled in the same manner as other entities.

Virtual entities roughly correspond to views in databases.

If you need to specify exactly how virtual entities are derived from other entities, you can use Abstraction relationships from UML. You can specify the derivation expression in the Mapping field.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of virtual entity usage in an ER diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Virtual entities can be derived from information in other entities. They are marked with the keyword «virtual» on diagrams. They can be handled in the same manner as other entities.</p><p>Virtual entities roughly correspond to views in databases.</p><p>If you need to specify exactly how virtual entities are derived from other entities, you can use Abstraction relationships from UML. You can specify the derivation expression in the Mapping field.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 13 Virtual Entity Usage in ER Diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of virtual entity usage in an ER diagram.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567397 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06615: Virtual entity transformation

- page_id: `249567397`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567397/Virtual+entity+transformation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:33:01.948+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > Transformations > ER to SQL (generic / Oracle) transformations
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

919216430

919216432

919216431

Virtual entities of ER models can be transformed into the following different elements of SQL models.

- Tables (just as ordinary, non-virtual entities).
- SQL views (ER to SQL(Oracle) transformation has an additional choice of simple views or materialized views).

The choice is controlled by the **Virtual Entities Transformed To**transformation property.

919216429

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Virtual entities' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transformation properties' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566833 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06616: Vision

- page_id: `249566833`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566833/Vision
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:22:18.337+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Supportive Diagrams Concepts > Business Motivation Diagram > Ends Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487106899

1487106899

1487106899

**Description**

A vision is a state where an organization wants to achieve in the future. It is common that a vision is made up of many aspects rather than concentrated on a specific factor of the business problem. It is the final, possibly unattainable, state the organization would like to accomplish. It does not describe how the organization will achieve the state. A Vision is often compound, rather than focused toward one particular aspect of the business problem. It is supported or made operative by Missions and amplified by Goals.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1487106898

**Related elements**

- Ends Concepts
- End Concept Relationships

**Related diagram**

- Business Motivation Diagram

It is supported or made operative by Missions and amplified by Goals.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="vision.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84ec5aaa-e98d-4ce8-8a64-dd2bfabc60eb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1487106898</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Ends+Concepts">Ends Concepts</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/End+Concept+Relationships">End Concept Relationships</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagram</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Business+Motivation+Diagram">Business Motivation Diagram</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970912 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06617: Web Publisher 2.0 report

- page_id: `243970912`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970912/Web+Publisher+2.0+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:01.530+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

347568349

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

347568351

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

347568350

Web Publisher 2.0 is a Java-Doc-like report template with a clickable navigation and an image map for all diagrams and elements. It allowsyou to publish models (including diagrams) in HTML with rich features, and to open them using your browser, e.g., Microsoft Edge, Opera, or Firefox.

This section describes the features of Web Publisher 2.0, illustrates how to generate a Web Publisher 2.0 report template by using the Magic Library.mdzip sample project, and gives you examples on how to work with each feature.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Web Publisher 2.0 report.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Web Publisher 2.0 report is not fully supported by Google Chrome (unless following the steps below), Opera, and Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based); therefore, the data tree structure in the Containment tree may be missing because they do not allow JavaScript to load local files via **XMLHttpRequest**. You can solve this problem by doing one of the following:

- Deploy Web Publisher 2.0 report on web server, or
- Start Google Chrome with " --allow-file-access-from-files " argument by following the steps below:
  1. Close the web browser (Chrome) instances.
  2. Open the web browser with " --allow-file-access-from-files " argument. 2.1 Right click on web browser launcher and select Properties .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

2.2 Add this argument "**--allow-file-access-from-files**" next to the web browser installation path.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

2.3 Open the web browser instance from the launcher.

2.4 Open the generated Web 2.0 report.

2.5 Start the web browser from the launcher or command line that contains "**--allow-file-access-from-files**" argument before.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Web Publisher 2.0 report is also supported by Firefox. You can configure Firefox before opening Web Publisher 2.0 report by doing the following:

1. In the Firefox address box, type about:config . [ATTACHMENT filename='config.png']
2. Find the security.fileuri.strict_origin_policy parameter and set its parameter value to false .

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can also open Web Publisher 2.0 on Safari by doing the following:

1. Open Safari.
2. On the toolbar of the browser, click Safari and select Preferences . The Preference dialog opens.
3. Click the Advanced tab and select the Show Develop menu in menu bar option. Close the dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='Safari Preference Advanced Dialog.png']
4. On the toolbar of the browser, click Develop and select Disable Local File Restrictions .

Safari is now able to open Web Publisher 2.0.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

In Report Wizard 2021x Refresh 2 and later, **$exporter2 (DOM : Exporter2)** is no longer used, and **$exporter3(XMLStream : ExporterTool3)** is used by default instead.

If you want to continue using Exporter2, use the following line in *index.html* of the Web Publisher 2.0 template:

```text

```

Cameo Collaborator is developed for replacing Web Publisher Collaboration report. This is a web-based solution for publishing and reviewing models online. See more detail at [Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Documentation](https://www.3ds.com/products-services/catia/products/no-magic/cameo-collaborator-for-teamwork-cloud/)

347568348

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
