In the <strong>General</strong> properties, set <strong>Show PV-2 / AcV-2 / NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> to False.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>To create an AcV-2 diagram manually</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>AcV-2 Project Timeliness</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>AcV-2 Project Timeliness</strong> dialog click <strong>Add</strong>.</li><li>Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The blank AcV-2 diagram is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c07458a6-ac7a-43e3-814c-ac3f25c28eaf"><ac:rich-text-body>You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Adding actual projects to AcV-2 diagram</h3><p>You can add a new or an existing actual project to the AcV-2 diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new actual project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the AcV-2 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt;<strong> Actual Project</strong>. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created actual project appears.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the actual project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add a new actual sub project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the actual project for which you want to create an actual sub project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the AcV-2 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Sub Project</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected actual project, click <strong>Add new Actual Sub Project</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Specify a time frame for the actual sub project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing actual project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the AcV-2 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. The <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, select an Actual project (or several actual projects) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h3>Deleting actual projects from AcV-2 diagram</h3><p>To delete an actual project from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an actual project.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete From Chart</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ccf057ea-e981-493b-9cb5-9ade4d266358"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Actual Sub Projects can only be deleted from the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To delete an actual project from the model</p><hr /><p>1. Select an actual project.<br />2. On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete</strong>.</p><h3>Relating actual projects in AcV-2 diagram</h3><p>You can relate actual projects to a sequence using the following relation types:</p><ul><li>Finish to Start. Target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project finishes.</li><li>Start to Start. The target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project starts.</li><li>Finish to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project finishes.</li><li>Start to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project starts.</li></ul><p>You can change actual project dates by moving an actual project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.</p><p><br /></p><p>To relate actual projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two actual projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the available relation types:<ul><li><strong>Finish to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Finish to Finish</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Finish</strong></li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation between actual projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two related actual projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select None.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To resolve the incorrect actual project sequence</p><hr /><ol><li>On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click <strong>Validate</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the suggested solutions.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="relate_in_acv2.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Available validation suggestions</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78e284ce-2000-4dbc-8a6a-9bee4430c047"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change dates of an actual project containing actual sub projects, actual sub project dates change correspondingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Adding Milestones in AcV-2 diagram</h3><p>To add a milestone</p><hr /><p><br /></p><ol><li>Select an actual project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected actual project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kind.</li><li>The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Specify milestone settings in the following wizard steps and click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h3>Deleting milestones in AcV-2 diagram </h3><p>To delete a milestone</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a milestone.</li><li>From the milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Applying actual project status in AcV-2 diagram</h3><p>To apply the actual project status to a new milestone</p><hr /><ol><li>From the milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Apply Status</strong>.</li><li>Specify the actual project status by following steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To edit the actual project status</p><hr /><ol><li>From the milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.</li><li>Edit the actual project status by following the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53d41aa7-6355-4f27-a5aa-33b7aa796a17"><ac:rich-text-body>If two or more milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select <strong>Specify Project Status</strong> and then select the milestone you want to edit the status for.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Removing actual project status in AcV-2 diagram</h3><p>To remove the project status</p><hr /><ol><li>From the milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.</li><li>In the opened <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>, click the Remove project status indicator mark button.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563859 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06634: Working with Architecture Management diagram

- page_id: `249563859`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563859/Working+with+Architecture+Management+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:25.348+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Architecture Management
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Architecture Management diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select any of the Architecture Management view specification packages (except Architecture Management Information) and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Architecture Management . [ATTACHMENT filename='architecture_diagram_new.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Architecture Management and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Service Architecture element, but the same is valid for other available elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Service Architecture (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_viewpoint_element.png']
2. Name the element.

Creating elements from the Containment tree differs from creating them in the diagram. The element availability depends on which Architecture Management view specification package you choose. In this example, the Service Architecture element creation from the Architecture Management Traceability package in the Containment tree is described, but any other element from a different package can be created in the same way.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the Architecture Management Traceability package, and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Service Architecture (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_service_architecture.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Capability Configuration (or another appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Service Architecture(or another appropriate element) created, you can start connecting it to other elements. In this example, the Service Architecture will be connected to the Actual Enterprise Phase using the Implements relationship.

To connect the elements with the Implements relationship

1. Select an element from which the Implements will be drawn.
2. From the diagram palette, choose Implements.
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Search for the Service Architecture </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(or another appropriate element)</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="361" ac:width="291"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_service_architecture.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="272" ac:width="1063"><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_service_architecture.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Capability Configuration</span> (or another appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e95dfc12-8507-4b0f-a54d-55c89892323c"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connect the elements</h3><p><br /></p><p>When you have <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service Architecture</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span>(or another appropriate element) created, you can start connecting it to other elements. In this example, the Service Architecture will be connected to the Actual Enterprise Phase using the Implements relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Implements relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element from which the Implements will be drawn.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the diagram palette, choose Implements.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="345" ac:width="778"><ri:attachment ri:filename="connect_elements_implements.png" /></ac:image></span></p></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966028 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06635: Working with attached files

- page_id: `243966028`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966028/Working+with+attached+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:26.784+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Projects with file attachments
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The actions you can perform with attached files are listed below:

Attached files can also be included in reports. The attached file is saved in the file system together with the generated report. In this report, any hyperlink to that attached file defined in the model is replaced with reference to the appropriate file in the file system.

##### Modifying the contents

Attached files can be opened directly from the modeling tool and reviewed or modified with appropriate programs (documents – with MS Word, worksheets – with MS Excel, HTML files – with any HTML editor).

To open an attached file, do one of the following

- In the Containment tree, double-click the attached file.
- In the Containment tree, right-click the attached file and select Open File .
- Double-click either the element that has an active hyperlink to the attached file, or the shape of that element (see the following figure).

The file is opened in the appropriate program in your operating system. You can review or modify it.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
After closing the modified file, you can choose whether to save it in the modeling tool or not. Saving the latest version of the file in the modeling tool updates the **Modified** **At** property value of the appropriate Attached File element.

If the attached file is read-only, the changes made to it cannot be saved. To allow modifying the file, do one of the following:

- If the file is attached to used project, change the accessibility mode of this used project from read-only to read-write .
- If the file is attached to server project, lock the Attached File element for edit.

##### Inserting into HTML text

Attached image files can be inserted into HTML texts and displayed on diagrams. 
 
To insert an attached image file into HTML text

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the attached image file and select Copy Image URL .
2. On a diagram pane, draw a text box with the HTML mode on.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Advanced HTML Editor dialog' space='']Open the **Advanced HTML Editor** dialog .
4. On the toolbar, click the Insert image button and select either:
  - From File button, then paste the URL into the Picture source field.
  - **From Attached File**button, then find and select the attached image file in the list. If you are working with a server project, make sure that the path to the image location is accessible from any computer that has Internet connection. Otherwise, the image won't be displayed, when the project is opened on another computer.
5. Click OK twice to close both the Picture Properties and Advanced HTML Editor dialogs.

The inserted image appears on the diagram. Now if you transfer the project to another computer, the diagram continues displaying the image. You can also insert images that are not yet attached to the project model. Read more about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Inserting images into HTML text' space=''][.](http://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Inserting+an+image+into+HTML+text)

##### Exporting back to the local file system

To export the attached file back to the local file system

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the attached file and select**Save File As** .
2. Select the location in the local file system and click **Save**.

The file is saved in the local file system.

##### Creating image shapes

You can use attached imagefiles (*.gif*, *.jpg*, *.jpeg*, *.svg*, or *.png*) to create image shapes on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''].

To create an image shape using an attached image file

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] select an already attached file or attach a new one. [How to attach a file in the Containment tree >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attaching%252C+reattaching%252C+and+detaching+files#Attaching,reattaching,anddetachingfiles-Attachingfiles) Image formatsYou can attach images of the following formats: *.gif*, *.jpg*, *.jpeg*, *.svg*, or *.png*.
2. Drag the attached file element to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''].The image shape is created. Image does not updateImage shape is not synchronized with attached file. If you replace an image in the attached file, the image shape on the diagram pane will not update.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The actions you can perform with attached files are listed below:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff2ea14a-5e49-4318-87a0-9ad533580594" />    </p><p>Attached files can also be included in reports. The attached file is saved in the file system together with the generated report. In this report, any hyperlink to that attached file defined in the model is replaced with reference to the appropriate file in the file system.</p><h3>Modifying the contents</h3><p>Attached files can be opened directly from the modeling tool and reviewed or modified with appropriate programs (documents – with MS Word, worksheets – with MS Excel, HTML files – with any HTML editor).</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To open an attached file, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Containment tree, double-click the attached file.</li><li>In the Containment tree, right-click the attached file and select <strong>Open File</strong>.</li><li>Double-click either the element that has an active hyperlink to the attached file, or the shape of that element (see the following figure).</li></ul><p>The file is opened in the appropriate program in your operating system. You can review or modify it.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:title="Element (left) and its shape (right) with active hyperlink to the attached file" ac:alt="Element (left) and its shape (right) with active hyperlink to the attached file" ac:height="68" ac:width="454"><ri:attachment ri:filename="active_hyperlink_to_attached_file.png" /></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />After closing the modified file, you can choose whether to save it in the modeling tool or not. Saving the latest version of the file in the modeling tool updates the <strong>Modified</strong> <strong>At</strong> property value of the appropriate Attached File element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05c871af-43a3-44e4-a88e-0ccb95217dcc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the attached file is read-only, the changes made to it cannot be saved. To allow modifying the file, do one of the following:</p><ul><li>If the file is attached to used project, change the accessibility mode of this used project from <em>read-only</em> to <em>read-write</em>.</li><li>If the file is attached to server project, lock the Attached File element for edit.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Inserting into HTML text</h3><p>Attached image files can be inserted into HTML texts and displayed on diagrams.<br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To insert an attached image file into HTML text</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right-click the attached image file and select <strong>Copy Image URL</strong>.</li><li>On a diagram pane, draw a text box with the HTML mode on.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Advanced HTML Editor dialog" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Advanced HTML Editor</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>On the toolbar, click the <strong>Insert image</strong> button and select either:<br /><br /><ul><li><strong>From File</strong> button, then paste the URL into the <strong>Picture source</strong> field.</li><li><p><strong>From Attached File </strong>button, then find and select the attached image file in the list.<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d581ff2-2ef7-4a34-923b-c4475947f5df"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you are working with a server project, make sure that the path to the image location is accessible from any computer that has Internet connection. Otherwise, the image won't be displayed, when the project is opened on another computer.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> twice to close both the <strong>Picture Properties</strong> and <strong>Advanced HTML Editor</strong> dialogs.</li></ol><p>The inserted image appears on the diagram. Now if you transfer the project to another computer, the diagram continues displaying the image. You can also insert images that are not yet attached to the project model. Read more about <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Inserting images into HTML text" /></ac:link><a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Inserting+an+image+into+HTML+text" style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</a></p><h3>Exporting back to the local file system</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To export the attached file back to the local file system</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the Containment tree, right-click the attached file and select<strong> Save File As</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select the location in the local file system and click <strong>Save</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The file is saved in the local file system.</span></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Creating image shapes</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use attached <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">image </span>files (<em>.gif</em>, <em>.jpg</em>, <em>.jpeg</em>, <em>.svg</em>, or <em>.png</em>) to create image shapes on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create an image shape using an attached image file<br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> select an already attached file or attach a new one. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attaching%252C+reattaching%252C+and+detaching+files#Attaching,reattaching,anddetachingfiles-Attachingfiles">How to attach a file in the Containment tree &gt;&gt;</a><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b358091b-36ab-4b19-bf3e-c86561895db6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Image <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968733 space=MT version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 06636: Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard

- page_id: `243968733`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968733/Working+with+Automatic+Instantiation+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:29:00.173+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Automatic Instantiation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

907302760

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

907302781

907302758

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

907302771

You can create several different sets of instances using the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard. You can add each set to a different package and display it on a newly created diagram.

Depending on the size of your system, a very large number of instance specifications can be created using the wizard. That is why we strongly recommend selecting a new package wherein all created instance specifications will be stored.

##### Step #1. Selecting parts to be instantiated

In this step, you can see all the available instance specification values (parts) of your selected element. At this point, you can select which parts or properties to instantiate, create or select values for parts and properties, as well as add or remove parallel parts.

You can select/deselect the parts by clicking the check marks next to the parts or the options in the Selection area.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Parts are displayed if the multiplicity is greater than 1. The number of parts displayed depends on the multiplicity.

- If the multiplicity is a particular value, for example 2, then you will see 2 parts displayed in the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard. Also, in this case, you will not be able to add parallel parts or remove existing parallel parts.
- If the multiplicity is defined as an interval (for example, 0..*, 1..10, 1..* ) or *, only one part will be created by default. If you need more parts, you can create them by pressing **Insert** or selecting **Add parallel part** from the selected property’s shortcut menu.
- If the multiplicity is 1, parts will not be created.

The parts and properties are displayed with the suggested types, names, and values. In this step of the wizard, you can change them. You can also change the type for the part only if there is an available sub-element for that part in your model (see the Case study).

**Selecting a value for an instance**

To select a value for an instance

1. Select a part to be instantiated to which you want to add a value.
2. In **Instantiated properties**, click a **Value** specification cell and then click the ... button.
3. The **Select Instance Specification** dialog opens.
4. In the opened dialog, select the instance specification you want to add for the selected instance.
5. Click the **OK** button.

To create a value for an instance

1. Select a part to be instantiated for which you want to create a value.
2. In **Instantiated properties**, click the **Value** specification cell and then click the **Show Shortcut Menu** button. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the opened shortcut menu, point to the **Value Specification** and then select one of the available value types.
4. In the **Value property** specification cell, type the desired value. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Changing the name of an instance**

Each instance has a defaultname that uses the dot notation. However, you can change the default name by clicking the value row next to the **Name** property and typing the new name for the instance. ****

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] ****

**Applying a custom stereotypeto an instance**

You can also apply the custom stereotype to the instance. Only those stereotypes can be applied that have *Instance Specification* as their metaclass and whose customizations have a meta property named *classifier*with the **New Types** property value set to the instantiated element’s type.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The custom «ControllerFMU» and «ControllerSTM» stereotypes have *Instance Specification* as their metaclass 
and their respective customizations *ControllerFMU Customization* and *ControllerSTM Customization* have a meta property named classifier with the **New Types** property value set to the «Controller» stereotype. 
Since the *Controller* class has the «Controller» stereotype applied, the «ControllerFMU» and «ControllerSTM» stereotypes can be applied to the *Controller* class's instance.

**Changing a type for an instance**

You can change the type for the property's instance only if that type is a subtype of a Class (connected throughGeneralization). As shown in the following figure, the *Engine GM* Class and *Engine GMR C*lass are subtypes of the *Engine*Class. When creating an instance for the *Vehicle* Class, you can change the type of the *engine*Property by selecting one of the following: *Engine GM* Class, *Engine GMR* Class, or *Engine*Class.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changing the type for a Part Property instance directly in the Automatic Instantiation dialog.

##### Step #2. Selecting a package

In this step, you can select the package to store instance specifications. You can select anexisting package from the element tree, create a new package, or clone selectedpackages. Click the **Finish** buttonafter this step if you do not want to display your created instance specifications.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a new Package in the element tree

1. In the element tree, select the Package for which you want to create the new one.
2. Click the **Creation Mode** button.
3. Click the **Create Owner** button.
4. From the open menu, select the package kind. The Specification window of that package opens.
5. Type its name.
6. Click **Close**.

To clone the Packages in the element tree

1. In the element tree, select the Package to clone. The Package is cloned recursively including all nested structures.
2. Click the **Clone** button.
3. The Specification window of cloned Package opens.
4. Type its name.
5. Click **Close**.

##### Step #3. Creating a diagram (optional)

In this step, you can choose whether you want to show your created instance specifications in a new diagram or not. If you do not wish to create a new diagram, you can simply skip this step.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a diagram using **Automatic Instantiation** Wizard

1. If you choose to create a new diagram for displaying instance specification(s), select the **Create a new diagram**check box.
2. If you wish to create and display links between your instance specification(s), select the **Create link between instances**check box.
3. Fill in the **Type diagram name**field by typing the diagram name. NoteBy default, the diagram name is the selected Package's name in Step 2.
4. From the **Select diagram type** drop-down list, you can select one of the diagram types.
5. Specify the owner for a new diagram:
  - Select existing Packages from the tree.
  - Click the **Create Owner** button to create a new one.
  - Click the **Clone** button to clone existing Packages.
6. After clicking the **Finish** button, a new diagram with instance specifications is created and displayed in your project.

907308985

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563411 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06637: Working with C1 Capability Taxonomy Table

- page_id: `249563411`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563411/Working+with+C1+Capability+Taxonomy+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:54.514+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C1 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Capability Taxonomy Table (C1)** provides all the capabilities referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document to develop high-level use cases and user requirements.

To add a new Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button. A new row is added.
2. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.

To add an existing Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table

1. In the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose the desired Capabilities.
3. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Capability Taxonomy Table (C1)</strong> provides all the capabilities referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document to develop high-level use cases and user requirements.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button. A new row is added.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose the desired Capabilities.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966831 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06638: Working with cells

- page_id: `243966831`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966831/Working+with+cells
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:09.946+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Data types

1343270176

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Data types

1343270178

1343270175

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Data types

1343270177

This section explains how to work with cells in the table: selection, entering and clearing values, opening an element's Specification window, and navigation.

##### selectingSelecting cells

In all tables, you can select:

- 
- Multiple cells.
- Entire table.

singleTo select a single cell

1. Press the left mouse button on the table.

multipleTo select multiple cells, do one of the following

- Press the left mouse button and drag a mouse pointer.
- Press Shift and use the left mouse button or any of the arrow keys from the keyboard.

entireTo select an entire table

1. Right-click the column # header.
2. From the shortcut menu, select the Select command. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_table.png']

##### Entering values into a cell

Individual cells in the table cannot be edited if the text is highlighted in grey. This means the cell contains a predefined value and is non-editable.

All changes made in the table are saved in the model. The procedure below describes how to enter a value into an editable cell.

To enter a value into a cell

1. Double-click a cell you want to edit.
2. One of the following actions is available, depending on the property type of the column in which the selected cell is found: - Type the value directly in the selected cell. - Click the Edit button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space=''] opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='gt_edit_cell.png'] - Click the Add (+) to specify a value. - Select the value from the drop down list.
3. In the opened property value editor specify required properties. The value is saved in the model.

##### Opening the Specification window of an element from a cell

The property can be edited in the element’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] if it is not locked.

To open the element’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] from a cell

Do either:

- If the cell is non-editable, double-click it.
- If the cell is editable, right-click on a cell, and from the shortcut menu select Specification. Then: [ATTACHMENT filename='gt_open_specification_submenu.png']
  - If the selected cell does not refer to any element, the Specification window of the element in the row will open.
  - If the selected cell refers to more than one element, the submenu with the list of appropriate elements will open. Choose the element whose [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] you want to open.

##### Clearing values from cells

You can clear values from a single or multiple cells in cells containing the following data types: String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration. You can clear the whole table, column, or row.

#### WARNING: Data types

Data types

Only String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types can be cleared from a cell.

The procedures below describe how to clear values from individual cells, from a block of cells, or from the entire table:

- 
- 

clear from cellsTo clear values from cells

1. Select single or multiple cells in the table. ]]>
2. Do one of the following: Selected cells of String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types become empty. The following example demonstrates that the Documentation cell is empty after clearing because its data type is a String. The Is Abstract cell value also is cleared because the value type is Boolean. [ATTACHMENT filename='cleared_cells.png']
  - From the shortcut menu, select the Clear command (see the following figure).
  - Press Delete from keyboard (see the following figure). [ATTACHMENT filename='clear_cell.png']

clear from tableTo clear values from an entire table

1. Select the entire table. ]]>
2. Do one of the following: - From the table shortcut menu, click the Clear command. - Press Delete on the keyboard.

##### Navigation between cells

Navigate between cells using the arrow keys from the keyboard and keyboard shortcuts.

| Press | To move... |
| --- | --- |
| Right Arrow key | One cell to the right. |
| Left Arrow key | One cell to the left. |
| Down Arrow key | One cell down. |
| Up Arrow key | One cell up. |
| Tab | One cell to the right. |
| Shift+Tab | To the previous cell. |
| Enter | To the cell below. |
| Shift+Enter | To the cell above. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8d39acf-06a8-4f1e-9eaa-b7a2b3db5e73"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1343270176</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea8c2eb6-c6a8-4414-835f-7dd5095aec47"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1343270178</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0852f4c1-340b-4aab-ac5d-9b02099da747"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1343270175</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9aaa06c4-676d-4a17-ac76-6dfe6ba0e617"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4411da9-78df-4d58-9031-a83342714a7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1343270177</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This section explains how to work with cells in the table: selection, entering and clearing values, opening an element's Specification window, and navigation.</p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac504504-91a7-47f7-b7b5-e74efac56144"><ac:parameter ac:name="">selecting</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Selecting cells</h3><p>In all tables, you can select:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="single"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Single cell.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link ac:anchor="multiple"><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Multiple cells</span>.</ac:link-body></ac:link></p></li><li><p><ac:link ac:anchor="entire"><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Entire table</span>.</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p></li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c5a6318-69c6-4152-80b8-fc47b1ff5eb0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">single</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To select a single cell</p><hr /><ol><li>Press the left mouse button on the table.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7af43c4-cea2-4c87-b592-2582c4fc6ae7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">multiple</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To select multiple cells, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Press the left mouse button and drag a mouse pointer.</li><li>Press Shift and use the left mouse button or any of the arrow keys from the keyboard.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="434418cb-8d9e-473b-9405-ddea682563bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">entire</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To select an entire table</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the column # header.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Select</strong> command.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_table.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Entering values into a cell</h3><p>Individual cells in the table cannot be edited if the text is highlighted in grey. This means the cell contains a predefined value and is non-editable.</p><p>All changes made in the table are saved in the model. The procedure below describes how to enter a value into an editable cell.</p><p><br /></p><p>To enter a value into a cell</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a cell you want to edit.</li><li>One of the following actions is available, depending on the property type of the column in which the selected cell is found:<br />- Type the value directly in the selected cell.<br />- Click the <strong>Edit</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing property values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[property value editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_edit_cell.png" /></ac:image><br />- Click the <strong>Add</strong> (+) to specify a value.<br />- Select the value from the drop down list.</li><li>In the opened property value editor specify required properties.<br />The value is saved in the model.</li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p><h3>Opening the Specification window of an element from a cell</h3><p>The property can be edited in the element’s <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> if it is not locked.</p><p>To open the element’s <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> from a cell</p><hr /><p>Do either:</p><ul><li>If the cell is non-editable, double-click it.</li><li>If the cell is editable, right-click on a cell, and from the shortcut menu select Specification. Then:<br /><ul><li>If the selected cell does not refer to any element, the Specification window of the element in the row will open.</li><li>If the selected cell refers to more than one element, the submenu with the list of appropriate elements will open. Choose the element whose <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> you want to open.</li></ul><ac:image ac:title="Submenu of Open Specification command" ac:alt="Submenu of Open Specification command"><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_open_specification_submenu.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><h3><span>Clearing values from cells</span></h3><p>You can clear values from a single or multiple cells in cells containing the following data types: String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration. You can clear the whole table, column, or row.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2971b0bd-dbbf-4154-8b9f-be21f40ee826"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Data types</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types can be cleared from a cell.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The procedures below describe how to clear values from individual cells, from a block of cells, or from the entire table:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="clear from cells"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to clear values from cells]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="clear from table"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to values from the entire table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a50da066-96e1-45af-9dff-8f75d3e8b2c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">clear from cells</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To clear values from cells</p><hr /><ol><li>Select single or multiple cells in the table. <ac:link ac:anchor="selecting"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to select cells in the table >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target">From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Clear</strong> command (see the following figure).</li><li>Press <strong>Delete</strong> from keyboard (see the following figure).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="clear_cell.png" /></ac:image></li></ul>Selected cells of String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types become empty. The following example demonstrates that the <em>Documentation</em> cell is empty after clearing because its data type is a String. The <em>Is Abstract</em> cell value also is cleared because the value type is Boolean.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cleared_cells.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3fe9a746-e02d-4fbb-a87e-0ae3e0ac5122"><ac:parameter ac:name="">clear from table</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To clear values from an entire table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the entire table. <ac:link ac:anchor="selecting"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to select cells in table >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Do one of the following:<br /> - From the table shortcut menu, click the <strong>Clear</strong> command.<br /> - Press Delete on the keyboard.</li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span><br /></span></p><h3><span>Navigation between cells</span></h3><p>Navigate between cells using the arrow keys from the keyboard and keyboard shortcuts.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Press</th><th>To move...</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Right Arrow key</td><td colspan="1">One cell to the right.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Left Arrow key</td><td colspan="1">One cell to the left.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Down Arrow key</td><td colspan="1">One cell down.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Up Arrow key</td><td colspan="1">One cell up.</td></tr><tr><td>Tab</td><td>One cell to the right.</td></tr><tr><td>Shift+Tab</td><td>To the previous cell.</td></tr><tr><td>Enter</td><td>To the cell below.</td></tr><tr><td>Shift+Enter</td><td>To the cell above.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966837 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06639: Working with columns

- page_id: `243966837`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966837/Working+with+columns
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:10.518+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Additional information

1344888677

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Additional information

1344888679

1344888676

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Additional information

1344888678

This section shows how to work with columns in the table: selection, clearing values, hiding, reordering, adding, and removing columns.

##### Selecting an entire column

To select an entire column

1. Right-click the column header.
2. From the shortcut menu, click the Select command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Clearing values from an entire column

#### WARNING: Data types

Data types

Only String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types can be cleared from a cell.

To clear values from an entire column

1. Right-click the column header.
2. Do one of the following: - From the shortcut menu, click the Clear command. - Press Delete from the keyboard.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Hiding a column

To hide a column

1. Select single or multiple cells, and from the shortcut menu, select Column > Hide .
2. Right-click the column header to open the shortcut menu, and select the Hide command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Reordering columns

To reorder columns

- Click the column header and drag it to the desired place. Only the first column cannot be moved.

##### Adding and removing columns

To add or remove columns

1. To open the available columns list: Using the **Select Columns** dialog, you can choose to display the properties of the specified element type, as well as the properties that are subtypes of the specified element type.
  - On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space=''] , click the Columns to open the menu with common properties corresponding to element types that have been selected for the table.
  - On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space=''] , click Columns > Select Columns to open the dialog with all properties, including tags of stereotypes corresponding to selected element types.
2. Do one of the following:

- 
  - Select the properties you want to see as columns in the table.
  - Unselect the properties you do not want to see as columns in the table.

##### Adding and removing Custom Columns

Custom Columns allow you to create structured expressions the results of which are only visible in the table for which the Custom Column is created. If you prefer to create an expression that can be reusable in multiple tables, matrices, maps, and other locations where structured expressions are accepted, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom traceability rules' space=''] instead.

To add Custom Columns

1. In the toolbar, click **Show Columns**/**Columns** > **New Custom Column**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. In the **Expression** dialog, specify criteria for the Custom Column, and click **OK**.

To remove Custom Columns

1. Right-click the Custom Column header you want to remove.
2. From the shortcut menu, select the Remove Custom Column command. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_custom_column.png']

##### Specifying Custom Columns

When you create a new Custom Column, you need to define the expression in the **Expression** dialog. Keep in mind:

- The Custom Column type and multiplicity (conveyed via the Single Value check box) are set automatically according to the specified expression once it is created or edited. You can also change them manually, however, they will only be applied if you make no modifications to the expression itself. [ATTACHMENT filename='derived_property_type_and_multiplicity_img.png']
- The root union of a Custom Column structured expression has the Unique check box which you can select to have only one identical element count as the result displayed in the table column cell. By default, the check box is selected. [ATTACHMENT filename='unique_ordered_options.png']

#### INFO: Additional information

Additional information

- To learn more about specifying expressions, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''] page.
- You can also select to show implied relationships in the table. The procedure for specifying which relationships to display in the table is the same as described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting criteria' space=''] .

##### Editing Custom Columns

To edit a Custom Column

1. In a table, right-click the header of the desired Custom Column and select Edit Custom Column .
2. In the Expression dialog, make desired changes and click OK .

##### Setting detailed column names

Column names in the table header are set automatically. They are element property names. You cannot change column names. If an element has some properties with the same name (for example, in associations or if a tag of stereotypes is selected as a column), detailed column names could provide more precise information.

To see detailed column names

- On the Options toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] and then select Show Detailed Column Name . The group name (between brackets) to which the selected property belongs, and/or the stereotype name (just before the property name), will be displayed in the column heading.

The following diagram shows three different columns with the same name for the association element: **Name**, **Name** (Role of A), and **Name** (Role of B), and a column for the class element with the stereotype «Teacher». **Name** (Tags). If the command **Show Detailed Column Name** were not selected, there would be four columns with the same headings, i.e., **Name**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

column header and drag it to the desired place. Only the first column cannot be moved.</li></ul><h3>Adding and removing columns</h3><p>To add or remove columns</p><hr /><ol><li>To open the available columns list:<br /><ul><li>On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click the <strong>Columns</strong> to open the menu with common properties corresponding to element types that have been selected for the table.</li><li>On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <strong>Columns </strong>&gt; <strong>Select Columns</strong> to open the dialog with all properties, including tags of stereotypes corresponding to selected element types.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82cb1a88-0985-43e5-b5a3-13effb53e27a"><ac:rich-text-body><p> Using the <strong>Select Columns</strong> dialog, you can choose to display the properties of the specified element type, as well as the properties that are subtypes of the specified element type.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Do one of the following:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Select the properties you want to see as columns in the table.</li><li>Unselect the properties you do not want to see as columns in the table.</li></ul></li></ul><h3>Adding and removing Custom Columns</h3><p>Custom Columns allow you to create structured expressions the results of which are only visible in the table for which the Custom Column is created. If you prefer to create an expression that can be reusable in multiple tables, matrices, maps, and other locations where structured expressions are accepted, you need to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom traceability rules" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a Derived Property]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> instead.  </p><p>To add Custom Columns</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the toolbar, click <strong>Show Columns</strong>/<strong>Columns</strong> &gt; <strong>New Custom Column</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="206" ac:width="263"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_custom_column.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Expression</strong> dialog, specify criteria for the Custom Column, and click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>To remove Custom Columns</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Custom Column header you want to remove.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Remove Custom Column </strong>command.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="73" ac:width="211"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_custom_column.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Specifying Custom Columns</h3><p>When you create a new Custom Column, you need to define the expression in the <strong>Expression</strong> dialog. Keep in mind:</p><ul><li>The Custom Column <strong>type</strong> and <strong>multiplicity</strong> (conveyed via the <strong>Single Value</strong> check box) are set automatically according to the specified expression once it is created or edited. You can also change them manually, however, they will only be applied if you make no modifications to the expression itself.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="derived_property_type_and_multiplicity_img.png" /></ac:image></li><li>The root union of a Custom Column structured expression has the <strong>Unique</strong> check box which you can select to have only one identical element count as the result displayed in the table column cell. By default, the check box is selected.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="unique_ordered_options.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62275b61-1db5-4461-b210-61eb3e15d88c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Additional information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li> To learn more about specifying expressions, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /></ac:link> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">page.</span></li><li> You can also select to show implied relationships in the table. The procedure for specifying which relationships to display in the table is the same as described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting criteria" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specifying dependency criterion]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 style="text-align: left;">Editing Custom Columns</h3><p>To edit a Custom Column</p><hr /><ol><li>In a table, right-click the header of the desired Custom Column and select <strong>Edit Custom Column</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Expression</strong> dialog, make desired changes and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Setting detailed column names</h3><p>Column names in the table header are set automatically. They are element property names. You cannot change column names. If an element has some properties with the same name (for example, in associations or if a tag of stereotypes is selected as a column), detailed column names could provide more precise information.</p><p><br />To see detailed column names</p><hr /><ul><li>On the Options toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png" /></ac:image> and then select <strong>Show Detailed Column Name</strong>.<br />The group name (between brackets) to which the selected property belongs, and/or the stereotype name (just before the property name), will be displayed in the column heading.</li></ul><p>The following diagram shows three different columns with the same name for the association element: <strong>Name</strong>, <strong>Name</strong> (Role of A), and <strong>Name</strong> (Role of B), and a column for the class element with the stereotype «Teacher». <strong>Name</strong> (Tags). If the command <strong>Show Detailed Column Name</strong> were not selected, there would be four columns with the same headings, i.e., <strong>Name</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of detailed column names in generic tableExample of detailed column names in generic table" ac:alt="Example of detailed column names in generic tableExample of detailed column names in generic table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_column_names.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565401 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06640: Working with conjugated Interfaces

- page_id: `249565401`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565401/Working+with+conjugated+Interfaces
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:39.539+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The <viewpoint name>*Conjugated Interface (~Interface) is a specialization ofInterface that has the same features as its original Interface except that itsFlow Propertiesare reversed (conjugated).

To create a Conjugated <viewpoint name>* Interface

- Right-click one or more interfaces and select Tools > Create Conjugated <viewpoint name>*Interface . The Conjugated <viewpoint name>* Interface is created with the same Flow Properties as the original but with opposite directions.

As shown in the figure below, the *out**/in*Resource Interface is conjugated to ~in*/out* which has the same*test results and date*Flow Properties but in opposite directions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

*******<viewpoint name>** - Operational, Resource, Service, Resource Service

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Conjugated Interface (~Interface) is a specialization of<span> </span></span>Interface <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">that has the same features as its original Interface except that its</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span></span>Flow Properties<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>are reversed (conjugated). </span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Conjugated <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;*</span> Interface</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click one or more interfaces and select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Conjugated <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Interface</strong>.<br />The<span> </span>Conjugated <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Interface is created with the same Flow Properties as the original but with opposite directions. </li></ul><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">As shown in the figure below, the <em>out</em></span><em>/in</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> Resource Interface is conjugated to ~in<em>/out</em> which has the same </span><em>test results and date </em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Flow Properties but in opposite directions. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="conjugated_interface_example.png" /></ac:image></span></p><hr /><p><strong>* </strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;</strong> - Operational, Resource, Service, Resource Service</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562514 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06641: Working with CV-5 typical table

- page_id: `249562514`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562514/Working+with+CV-5+typical+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:47.009+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

627926007

627926020

627931615

**On this page**

627926010

##### Creating CV-5 typical table

The context of the CV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-1 diagram' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the CV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the CV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the CV-5 typical table table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a CV-5 typical table

To create an empty CV-5 typical table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a CV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) table.
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. An***Exhibits***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a CV-5 typical table

To remove Capabilities from a CV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more CV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Exhibits***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a CV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a CV-5 typical table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a CV-5 typical table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

627926006

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563870 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06642: Working with Date and Time Summary Table

- page_id: `249563870`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563870/Working+with+Date+and+Time+Summary+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:25.651+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Summary and Overview
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1726920497

1726920518

1726920508

The **Date and Time Summary Table (Sm-Ov)** depicts all dates and times specified in the ISO8601 date-time format.

Adding the date-time elements into the table, you can see their defined values, owners, and the list of the elements in which the date-time elements are used as tagged values.

To add existing model elements

1. In the table toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select ISO8601 Date Time dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, choose which date-time elements you need to add to the table. If you need to add more than one element, click the Multiple Selection button.
3. Click **OK** when you are done.

The selected date-time elements are added as rows.

1726920495

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2f84e27-9737-4e06-bc96-c0c095bfc824"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1726920497</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="734ce300-ae23-4361-82a6-4c5918666512"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1726920518</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8ea68af-f4e8-496f-ba0d-9b3d65d5df7d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1726920508</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Date and Time Summary Table (Sm-Ov)</strong> depicts all dates and times specified in the ISO8601 date-time format.</p><p>Adding the date-time elements into the table, you can see their defined values, owners, and the list of the elements in which the date-time elements are used as tagged values.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add existing model elements</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. The <strong>Select ISO8601 Date Time</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the dialog, choose which date-time elements you need to add to the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b7b60e6-1344-45fe-bb0b-299147c13860"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to add more than one element, click the Multiple Selection button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p></li></ol><p>The selected date-time elements are added as rows.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8290fc10-3a7c-4366-8b93-3f90c517d309"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1726920495</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563841 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06643: Working with Dictionary diagram

- page_id: `249563841`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563841/Working+with+Dictionary+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:00.883+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Architecture Management > Architecture Management Information
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Dictionary (Am-If)** provides definitions of all terms used throughout the architectural data.

To create a new or add an existing term

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button.
2. In the Select Elements dialog, choose the elements which represent the terms.
3. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Dictionary (Am-If)</strong> provides definitions of all terms used throughout the architectural data.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a new or add an existing term</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog, choose the elements which represent the terms.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965893 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06644: Working with Enterprise Architect Import

- page_id: `243965893`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965893/Working+with+Enterprise+Architect+Import
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:48.462+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

479168392

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

100.00002%

479168395

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

479168394

Enterprise Architect Import (EA) will automatically load when you start a program. You can use it to import UML 2.1 XMI 2.1 to your modeling tool.

To import Enterprise Architect UML XMI file

1. On the main menu of the modeling tool, click File > Import From > Enterprise Architect UML2.1 XMI 2.1 File . The Import Enterprise Architect Files dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='Import Enterprise Architect Files.png']
2. In the dialog, you must specify:
  - The input file - the XMI file exported from Enterprise Architecture NoteEnterprise Architect Import supports the EA XMI files exported with the option specified as: XMI Type = **UML2.1(XMI 2.1).**
  - The output file name
  - A conversion option
3. Click Import to import the file.

The following table describes conversion options:

| Conversion Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Switch aggregation side | To configure the aggregation switch-side. This option is recommended for XMI files exported from EA 7.1. |
| Exclude EA specific data | To exclude all EA-specific data from being imported (see Transforming EA Specific Data to see a list of EA specific-data that can be transformed into UML elements with stereotypes). |
| Open project after conversion | To load the output project file once the conversion process has been completed. |
| Generate conversion log | To generate a conversion log and save it in the same folder as the output file. The same conversion information will also be displayed on the Notification Window.NoteYou can open the window by pressing Ctrl + M. |

479168391

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can use it to import UML 2.1 XMI 2.1 to your modeling tool.</p><p><br /></p><p>To import Enterprise Architect UML XMI file</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu of the modeling tool, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Enterprise Architect UML2.1 XMI 2.1 File</strong>.
