<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564798 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06645: Working with Environment diagram

- page_id: `249564798`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564798/Working+with+Environment+diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-27T14:56:20.040+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Parameters Pm
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Environment** diagram shows the elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept, or set of systems.

The Environment diagram can be created under the Parameter package of each domain (e.g Operational Parameters, Strategic Parameters)

##### Creating a diagram

An example is shown with the Operational Parameters, but the Environment diagram can be created under other domain Parameters packages also.

To create an Environment diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Parameters and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Environment**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Environment**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Location element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create a Location in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Location and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Location in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Operational Structure (or Taxonomy, Connectivity, Constraints) package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Location and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_location.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connect the Location

When you have the Location created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].
2. Make sure that **All** is chosen for **Properties**.
3. Search for **Is Abstract** and set it to *true*.

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Condition' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Environment' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Location' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Property Set' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resource' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Asset' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Condition' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment Property' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Geo Political Extent Type' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Location' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Location Holder' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Performer' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565404 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06646: Working with exchanges

- page_id: `249565404`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565404/Working+with+exchanges
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:47.430+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

3

##### Showing or hiding realized <viewpoint name>* Exchanges

You can choose to either show or hide the conveyed<viewpoint name>*Exchange Item on the element realizing<viewpoint name>*Exchanges.

To show/hide the realized <viewpoint name>*Exchange Items

1. On the diagram, click the element for which you want to show or hide the realized exchange.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulator toolbar' space=''] :
  1. If the<viewpoint name>* Exchange Item is not displayed , the tooltip for the button is [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Show Realized <viewpoint name>*Exchanges . Click it to hide the <viewpoint name>*ExchangeItem.
  2. If the <viewpoint name>*ExchangeItem is displayed , the tooltip for the button is[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Hide Realized<viewpoint name>***Exchanges** . Click it to hide the <viewpoint name>*ExchangeItem. 
If theelement realizes only one <viewpoint name>*Exchange, the <viewpoint name>*ExchangeItem is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.
3. If the element realizes multiple <viewpoint name>* Exchanges, the <viewpoint name>* Exchange Items dialog opens. In the dialog, do the following:
  1. Select the check boxes for the <viewpoint name>* Exchanges whose <viewpoint name>* Exchange Item you want to be displayed.
  2. Clear the check boxes for the <viewpoint name>*Exchanges whose <viewpoint name>*ExchangeItem you want to be hidden. To select all check boxes, click the**Select All**button. To clear all check boxes, click the**Clear All**button.
  3. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='show_operational_exchanges.png']

When you hide the realized exchanges, you can choose whether you want to hide thearrow notation on the realizing elements. The**Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space=''] controls the arrow visibility.

To change the arrow notation visibility on realizing element when the Exchange Item is hidden or unspecified

1. From the modeling tool main menu, choose **Options**> **Project**. The **Project Options** dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, go to **General**> **Diagrams.**
3. In the **Diagrams**property group, find the **Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing** option and set it to true.
4. Click **OK** when you are done.

##### Dealing with Exchanges after Reconnecting Connectors

When you need to reconnect the connector from one element to another, there are several possible options regarding the exchanges:

- None (default)
- Create New <viewpoint name>* Exchange
- Realize Compatible <viewpoint name>* Exchanges
- Update End of <viewpoint name>* Exchange
- Remove Incompatible <viewpoint name>* Exchanges

###### None

Choosing this option, the incompatible exchanges where source and target does not comply with new source and target, will be removed. This option is default.

###### Create New <viewpoint name>* Exchange

When you reconnect theconnectorto a different element, a shortcut menu opens. In the shortcut menu, you can select to create a new <viewpoint name>* Exchange. The**Realized <viewpoint name>* Exchange** wizardsopens where you can create a new Exchange to be realized instead of the previously realized exchanges.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Realize Compatible <viewpoint name>* Exchanges

When you reconnect the connector to a different element, a shortcut menu opens. In this shortcut menu, you can select to realize the exchanges, which exist between the sender and the new receiver. If the connector links ports, the conveyed items of the resulting exchanges would be ensured to adhere the flow properties on both ends of the connector.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Update End of <viewpoint name>* Exchange

Choosing this option, the end of the exchange will be updated to match the new sender or new receiver on the reconnected connector's end.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Remove Incompatible <viewpoint name>* Exchanges

Visible only when the sender and receiver are not changed after reconnection, but the exchanges that were already realized become incompatible, meaning they no longer comply with the new flow properties on both ends of the connector.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

*** <viewpoint name>**- Operational, Personnel, Resources, Services, Security (Operational and Resource).

**Related diagrams**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Sequences diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Process Flow diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Service Sequences diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Structure diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Processes Flow diagram' space='']

0.0px;">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Exchange Item on the element realizing </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Exchanges.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To show/hide the realized <span>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* manipulator toolbar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:<ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If the<span> &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Exchange </span></span></span>Item is not displayed<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">, the tooltip for the button is</span><span> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_show_hide.png" /></ac:image></span><strong>Show Realized <span>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Exchanges</strong>.<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click it to hide the <span>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Exchange </span>Item.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If the <span>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Exchange </span>Item</span> is displayed<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">, the tooltip for the button is<span> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_hide.png" /></ac:image></span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Hide Realized<span> &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span><strong>Exchanges</strong></strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Click it to hide the <span>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Exchange </span>Item.<br />If the<span> element realizes</span> only one <span>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Exchange, the <span>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Exchange </span>Item is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.<br /></span></li></ol></li><li>If the element realizes multiple <span>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Exchanges, the <span>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Exchange Items dialog opens. In the dialog, do the following:<ol><li>Select the check boxes for the <span>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Exchanges whose <span>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Exchange </span>Item you want to be displayed.</li><li><p>Clear the check boxes for the <span>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Exchanges whose <span>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Exchange </span>Item you want to be hidden.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6781832-be95-4506-9fae-953500d91549"><ac:rich-text-body>To select all check boxes, click the<span> </span><strong>Select All<span> </span></strong>button. To clear all check boxes, click the<span> </span><strong>Clear All<span> </span></strong>button.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click<span> </span><strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_operational_exchanges.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>When you hide the realized exchanges, you can choose whether you want to hide the<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> arrow notation on the realizing elements. The </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><strong>Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> controls the arrow visibility.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">To change the arrow notation visibility on realizing element when the Exchange Item is hidden or unspecified</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">From the modeling tool main menu, choose <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the dialog, go to <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>Diagrams.</strong></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the <strong>Diagrams </strong>property group, find the <span><strong>Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing</strong> option and set it to true.</span></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</span></span></li></ol><h3>Dealing with Exchanges after Reconnecting Connectors</h3><p>When you need to reconnect the connector from one element to another, there are several possible options regarding the exchanges:</p><ul><li>None (default)</li><li>Create New &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchange</li><li>Realize Compatible &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchanges</li><li>Update End of &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchange</li><li>Remove Incompatible &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchanges</li></ul><h4 style="text-align: left;">None</h4><p style="text-align: left;">Choosing this option, the incompatible exchanges where source and target does not comply with new source and target, will be removed. This option is default. </p><h4>Create New &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchange</h4><p><span>When you reconnect the </span>connector<span> to a different element, a shortcut menu opens. In the shortcut menu, you can select to create a new &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchange. The </span><strong>Realized &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchange</strong> wizards<span> opens where you can create a new Exchange to be realized instead of the previously realized exchanges.</span></p><p><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reconnect_create_new_exchange.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h4>Realize Compatible &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchanges</h4><p><span>When you reconnect the connector to a different element, a shortcut menu opens.</span> In this shortcut menu, you can select to realize the exchanges, which exist between the sender and the new receiver. If the connector links ports, the conveyed items of the resulting exchanges would be ensured to adhere the flow properties on both ends of the connector.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reconnect_realize_compatible_exchange.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Update End of &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchange</h4><p>Choosing this option, the end of the exchange will be updated to match the new sender or new receiver on the reconnected connector's end.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reconnect_update_connector_end.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Remove Incompatible &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Exchanges</h4><p>Visible only when the sender and receiver are not changed after reconnection, but the exchanges that were already realized become incompatible, meaning they no longer comply with the new flow properties on both ends of the connector.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reconnect_remove_incompatible_exchange.png" /></ac:image></p><hr /><p><strong>* &lt;viewpoint name&gt;</strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>- Operational, Personnel, Resources, Services, Security (Operational and Resource).</span></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966413 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06647: Working with Execution Specifications

- page_id: `243966413`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966413/Working+with+Execution+Specifications
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:59.461+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > UML diagrams > Sequence diagram
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

872285712

872285714

438547513

**On this page**

872285713

The activation bars represent real model elements - Behavior and Action Execution Specifications. This benefits of using UML Execution Specifications are:

- Navigation. By double-clicking the activation bars (Behavior Execution Specifications) you can navigate from a sequence diagram to other behavior diagrams.
- Time and Duration Constraints can be attached to Behavior and Action Execution Specifications.

This method of modeling is optional. By default, the modeling approach where activation bars represent lifelines is used.

To switch to the new mode with activation bars representing UML Execution Specifications

- In the Sequence diagram palette, click the **Enable Execution Specification Modeling** button. This action enables the new modeling mode for each Sequence diagram individually.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- When you switch to this modeling approach, you will not be able to return to previous modeling method.
- If you open the Sequence Diagram with the activation bars representing UML Execution Specifications with the modeling tool version 19.0, such diagram will be read-only.

After you have enabled the Execution Specification Modeling, the activation bars in Sequence diagram represent:

- Behavior Execution Specification
- Action Execution Specification

You can refactor one Execution Specification to another at any time.

##### Setting behaviors or actions on the Execution Specifications

From the Containment tree, you can drag the Behavior or Action on the Execution Specification.

When you drag the behavior:

- If the activation bar represents Behavior Execution Specification, the dropped behavior is set for the behavior property of the Behavior Execution Specification.
- If the activation bar represents Action Execution Specification, the Action Execution Specification is converted to the Behavior Execution Specification and then the dropped behavior is set for the behavior property of the Behavior Execution Specification.

When you drag the action:

- If the activation bar represents Action Execution Specification, the dropped action is set for the action property of the Action Execution Specification.
- If the activation bar represents Behavior Execution Specification, the Behavior Execution Specification is converted to the Action Execution Specification and then dropped action is set for the action property of the Action Execution Specification.

Once the behavior or action is set for the Execution Specification, you can easily navigate to the diagrams of the set behavior or action.

To navigate to the behavior diagram

- Double-click the Behavior Execution Specification.

To navigate to the activity diagram

- Double-click the Action Execution Specification.

##### Setting the Behavior Execution Specification behavior by adding an operation for the message

When you have an operation with a method, you can add that operation to the message and the method is set as a behavior of the Behavior Execution Specification at the end of the message.

To set an operation with a method

1. In the Sequence diagram, draw a message between Behavior Execution Specifications.
2. In the Containment tree, select an operation with a method and drag it to the message.
3. Open the Specification window of the Behavior Execution Specification which is at the end of the message. In the Behavior property cell, the operation method is set as a behavior.

- If you change the operation to a different one that has a different method, that method will overwrite the behavior of the Behavior Execution Specification.
- If you change the operation to a different one that has no method, the previously set method will remain as a behavior of the Behavior Execution Specification.

##### Drawing the Time and Duration constraints

When you draw new Time constraints in a Sequence diagram (from the diagram palette):

- If you click near the top of the activation bar, the constrainedElement will be set to the Execution Specification of that bar, and firstEvent=true .
- If you click near the bottom of the activation bar, the constrainedElement will be set to the Execution Specification of that bar, and firstEvent=false .

When you draw new Duration constraints in a Sequence diagram (from the diagram palette):

- If you click near the top of the activation bar, the constrainedElement [i] will be set to the Execution Specification of that bar, and firstEvent[i]=true.
- If you click near the bottom of the activation bar, the constrainedElement [i] will be set to the Execution Specification of that bar, and firstEvent[i]=false .

872285711

ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564525 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06648: Working with Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `249564525`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564525/Working+with+Functions+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:03.797+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Resources > Resources Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282779718

1282779730

1282779720

The rows of the Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

##### Creating Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

To create a Function to an Operational Activity

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

##### Mapping Function to Operational Activity in Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Implements.

1282779717

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968432 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06649: Working with generic numbering mechanism

- page_id: `243968432`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968432/Working+with+generic+numbering+mechanism
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-09T15:33:04.941+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Generic numbering mechanism
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

With the generic numbering mechanism you can number the elements by setting their IDs. There are two element numbering modes: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic numbering' space='']and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manual numbering' space='']. If you choose the automatic numbering mode, a number for an element will be created automatically according to a predefined numbering format. Using the manual numbering mode you can create a custom number format for the element.

Make sure that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Numbering Customizations' space='UPDG'] are already created.

**Related pages**

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>With the generic numbering mechanism you can number the elements by setting their IDs. There are two element numbering modes: <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Automatic numbering" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[automatic ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manual numbering" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[manual]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. If you choose the automatic numbering mode, a number for an element will be created automatically according to a predefined numbering format. Using the manual numbering mode you can create a custom number format for the element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54153b7d-f645-4ace-9167-986af3ed7caf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure that <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDG" ri:content-title="Creating Numbering Customizations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[numbering customizations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are already created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="6815dda7-87ef-435b-bb99-610871778df6"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565314 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06650: Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table

- page_id: `249565314`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565314/Working+with+hierarchical+Actual+Measurements+table
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-12T13:30:21.955+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Measurements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The hierarchical table for actual measurements allows working with the actual measurements directly in the table. You can add new or existing Actual Property Sets and define values. When you add a Property Set to the table, all the existing Actual Property Sets will be collected recursively according to the defined scope.

For your convenience, it is advised to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space=''] first.

To create <domain name> Actual Measurements Table

- Do one of the following:
  - Right-click the <viewpoint name> Parameters package and choose Create Diagram > <viewpoint name> Actual Measurements Table .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and select **<domain name> Actual Measurements Table**.
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='']Typical Measurements Table's]]> toolbar, click the **Evaluate Measurements** button.

To work with <domain name> Actual Measurements Table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Click the **Add New** button and from the **Select Element** dialog, choose [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='']. The appropriate Property Sets are collected to the Select Element dialog based on the defined scope and whether they have measurements. The new Actual Property Sets with their corresponding structure recursively are created.
  - Click the **Add Existing**button and from the **Select Element** dialog, choose existing Property Sets. In the **Select Element** dialog, elements are offered according to the selected perspective - Type, Instance, or Any element.If the Type radio button is selected, all the appropriate Property Sets with instances and are not added in the table yet.If the Instance radio button is selected, all the appropriate instances according to the Property Set scope and not added to the table yet. The existing Actual Property Sets are added to the table with their corresponding structure recursively.
2. Double-click the Measure, Start Date, or End Date cell to add desired values. You can also choose to add more columns to the table.

#### TIP: TIP

TIP

You can also drag and drop the Property Sets from the appropriate domain in the Containment tree. The Actual Property Sets will be collected to the table recursively.

To add missing measurements

- Do one of the following:
  - In the table, choose an element for which you want to add missing measurements, and in the table toolbar, click Add Missing Measurements > Update Selected Actual Property Set . The existing measurements are added for the selected Actual Property Set if the measurements already exist in the model but are not displayed in the table. If there are no measurements, they are created for the selected Actual Property Set.
  - In the table toolbar, click Add Missing Measurements > Update All Actual Property Sets . The existing measurements are added for all the Actual Property Sets if the measurements already exist in the model but are not displayed in the table. If there are no measurements, they are created for all the Actual Property Sets.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='']

**Related domains**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Personnel Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Projects Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Security Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Services Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standards Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Summary and Overview' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-7 Services Measures Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MODAF. SV-7 Resource Performance Parameters Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='NSV-7 System Typical Quality Requirements Description' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C7 Performance Parameters' space='']

If there are no measurements, they are created for all the Actual Property Sets.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Property Set elements for measurement tables" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related domains</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Personnel Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Projects Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resources Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Security Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Services Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Standards Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Summary and Overview" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-7 Services Measures Matrix" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="MODAF. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565307 space=MT version=6 -->
## PAGE 06651: Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table

- page_id: `249565307`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565307/Working+with+hierarchical+Typical+Measurements+table
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-12-12T13:31:50.076+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Measurements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The hierarchical table for typical measurements allows working with the measurements directly in the table. You can add new or existing [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space=''] and create measurements for them. Moreover, define measurement types, default values, and documentation directly in the table.

##### Working with Typical Measurements Table

To work with <domain name> Typical Measurements Table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Click the **Add New** button and from the drop-down list select [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='']. The appropriate Property Sets are collected to the drop-down list according to the domain in which the table is created.
  - Click the Add Existing button to add an existing Property Set. The new row in the table is created.
2. Select Property Set and in the table toolbar, click the Add New Measurement button.
3. Double-click the Type, Default Value, or Documentation cell add the desired measurement to specify it. You can also choose to add more columns to the table.

#### INFO: TIP

TIP

You can also drag and drop the Property Sets from the appropriate domain from the Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An earlier version of the table for Typical Measurements vs. new hierarchical table for Typical Measurements

##### Display modes

There are three display modes available for the hierarchical Typical Measurements Table:

- Display Structure. Choose this mode to show all owned measurements in a structure view. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_mode_structure.png']
- Display Structure with owned Measurements Recursively . Choose this mode to show all owned measurements recursively in a structure view. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_mode_structure_owned_recursively.png']
- Display List of Measurements Recursively. Choose to show elements with all their measurements in a hierarchical order. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_mode_list_recursively.png']

To choose a display mode

1. In the hierarchical Typical Measurements Table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='options.png'] .
2. In the shortcut menu, click Display Mode and then choose one of the available modes.

##### Evaluating measurements

After the Typical Measurements Table is created and filled out, you can create an Actual Measurements Table.

To create an Actual Measurements Table from the Typical Measurements Table

- In the filled-out Typical Measurements Table's toolbar, click the Evaluate Measurements button. The hierarchical [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space=''] is created.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Typical Measurements Table.

To generate the report from the table toolbar

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

The report is generated with all the data displayed in the table.

To generate the report from the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the desired package.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Generate Report > UAF > <more> > Typical Measurements Excel Spreadsheet .
3. Define the name and select a location for your report.
4. Click Save .
5. In the open question dialog, choose whether you want to view the report after it is generated. If you choose to view it, the report is opened afterward.

In the report, the Property Sets with at least one measurement are collected from the chosen package.

If you need to include more packages as the report scope, then use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='']**.

To generate the report from the Report Wizard

1. In the main menu, click Tools and then choose [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='']**Report Wizard**.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space=''] dialog, expand the UAF package and choose the Typical Measurements Table template.
3. Click Next .
4. Select the report data and click Next .
5. Select the desired scope (particular package or entire model).
6. Click Next .
7. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
8. Click Generate .

- If the chosen scope contains a single open hierarchical Typical Measurements table, the report is generated from the data of that table.
- If the chosen scope contains more than one open table, the generated report contains the data from all scope.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='']

**Related domains**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Personnel Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Projects Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Security Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Services Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standards Parameters' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Summary and Overview' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-7 Services Measures Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MODAF. SV-7 Resource Performance Parameters Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='NSV-7 System Typical Quality Requirements Description' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C7 Performance Parameters' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

/><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_mode_list_recursively.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To choose a display mode</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d7d19ece-2ae5-43dc-88c0-4ffba5e52669">In the hierarchical Typical Measurements Table toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li data-uuid="4be41d03-7a2f-4a7e-bf6d-4131fda43113">In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Display Mode</strong> and then choose one of the available modes.</li></ol><h3>Evaluating measurements</h3><p>After the Typical Measurements Table is created and filled out, you can create an Actual Measurements Table. </p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Actual Measurements Table from the Typical Measurements Table</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="b5e29a03-b27c-4040-b124-c9fef1e0491b">In the filled-out Typical Measurements Table's toolbar, click the <strong>Evaluate Measurements</strong> button.<br />The hierarchical<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Actual Measurements Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is created.</li></ul><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the Typical Measurements Table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report from the table toolbar</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="b4c317dc-4d9c-4458-98c7-5b372515ab61">In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="616cb5ea-24d5-4afd-a86d-4249e0e93c2a">Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li data-uuid="bd227d86-44b9-4982-b835-b8e3d97c4037">Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40258efe-aa30-40de-a1f0-a92cee4ea3aa"><ac:rich-text-body>The report is generated with all the data displayed in the table.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />To generate the report from the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="44ccc642-60f7-411c-9682-5f655eff2d6e">In the Containment tree, right-click the desired package.</li><li data-uuid="7809c733-5428-4df3-a500-e57450dca31a">From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Generate Report</strong> &gt; <strong>UAF</strong> &gt; <strong>&lt;more&gt;</strong> &gt; <strong>Typical Measurements Excel Spreadsheet</strong>. </li><li data-uuid="3ca6eafb-d3de-4bd0-8957-dadeed5c59f9">Define the name and select a location for your report.</li><li data-uuid="187d36fb-05e0-416e-94ee-026a2cce2494">Click <strong>Save</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="6a25c8d4-34aa-467d-b869-97f5b89975b3">In the open question dialog, choose whether you want to view the report after it is generated. If you choose to view it, the report is opened afterward.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f2efff0-3e15-4d1e-a4e4-b1415a6e4a11"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the report, the Property Sets with at least one measurement are collected from the chosen package.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>If you need to include more packages as the report scope, then use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report from the Report Wizard</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="63773201-01c3-4fbf-89c1-58a86afdb023">In the main menu, click <strong>Tools</strong> and then choose <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="f0b2b65c-fcb8-404b-82ce-143b5470a28e">In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> dialog, expand the <strong>UAF</strong> package and choose the <strong>Typical Measurements Table</strong> template.</li><li data-uuid="8fff9303-196d-412e-a656-95e73826aa7f">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="d411879a-5ccb-4aeb-b5cc-442cca60af95">Select the report data and click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="d53926ce-755f-41e1-a2c5-9b88bdaff19b">Select the desired scope (particular package or entire model).</li><li data-uuid="abf708f3-b677-4b0d-9f0a-91b4332aa08e">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="609febd2-bc9e-4f1a-8189-6a163ea964cb">Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li data-uuid="b0cc2b71-c2f1-488d-a2c5-1472dd1ed5ef">Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7df32fec-8fc3-4123-9b22-828d97123b8b"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="4a189304-9921-4295-b94e-6ee668998ff6">If the chosen scope contains a single open hierarchical Typical Measurements table, the report is generated from the data of that table.</li><li data-uuid="67b77c1f-2579-4c93-aa65-22a03c1267f1">If the chosen scope contains more than one open table, the generated report contains the data from all scope.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="b8ea06d8-97f8-453c-a6f3-ebb9362508ca"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="834338c4-f6c4-4f30-a894-7426284b974e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Property Set elements for measurement tables" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related domains</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="8cc13cc6-4fb0-47dd-95b2-e6b64096cadd"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="bc1b6232-39b4-4bd7-8bb9-15e9248244aa"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Personnel Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="98eaf31e-d5bc-41fd-81fc-3f4095dc0850"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Projects Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="846998c3-cfd9-499c-b7d8-a3b595f2f7ca"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resources Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="84fb7bb5-0ce8-40f5-9bfd-ab3c52f5699c"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Security Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="eee05bbc-8486-4eb0-bf57-a9ffef648f49"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Services Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="34547e30-27f8-4767-b6af-5bc37f39f087"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Standards Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="940efaf6-5b90-4051-9bd6-b712370390ca"><ac:link><ri:page SV-7 Resource Performance Parameters Matrix" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="34c157be-dbe1-455f-8ede-4c2a31b3a055"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="NSV-7 System Typical Quality Requirements Description" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="93bb5f26-76d5-438a-8c96-fdf72545ce4c"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="C7 Performance Parameters" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563473 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06652: Working with L1 Node Types Table

- page_id: `249563473`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563473/Working+with+L1+Node+Types+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:56.326+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L1 Node Types
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Node Types****Table (L1)**provides the definitions for all the operational concepts.

To add a new element in the Node Types table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button and choose from the following:
  - Known Resource
  - Operational Architecture
  - Operational Mitigation
  - Operational Performer A new row is added.
2. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added element.

To add an existing operational concept in the Node Types table

1. In the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Element dialog, choose the desired operational concept (Known Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, Operational Performer).
3. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<span> <strong>Node Types</strong></span><strong> Table (L1)</strong><span> </span>provides the definitions for all the operational concepts.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new element in the Node Types table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the<span> </span><strong>Add New</strong><span> </span>button and choose from the following:<ul><li>Known Resource</li><li>Operational Architecture</li><li>Operational Mitigation</li><li>Operational Performer<br />A new row is added.</li></ul></li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added element.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing operational concept in the Node Types table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong><span> </span>button.</li><li>In the<span> </span><strong>Select Element </strong>dialog, choose the desired operational concept (Known Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, Operational Performer).</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added elements.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563572 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06653: Working with L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `249563572`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563572/Working+with+L2-L3+Logical+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:19.827+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

911370602

INLINE

911370604

INLINE

478104274

INLINE

**On this page**

911370603

INLINE

##### Creating a diagram

To create an L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the L2-L3 package. Do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**L2-L3 LogicalTaxonomy**.
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **L2-L3 LogicalTaxonomy**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.
2. Name the element.

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the L2-L3 package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it.
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_op_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connect the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].
2. Make sure that **All** is chosen for **Properties**.
3. Search for **Is Abstract** and set it to *true*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

- A L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.
- The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space=''] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565422 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06654: Working with matrices

- page_id: `249565422`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565422/Working+with+matrices
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-08-22T09:01:34.833+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['reset', 'predefined', 'matrix', 'matrices', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For more generic procedures on working with matrices, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space=''] chapter.

##### Building matrices

To build a matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope .
2. Specify the Column Scope .
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

##### Mapping elements in matrices

To map elements

- Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

##### Resetting to default

When working with the predefined matrices, you can make some changes to the default settings, e.g., change the row or column element type, dependency criteria, or directions. You can restore the following settings to the default at any time:

- Description
- Direction
- Dependency Criteria
- Show Elements
- Column Element Type
- Row Element Type

To restore the default predefined matrix settings

- In the matrix toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='options.png'] and choose the Reset to Default command.

##### Description area

The matrix descriptions in the Description area come from two different locations: tag value orstereotype.

If the tag value is set, the matrix description is the information from the tag value. If the tag value is not set, the description comes from the matrix stereotype. The information in the matrix stereotype is always more recent.

If you want to see a more recent matrix description (which may change with a new modeling tool/plugin release), clear the tag value. For more information about editing tagged values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing tagged value' space='MED'].

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Implementation Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Resource Taxonomy Matrix' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Project Activities to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources to Operational Activities Mapping matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Controls to Risks Mapping Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Strategic Phases to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb976870-3395-4c33-8e01-0b81c35660c8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more generic procedures on working with matrices, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency Matrix" /></ac:link> chapter.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Building matrices</h3><p>To build a matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong>.</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong>.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png" /></ac:image>.</li></ol><h3>Mapping elements in matrices</h3><p>To map elements</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ul><h3>Removing the relation from the matrix</h3><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul><h3>Resetting to default</h3><p>When working with the predefined matrices, you can make some changes to the default settings, e.g., change the row or column element type, dependency criteria, or directions. You can restore the following settings to the default at any time:</p><ul><li>Description</li><li>Direction</li><li>Dependency Criteria</li><li>Show Elements</li><li>Column Element Type</li><li>Row Element Type</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To restore the default predefined matrix settings</p><hr /><ul><li>In the matrix toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options.png" /></ac:image> and choose the <strong>Reset to Default</strong> command.</li></ul><h3>Description area</h3><p>The matrix descriptions in the Description area come from two different locations: <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">tag value or </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">stereotype.</span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">If <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="837dd328-563c-4326-b043-fa9de45232e1">the</ac:inline-comment-marker> tag value is set, the matrix description is the information from the tag value. If the tag value is not set, the description comes from the matrix stereotype. The information in the matrix stereotype is <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="98491d7d-2dab-4fe4-84f9-1b9655b0d910">always</ac:inline-comment-marker> more recent.</span></p><p>If you want to see a more recent matrix description <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="654ad161-3504-4c09-99a5-80a3ec0a83e9">(which may change with a new modeling tool/plugin release)</ac:inline-comment-marker>, clear the tag value. For more information about editing <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c0cb9ce2-dfb8-4bf3-8cf8-2e48475974a4">tagged</ac:inline-comment-marker> values, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Editing tagged value" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency Matrix" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Implementation Matrix" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Actual Resource Taxonomy Matrix" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with with Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048820 space=MT version=7 -->
## PAGE 06655: Working with Mission Engineering Threads diagram

- page_id: `258048820`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/258048820/Working+with+Mission+Engineering+Threads+diagram
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-10-28T15:49:23.156+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Resources > Resources Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Mission Engineering Threads** diagram describes the functions that are normally conducted in the course of implementing operational activity(ies) in support of capability(ies). It describes the functions, their Inputs/Outputs, function actions, and flows between them.

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Mission Engineering Threads diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Resources Processes and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Mission Engineering Threads**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Mission Engineering Threads,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Mission Engineering Thread element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create a Mission Engineering Thread in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Mission Engineering Thread and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Mission Engineering Thread in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Resources Processes package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Mission Engineering Thread and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_mission_engineering_thread.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Mission Engineering Thread (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connect the Mission Engineering Thread

When you have the Mission Engineering Thread created, you can start connecting them using the Implements relationship.

To connect the elements with the Implements relationship

1. Select an element from which the Implements will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Implements. You can also select the Implementsrelationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249580036 space=MT version=9 -->
## PAGE 06656: Working with Mission Phases diagram

- page_id: `249580036`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249580036/Working+with+Mission+Phases+diagram
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2025-10-28T15:52:38.171+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Processes
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Mission Phases (St-Pr-MP)** diagram shows the relationship between mission phases, Capabilities, and the actual organizations involved.

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Mission Phases diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Processes and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Mission Phases**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Mission Phases****,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Actual Mission element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an Actual Mission in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Actual Mission and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an Actual Mission in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Strategic Processes package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Actual Mission and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_actual_mission.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Actual Mission (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connect the Actual Mission

When you have the Actual Mission elements created, you can start connecting them using the Sequence relationship.

To connect the elements with theSequence relationship

1. Select an element from which the Sequence will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Sequence . You can also select the Sequence relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=283247571 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06657: Working with Mission Threads diagram

- page_id: `283247571`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/283247571/Working+with+Mission+Threads+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-14T16:13:00.805+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Mission Threads** diagram describes the activities that are normally conducted in the course of achieving business goals that support a capability. It describes operational activities, their Inputs/Outputs, operational activity actions and flows between them.

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Mission Threads diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Processes and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Mission Threads**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Mission Threads,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Mission Thread element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create a Mission Thread in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Mission Thread and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Mission Thread in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Operational Processes package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Mission Thread and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_mission_thread.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Mission Thread (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connect the Mission Thread

When you have the Mission Thread created, you can start connecting them using the Defines relationship.

To connect the elements with the Defines relationship

1. Select an element from which the Implements will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Defines. You can also select the Definesrelationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Mission Threads</strong> diagram describes the activities that are normally conducted in the course of achieving business goals that support a capability. It describes operational activities, their Inputs/Outputs, operational activity actions and flows between them.</p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create a Mission Threads diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="218541dd-9af5-4ffb-b80d-b2c4e4b4eeec"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Processes and do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Mission Threads</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="336" ac:width="333"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_mission_threads_diagram_containment.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Mission Threads, </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="279c3351-7510-4186-af7c-fb36ef76532b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Mission Thread element, but the same is valid for other elements.<br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create a Mission Thread in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="459e331c-eaf9-43e6-acf9-73d4d6fad30a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the diagram palette, click the Mission Thread and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="265" ac:width="429"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_mission_thread_palette.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="a492d27f-0195-4ca5-8ea0-6259f1ec1660"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Name the element.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create a Mission Thread in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5e874c84-1f0b-4650-ac8d-815101566790"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Containment tree, right-click the Operational Processes package and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. /></span></li><li data-uuid="df887273-ce88-41b4-a4c1-fed8b2a9ae4d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Name the element.</span></li><li data-uuid="79f5d5ad-08d8-4ce3-bbb3-1f30104fe617">Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="222" ac:width="764"><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_mission_thread.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="521ba379-76c6-46a0-9693-ace6fd4f5059">Copy a list in your <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=264929460 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06658: Working with Mission Threads Flow diagram

- page_id: `264929460`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/264929460/Working+with+Mission+Threads+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-11-28T16:08:59.940+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Mission Threads diagram' space=''] must be created first.

To create a Mission Thread Flow diagram for Mission Thread flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Performer or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click Cancel in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Operational Performer or Operational Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Operational Activity Actions or drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Operational Activity Actions wit h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Operational Process Flow diagram with the top element -*Distress Signal Handling*as a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The OperationalProcess Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Operational Activity Action or Operational Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Operational Process Flow diagram for an Operational Activity Action or Operational Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create an Operational Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of an Operational Activity Action or Operational Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Operational Activity Action or Operational Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Operational Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Performer or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new OperationalProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Operational Activity Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Operational Performer and Operational Activity (the behavior of the Operational Action). Furthermore, if the Operational Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Operational Activity Action.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Furthermore, if the Operational Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Operational Activity Action.</span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249580042 space=MT version=6 -->
## PAGE 06659: Working with Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram

- page_id: `249580042`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249580042/Working+with+Mission+Vignette+and+Scenario+diagram
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-09-30T15:55:43.171+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Parameters Pm
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram (Mi-Pm-VS)** shows the elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments and conditions applicable to capability, operational concept, or set of systems.

The Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram can be created under the Parameter package of each domain (e.g, Operational Parameters, Strategic Parameters)

##### Creating a diagram

An example is shown with the Strategic Parameters, but the Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram can be created under other domain Parameters packages also.

To create a Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Parameters and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Mission Vignette and Scenario**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for****Mission** Vignette and Scenario,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Mission Scenario element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create a Mission Scenario in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Mission Scenario and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Mission Scenario in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Strategic Parameters package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Mission Scenario and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_mission_scenatio.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Mission Scenario (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connect the Mission Scenario

When you have the Mission Scenario elements created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].
2. Make sure that **All** is chosen for **Properties**.
3. Search for **Is Abstract** and set it to *true*.

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

to <em>true</em>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To view the inherited properties</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d49b463b-0cad-4a97-81a7-94413e449c7a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="efb2d1e2-bc4e-475e-ab6a-792b4cdb61e3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````
